source.subject: AWS listing hero positioning & trial onboarding — Acme Analytics
messages:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Nora Singh <nora.singh@acmeanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 4 May 2029 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Draft hero & onboarding flow for AWS listing

Hi Nora —

Quick one: attached are two drafts we want your eyes on for the AWS Marketplace product page and the trial onboarding flow. High-level asks below so we can keep the first pass tight: 

- Hero copy: are the claims aligned with Acme’s customer messaging? Any brand or legal must-not claims? (see redwood_aws_hero_variants.docx)
- Trial onboarding: does the step sequence match what your success team expects? Any steps that would require additional entitlements or AMI configs? (see trial_onboarding_flow.pdf)
- Co-sell collateral: we want a one-pager the field can use describing the trial experience and success metrics — is there existing collateral we should adapt?

If you can flag changes inline and suggest preferred hero variant (A/B), that’ll help us move to copy freeze. If helpful, we can set a 30-min sync early next week to walk through.

Attaching:
- redwood_aws_hero_variants.docx (three headline variants + subheads)
- trial_onboarding_flow.pdf (flow + required entitlements)

Thanks — appreciate your time on this.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Head of Marketplace Go‑to‑Market
Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0127

---
Note: internal draft — please don’t forward external versions until we agree on hero text.

From: Nora Singh <nora.singh@acmeanalytics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@acmeanalytics.com
Date: Sat, 4 May 2029 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft hero & onboarding flow for AWS listing

Hi Kimberly —

Thanks for the drafts. Quick feedback from product & GTM below; I’ve attached Acme_brand_guidelines.pdf for tone and logo usage.

Hero copy:
- Variant 2 (concise benefits-first) aligns best with our buyer-facing voice.
- Please avoid the phrase “enterprise-grade inferencing” without a short parenthetical calling out data residency — legal flagged that for marketplace pages.
- Can we add a single line referencing our prebuilt connectors to SFDC and S3? That’s a commonly searched capability.

Trial onboarding:
- Flow looks good; two small asks: (1) add an explicit step for provisioning IAM role that our procurement team requires, and (2) call out expected dataset size limits for the trial AMI (we cap at 50GB in the sandbox).

Co-sell collateral:
- We have a one-pager but it’s sales-facing. I’m looping procurement because they’ll want a procurement-ready snippet and the pricing footnote.

Can we do a working session on Monday at 10:00am PT? If that works, please invite our field lead (I’ll add her once you confirm).

Attaching: Acme_brand_guidelines.pdf

Cheers,
Nora
Head of Strategic Partnerships
Acme Analytics
Nora.Singh@acmeanalytics.com

On Sat, May 4, 2029 at 09:18 Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original message omitted]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 4 May 2029 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Draft hero & onboarding flow for AWS listing — action items

Marissa / Rafael —

Forwarding Nora’s reply. Action items I want us to own before Monday’s sync (proposed 2029-05-06 10:00am PT if Nora confirms):

Marissa — copy
- Update hero copy to lean Variant 2 and remove the phrase flagged by legal. Provide 2 subhead options: one that calls out prebuilt connectors and one shorter CTA-centric subhead.
- Produce hero_copy_v2.txt and attach to the updated email to Nora.

Rafael — trial & ops
- Add an explicit IAM provisioning step and a short note about the 50GB sandbox cap. Confirm whether our AMI needs a different user-data script for Acme’s environment.
- Prepare a one-page field play that maps trial steps to expected success metrics (MQL -> POC conversion). Put that into redwood_amazon_listing_playbook.md.

Logistics/links:
- I uploaded a starter playbook to the shared Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cAcmeListingAssets
- HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/ACME-2029-101 (please update deal stage to "Marketplace copy review")

Let’s keep the external thread tight — I’ll send the updated hero copy and playbook to Nora once we have the two items above. Rafael, flag any blockers on AMI/user-data by EOD Monday.

Thanks —
Kimberly

---
> On Sat, May 4, 2029 at 11:02 Nora Singh <nora.singh@acmeanalytics.com> wrote:
> > [reply omitted]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Nora Singh <nora.singh@acmeanalytics.com>
Date: Mon, 6 May 2029 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft hero & onboarding flow for AWS listing — updated hero & meeting

Nora / Kimberly / Rafael —

Thanks — I updated the hero to Variant 2 and removed the flagged phrase. Attached is hero_copy_v2.txt with two subhead options:

1) Connector-first: "Works with your data: native S3 + SFDC connectors for fast ingest."
2) CTA-first: "Start a free sandbox — prebuilt connectors included."

I also added the IAM provisioning step and a short note about the 50GB sandbox limit in the playbook draft (redwood_amazon_listing_playbook.md). Rafael — please review the user-data deviation notes I added in section 2.

Meeting: Monday 2029-05-06 10:00am PT is confirmed — I sent a calendar invite to Nora and included a 30-min agenda: hero copy, onboarding steps, procurement footnote.

Next steps:
- Kimberly to send updated assets to Nora after Rafael confirms AMI notes (EOD today).
- Rafael to confirm whether an alternate AMI is required for Acme’s tooling (EOD today).
- Marissa to prepare the two-line procurement snippet for the listing and a sales-facing one-pager.

Attachments: hero_copy_v2.txt

—
Marissa Cole
Senior Product Marketing Manager, Marketplace
marissa.cole@redwood.com

On Sat, May 4, 2029 at 13:45 Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com> wrote:
> [internal action items omitted]

source.subject: Data execution geography & subprocessor pathways — Novum Health
messages:
From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Novum Health Security <security@novumhealth.com>
Date: 2028-12-06T09:14:00-08:00
Subject: Where will our inference requests run? (regions / vendors)

Hi Marissa,

Thanks for taking our call yesterday. As part of our security review we need a short, concrete explanation of where Novum's inference requests and any derived artifacts might execute or be stored (temporary or persistent). Specifically:

- Which cloud regions are used for model execution and transient KV/prefix caches?
- Which third-party subprocessors touch request payloads or logs?
- Are there any automatic cross-border hops (for example, US -> EU) we should be aware of?

Can you point us to a short map or a CSV list of subprocessors? We have a short procurement window and would like to close this week.

Best,
Elena
Security & Privacy Lead
Novum Health
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-12-06T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Where will our inference requests run? (regions / vendors)

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear bullets. Attaching a one-page execution map (processing_map_v1.pdf) and a CSV export of our current subprocessor roster (subprocessor_inventory.csv). TL;DR up front:

- Hosted requests: executed in the region you select for your account (we currently offer US, EU, and APAC regions). If you pick a US region, the primary execution and caches remain in US regions.
- Fallbacks & autoscaling: if capacity is exhausted in your pinned region we have configured policy-driven fallbacks that prefer the same geo (US -> other US regions) and only fall back to EU/APAC under explicit customer permission or if the account is tied to global routing.
- Subprocessors: execution involves cloud compute (AWS/GCP), object storage for model artifacts and telemetry sinks, and an edge partner for TLS termination and short-lived caching. Full list in CSV; high level named vendors are AWS (us-east-1, eu-west-1), GCP (us-central1, europe-west1), and EdgeServe Networks (edge cache nodes).
- Temporary data: KV cache and prefix cache are in-memory with TTLs (default 2 hours) and spill to ephemeral disk only on overloaded hosts; we do not persist request payloads by default. Logs retained per your contract — standard hosted retention is 30 days unless otherwise negotiated.

I'll paste the concise answers below and then attach the files. If you want us to lock routing to a specific region with no cross-border fallback, we can add that to the account config during onboarding (it becomes part of the SLA).

– Marissa
Director, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference

Attachments: processing_map_v1.pdf (1 page), subprocessor_inventory.csv (2 columns)
From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-12-06T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Where will our inference requests run? (regions / vendors)

Thanks Marissa — the PDF is helpful. A couple follow-ups:

1) Can you enumerate which subprocessors have access to unredacted request payloads? Our legal team needs names and whether they are processors or sub-processors.
2) For the edge partner (EdgeServe), is the cache ever hosted outside the customer-selected region?

If you can reply with a one-paragraph answer for each, I can include them in our risk memo.

Regards,
Elena

On Wed, Dec 6, 2028 at 09:34 Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena — thanks for the clear bullets. Attaching a one-page execution map (processing_map_v1.pdf) and a CSV export of our current subprocessor roster (subprocessor_inventory.csv). TL;DR up front:...
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-12-06T13:02:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Where will our inference requests run? (regions / vendors)

Elena — quick concise answers for the memo:

1) Subprocessors with access to unredacted request payloads:
- AWS (compute in selected region): processor (we are the controller; AWS processes compute and ephemeral storage).
- GCP (optional region-specific compute): processor.
- EdgeServe Networks (TLS termination + short-lived cache): subprocessor; caches are encrypted at rest and keys are customer-isolated for Dedicated/Private customers.
- Analytics/telemetry sinks (e.g., Datadog): subprocessor; these receive obfuscated/aggregated telemetry by default — full payload ingestion is only enabled with explicit customer opt-in.

2) Edge cache geography: EdgeServe operates regional POPs. For a US-selected region we configure edge caches to prefer US POPs. However, for global routing (if you enable multi-region burst) some cache misses or health probes may be serviced from nearest available POP which can cross borders. If you require strict geo-containment we will enable route-pin to keep all traffic within the selected legal territory — this is standard for Private/Dedicated contracts.

Operational notes:
- TTLs: KV/prefix caches default 2 hours; ephemeral disks used only on memory pressure and scrubbed on instance termination.
- Retention: request logs retained 30 days (hosted) unless contract changes.
- Deletion: we support on-demand deletion requests scoped to an account and time window; we provide audit evidence when deletion completes.

We can export a CSV that marks which subprocessors have payload access and the contractual role (processor/subprocessor) — Rafael, can you attach that scoped export and a short vendor contact for Novum?

Thanks,
Marissa

> Attachments: processing_map_v1.pdf, subprocessor_inventory.csv

1) Can you enumerate which subprocessors have access to unredacted request payloads? Our legal team needs names and whether they are processors or sub-processors.
2) For the edge partner (EdgeServe), is the cache ever hosted outside the customer-selected region?

If you can reply with a one-paragraph answer for each, I can include them in our risk memo.

Regards,
Elena

On Wed, Dec 6, 2028 at 09:34 Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena — thanks for the clear bullets. Attaching a one-page execution map (processing_map_v1.pdf) and a CSV export of our current subprocessor roster (subprocessor_inventory.csv). TL;DR up front:...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-12-06T13:02:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Where will our inference requests run? (regions / vendors)

Elena — quick concise answers for the memo:

1) Subprocessors with access to unredacted request payloads:
- AWS (compute in selected region): processor (we are the controller; AWS processes compute and ephemeral storage).
- GCP (optional region-specific compute): processor.
- EdgeServe Networks (TLS termination + short-lived cache): subprocessor; caches are encrypted at rest and keys are customer-isolated for Dedicated/Private customers.
- Analytics/telemetry sinks (e.g., Datadog): subprocessor; these receive obfuscated/aggregated telemetry by default — full payload ingestion is only enabled with explicit customer opt-in.

2) Edge cache geography: EdgeServe operates regional POPs. For a US-selected region we configure edge caches to prefer US POPs. However, for global routing (if you enable multi-region burst) some cache misses or health probes may be serviced from nearest available POP which can cross borders. If you require strict geo-containment we will enable route-pin to keep all traffic within the selected legal territory — this is standard for Private/Dedicated contracts.

Operational notes:
- TTLs: KV/prefix caches default 2 hours; ephemeral disks used only on memory pressure and scrubbed on instance termination.
- Retention: request logs retained 30 days (hosted) unless contract changes.
- Deletion: we support on-demand deletion requests scoped to an account and time window; we provide audit evidence when deletion completes.

We can export a CSV that marks which subprocessors have payload access and the contractual role (processor/subprocessor) — Rafael, can you attach that scoped export and a short vendor contact for Novum?

Thanks,
Marissa

> Attachments: processing_map_v1.pdf, subprocessor_inventory.csv
text:
From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Novum Health Security <security@novumhealth.com>
Date: 2028-12-06T09:14:00-08:00
Subject: Where will our inference requests run? (regions / vendors)

Hi Marissa,

Thanks for taking our call yesterday. As part of our security review we need a short, concrete explanation of where Novum's inference requests and any derived artifacts might execute or be stored (temporary or persistent). Specifically:

- Which cloud regions are used for model execution and transient KV/prefix caches?
- Which third-party subprocessors touch request payloads or logs?
- Are there any automatic cross-border hops (for example, US -> EU) we should be aware of?

Can you point us to a short map or a CSV list of subprocessors? We have a short procurement window and would like to close this week.

Best,
Elena
Security & Privacy Lead
Novum Health


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-12-06T09:34:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Where will our inference requests run? (regions / vendors)

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear bullets. Attaching a one-page execution map (processing_map_v1.pdf) and a CSV export of our current subprocessor roster (subprocessor_inventory.csv). TL;DR up front:

- Hosted requests: executed in the region you select for your account (we currently offer US, EU, and APAC regions). If you pick a US region, the primary execution and caches remain in US regions.
- Fallbacks & autoscaling: if capacity is exhausted in your pinned region we have configured policy-driven fallbacks that prefer the same geo (US -> other US regions) and only fall back to EU/APAC under explicit customer permission or if the account is tied to global routing.
- Subprocessors: execution involves cloud compute (AWS/GCP), object storage for model artifacts and telemetry sinks, and an edge partner for TLS termination and short-lived caching. Full list in CSV; high level named vendors are AWS (us-east-1, eu-west-1), GCP (us-central1, europe-west1), and EdgeServe Networks (edge cache nodes).
- Temporary data: KV cache and prefix cache are in-memory with TTLs (default 2 hours) and spill to ephemeral disk only on overloaded hosts; we do not persist request payloads by default. Logs retained per your contract — standard hosted retention is 30 days unless otherwise negotiated.

I'll paste the concise answers below and then attach the files. If you want us to lock routing to a specific region with no cross-border fallback, we can add that to the account config during onboarding (it becomes part of the SLA).

– Marissa
Director, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference

Attachments: processing_map_v1.pdf (1 page), subprocessor_inventory.csv (2 columns)


From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@novumhealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-12-06T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Where will our inference requests run? (regions / vendors)

2) For the edge partner (EdgeServe), is the cache ever hosted outside the customer-selected region?

Elena

source.subject: AWS Marketplace: AMI visibility & pricing footnote — quick alignment
messages:
From: Tom Hargreaves <tom.hargreaves@acmeanalytics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Cc: AWS Marketplace Onboarding <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Jul 2028 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace listing: visibility + pricing footnote check

Hi Kimberly,

Quick one: we're ready to publish the product tile for Acme Analytics on AWS Marketplace but AWS flagged two items they want explicit confirmation on before approval: the AMI visibility settings (public vs. private-seller AMI) and the pricing footnote language for bring-your-own-license (BYOL) guidance.

Attached is the latest product page copy (marketplace_copy_v2.docx) and an AMI visibility matrix where we mapped current images to visibility scope (ami_visibility_matrix.csv). Can you please confirm:

- Which AMIs should be visible to the public vs. restricted to buyer accounts?
- Whether the BYOL pricing footnote below is accurate from a billing/finance standpoint: "Charges shown are for compute-only usage; software licensing and third-party subscriptions may be billed separately by the software vendor."

If you can reply with one-liner confirmations, I can push the listing through the AWS staging flow and share the listing preview. Target launch: next Tuesday.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — our onboarding team is on standby if you want to hop on a 20-min call.

Thanks,
Tom Hargreaves
Partner Success | Acme Analytics
[Attachment: marketplace_copy_v2.docx] [Attachment: ami_visibility_matrix.csv]
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Hargreaves <tom.hargreaves@acmeanalytics.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Jul 2028 09:42:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Marketplace listing: visibility + pricing footnote check

Vivek / Rafael — forwarding Tom's note. Please see attachments and the matrix. Need quick alignment so we can sign off today.

Specific asks:
- Vivek: confirm the BYOL footnote language is OK from a pricing/CTC (cost-to-serve) and procurement perspective, or suggest a tightened phrasing if necessary.
- Rafael: tell me which AMIs we should mark as public vs. restricted in the matrix and whether any additional tags are required for GovCloud or regional AMIs.

Tom — we'll get back with confirmations by ~10:30 PT.

Kimberly Park
Head of Partner GTM | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: marketplace_copy_v2.docx] [Attachment: ami_visibility_matrix.csv]
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Tom Hargreaves <tom.hargreaves@acmeanalytics.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Jul 2028 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Marketplace listing: visibility + pricing footnote check

Kimberly / Vivek / Tom — quick read-through of the matrix:

- Recommendation for AMI visibility: mark ami-0a1b2c-public and ami-0f9e8d-public as Public (these are the customer-facing UAT/GA images). All others (ami-0b2c3d-int, ami-0c3d4e-enc) should be Seller-private; we will provide marketplace activation instructions for buyers after purchase.
- Regional/GovCloud: we do have separate images for GovCloud (ami-gov-01) and EU (ami-eu-02) — those should be set to restricted by default until legal confirms export/compliance posture.

On the BYOL footnote: I prefer tighter wording. Suggest: "Displayed charges are for compute usage only. Licensing or subscriptions for third-party software may be billed separately by the software vendor or under a separate contract. Buyers should consult seller documentation for licensing terms."

If Vivek is good on pricing language, we can send the suggested revision back to Tom and request AWS staging approval. I pasted the suggested sentence above.

—Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Marketplace Product Manager | Redwood Inference
From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Hargreaves <tom.hargreaves@acmeanalytics.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Jul 2028 10:33:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Marketplace listing: visibility + pricing footnote check

Thanks team — Rafael's AMI split looks right to me. On the BYOL footnote: Rafael's tightened phrasing reads clearer and reduces ambiguity for procurement teams. Confirmed from a cost-to-serve and billing disclosure perspective — no change required to billing metadata on our side. Recommend we use Rafael's proposed sentence verbatim.

Kimberly — if you want, I can draft the exact one-line to send to AWS. Otherwise, Rafael / you can push the copy back to Tom and request the staging preview.

Vivek Kulkarni
Director, Cloud Economics | Redwood Inference

> 
> Quick one: we're ready to publish the product tile for Acme Analytics on AWS Marketplace but AWS flagged two items they want explicit confirmation on before approval: the AMI visibility settings (public vs. private-seller AMI) and the pricing footnote language for bring-your-own-license (BYOL) guidance.

> - Whether the BYOL pricing footnote below is accurate from a billing/finance standpoint: "Charges shown are for compute-only usage; software licensing and third-party subscriptions may be billed separately by the software vendor."

source.subject: Demo: Redwood Inference — calendar invite corrupted, sending Zoom fallback
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@finosys.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-03-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Demo: Redwood Inference — scheduling

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for the time earlier. Attaching an invite for a 45-minute product demo where Ben (Sales Eng) will walk through latency/cost tradeoffs and Marissa (CS) will cover rollout options. Suggested time is Tues 3/21 at 10:00am PT — please accept or propose another slot.

Attachments: redwood-demo-slide-deck.pdf, invitation.ics

If the calendar invite behaves oddly on your side (we occasionally see some clients' Exchange/Outlook strip Meet tokens), reply and I'll send a direct join link.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

From: Elena Park <elena@finosys.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-03-14T12:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Demo: Redwood Inference — scheduling

Hi Rafael —

Thanks — I tried to add the invite but the invite shows in my calendar as "Private event" and there is no join link in the event body. The .ics attachment that came through looks like a 0kb file when I download it and my client (Outlook/Exchange) throws an "ICS parse error". Andrew and I need to confirm the join URL in calendar rather than separately.

Can you send a fallback Zoom (or plain Meet) link we can use while you investigate? Also, if you can resend an .ics that is plain UTF-8 (not obscure binary blob), that would help our calendar admin.

Appreciate it —
Elena
Director of Product, FinoSys
elena@finosys.com

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@finosys.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-03-14T12:31:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Demo: Redwood Inference — scheduling

Elena — thanks for the quick note and the detail. Sorry about the corrupt ICS — we have a known issue where some Exchange servers choke on iCal MIME encoding if the organizer display name includes non-ASCII characters; looks like your tenant treated the attachment as an opaque blob.

Temporary plan (please follow these steps so the calendar entry is correct for your team):

source.subject: Offer page facade and co-sell playbook review
messages:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: AWS Partner Ops <aws-partner-ops@amazon.com>
Date: 2028-03-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Offer page facade and co-sell playbook review

Hi team —

Attached: offer_page_draft_v3.docx, co-sell_playbook_v2.pdf

We need a quick alignment on the AWS offer page copy and the co-sell playbook before the field call with Acme next week. Key asks:
- Final hero headline + subhead that emphasize predictable latency and reserved capacity benefits (one-liner + 8-12 word subhead).
- Two short usage scenarios for the bullets (real customer language, not technical).
- Any claims that need footnotes (pricing / SLA / isolation) called out for Rafael to review.
- Confirm AMI/AMI tag language and private offer footnotes for procurement.

Proposed timeline: please return first-pass edits by EOD Thursday so we can iterate and share with Acme on Friday. HubSpot deal and shared Drive link below.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1aBcdExampleDriveLink
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/deal/ACME-2028-PRIME

Notes for field: Acme cares about predictable per-token cost for batch analytics and that they can pin model versions in Private deployments. Keep bullets customer-focused.

Thanks —
Marissa
Sr. GTM Ops, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142

---

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-03-01T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Offer page facade and co-sell playbook review

Thanks Marissa — quick pass here. Inline notes in Drive and a few high-level suggestions below:

Hero + subhead
- Current hero reads a bit product-y. Suggest: "Consistent low-latency inference at scale" / subhead: "Reserved GPU pools for predictable throughput and lower per-token costs."

Bullets (usage scenarios)
- "Customer Support: Serve fast, personalized responses to enterprise customers while reducing cost per interaction."
- "Batch Analytics: Run large-scale document inference overnight with reserved capacity and predictable pricing."

Callouts / footnotes
- Flag any statements about cost savings with a footnote: "Savings dependent on model/sequence mix; contact sales for estimate." Rafael, can you flag if that suffices?

AMI / procurement
- We should avoid AMI-specific instructions on the public page; for private offers include a short "Technical requirements" section with a link to the AMI onboarding doc.

I uploaded a marked PDF: offer_page_comments.v1.pdf (in Drive).

-Kim
Director, Product Marketing

---

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, AWS Partner Ops <aws-partner-ops@amazon.com>
Date: 2028-03-01T11:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Offer page facade and co-sell playbook review

Appreciate the draft — two product notes from my side: 

1) Hero alternative (customer POV): "Ship AI features reliably — without surprises." Subhead: "Unified inference runtime for hosted, dedicated, or private deployments with SLA-backed capacity."

2) Usage scenario addendum: include a short "what success looks like" metric for each scenario (e.g., ">3x reduction in tail-latency failures for chat ops", "<10% variance in nightly inference job completion time"). Keep metrics qualitative on the public page but we can show quantitative examples in field playbook.

Also: recommend adding an "enterprise controls" bullet: "VPC, KMS, and audit logs for regulatory workloads."

I'll add suggested copy into the Drive doc this afternoon.

-Jonas
Product Lead, Inference Runtime

---

From: Sally King <sally.king@acme.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <tom.nguyen@acme.com>, AWS Partner Ops <aws-partner-ops@amazon.com>
Date: 2028-03-02T08:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Offer page facade and co-sell playbook review

Thanks for the draft — Acme validated internally and a couple quick asks: 

- Can we see 2–3 short field-ready one-pagers (1 page each) that map the hero messages to buyer personas (Platform Eng, Security, Product)?
- Procurement asked us to add sample contract language for data isolation and retention. Rafael, can you advise?

Also want to confirm a quick demo + field alignment on Wed 3/7 at 1pm PT if that still works.

On Wed, Mar 2, 2028 at 8:00 AM Sally King <sally.king@acme.com> wrote:
> Looking forward to the call — please include a short slide that shows "How we price reserved capacity" in plain English.

Best,
Sally King
Director, ML Platform
Acme Data Systems

---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Sally King <sally.king@acme.com>
Date: 2028-03-03T13:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Offer page facade and co-sell playbook review

I've reviewed the draft hero/claims. A few legal redlines and suggested footnotes attached (pricing_footnotes.docx). Highlights: 
- Any assertive claim on "lower per-token costs" must be qualified: include a footnote similar to Kim suggested.
- For AMI/private-offer language, add a short procurement note: "Customer's cloud account and entitlement mapping required; see private offer onboarding doc."
- For data isolation claims, propose this sample clause for Acme procurement to review: "Redwood will ensure tenant isolation for dedicated deployments and log access is controlled via customer KMS keys; retention policies to be mutually agreed."

I'll be on the Wed 3/7 session to answer procurement/legal questions.

-Rafael
Legal Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachment: pricing_footnotes.docx

---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Sally King <sally.king@acme.com>, AWS Partner Ops <aws-partner-ops@amazon.com>
Date: 2028-03-04T15:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Offer page facade and co-sell playbook review

Great feedback — consolidated plan and next steps: 

1) Kimberly + Jonas to lock hero, subhead, and two customer-facing scenarios by EOD Monday 3/6. Please keep two variants (customer POV + technical POV).
2) Rafael to approve final footnotes and provide suggested procurement clause (Rafael — target EOB Monday 3/6).
3) Marissa to assemble three 1-pagers for Platform Eng / Security / Product and share DocuSign-ready procurement snippet for Acme prior to Wed 3/7 demo.
4) Field playbook: include pricing explainer slide + sample monthly cost math for a mid-size deployment. Kim, Jonas — please add the metrics notes Jonas suggested into the playbook.

HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/deal/ACME-2028-PRIME (I'll update deal stage to "Field Review" after we post the one-pagers).
DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/recipient/ExampleDocuSignLink
Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1aBcdExampleDriveLink

If anyone can't meet the timelines above, flag now. Otherwise I'll circulate the consolidated package to Acme and AWS Partner Ops on Tuesday AM.

Thanks all —
Marissa
Sr. GTM Ops, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142

---

Notes for field: Acme cares about predictable per-token cost for batch analytics and that they can pin model versions in Private deployments. Keep bullets customer-focused.

Thanks —
Marissa
Sr. GTM Ops, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-03-01T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Offer page facade and co-sell playbook review

Thanks Marissa — quick pass here. Inline notes in Drive and a few high-level suggestions below:

Hero + subhead
- Current hero reads a bit product-y. Suggest: \"Consistent low-latency inference at scale\" / subhead: \"Reserved GPU pools for predictable throughput and lower per-token costs.\"

Bullets (usage scenarios)
- \"Customer Support: Serve fast, personalized responses to enterprise customers while reducing cost per interaction.\"
- \"Batch Analytics: Run large-scale document inference overnight with reserved capacity and predictable pricing.\"

Callouts / footnotes
- Flag any statements about cost savings with a footnote: \"Savings dependent on model/sequence mix; contact sales for estimate.\" Rafael, can you flag if that suffices?

AMI / procurement
- We should avoid AMI-specific instructions on the public page; for private offers include a short \"Technical requirements\" section with a link to the AMI onboarding doc.

I uploaded a marked PDF: offer_page_comments.v1.pdf (in Drive).

-Kim
Director, Product Marketing

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, AWS Partner Ops <aws-partner-ops@amazon.com>

Appreciate the draft — two product notes from my side: 

1) Hero alternative (customer POV): \"Ship AI features reliably — without surprises.\" Subhead: \"Unified inference runtime for hosted, dedicated, or private deployments with SLA-backed capacity.\"

2) Usage scenario addendum: include a short \"what success looks like\" metric for each scenario (e.g., \">3x reduction in tail-latency failures for chat ops\", \"<10% variance in nightly inference job completion time\"). Keep metrics qualitative on the public page but we can show quantitative examples in field playbook.

Also: recommend adding an \"enterprise controls\" bullet: \"VPC, KMS, and audit logs for regulatory workloads.\"

I'll add suggested copy into the Drive doc this afternoon.

-Jonas
Product Lead, Inference Runtime

- For data isolation claims, propose this sample clause for Acme procurement to review: \"Redwood will ensure tenant isolation for dedicated deployments and log access is controlled via customer KMS keys; retention policies to be mutually agreed.\"

source.subject: Medisynth RFP — evaluation credit scaling & SLA guardrails
messages:
From: Peter Chu <peter.chu@medisynth.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <alicia@medisynth.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Feb 2028 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: RFP: Medisynth bakeoff — pilot credits & parity ask

Hi Rafael,

Thanks for taking our call last week. We have an upcoming RFP bakeoff where Medisynth will be running side-by-side comparisons against a couple of incumbents (CloudAI and EdgePrompt). To run a meaningful benchmark across our core workflows (clinical note generation, triage classification, and embedding search), we need a predictable evaluation window and at least one round of model-parity access to ensure the experiment isn't biased by throttling or stripped-down variants.

High level ask: would Redwood provide a pilot credit package for a 6-week evaluation and guarantee access to the listed model profiles (including a quantized/responsive variant) for parity? The RFP is attached.

We expect ~120k tokens of heavy-generation work plus embedding calls during the period. Procurement is cc'd for contract routing.

Attachments: MediSynth_RFP.pdf (RFP brief)

Can you confirm feasibility and any gating items (SOC2 evidence, data retention constraints, or NDA)?

Best,
Peter Chu
Director, ML Systems | Medisynth Health


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Peter Chu <peter.chu@medisynth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Feb 2028 10:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: RFP: Medisynth bakeoff — pilot credits & parity ask

Hi Peter — appreciate the RFP. Quick read-through: we can support a time-boxed pilot and provide model variants suitable for apples-to-apples benchmarking, but a few operational guardrails are required before we green-light credits.

What we typically need:
- Signed pilot terms (limited-time credit + usage telemetry)
- Completed security questionnaire (SOC 2 scope)
- Confirmation on whether the evaluation data is production PHI (affects on-prem/private discussions)

Proposed starter package: 100k gen tokens + 25k embedding units (6-week window), subject to SOC2 questionnaire and acceptance of pilot terms. If that works I will loop in procurement to issue a pilot SOW stub.

Cc'ing product ops for sizing and legal for standard guardrails.

Rafael
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachment: Pilot_SOW_draft.docx (stub)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Feb 2028 11:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Internal: Medisynth RFP — credit sizing + guardrails

Team — looping you in to decide on the final credit sizing and any special SLA language for bakeoffs. Key points from external:
- They call for parity access (no throttled model variant)
- Estimated 120k heavy-generation tokens + embeddings, over ~6 weeks
- They are comparing us to CloudAI — likely sensitive to latency profiles

My recommendation:
- Approve 150k gen-token equivalent (we convert embedding units into token-equivalent for simplicity), expire after 45 days from activation.
- Require SOC2 questionnaire completion and a signed pilot terms addendum that includes: non-resale clause, telemetry sharing (anonymized usage stats), and a clause preventing the use of results for benchmarking against us without prior notice (protects IP).
- Add a short perf-SLO note: during test window we will try to maintain median p95 response latency consistent with our public profile; if we can't meet parity we offer to pause the comparison and provide a make-good credit.

Risks: setting a high precedent for RFP credits, and potential for excessive support load from their engs. Operationally we should gate activation until security questionnaire is returned.

Would like sign-off from Vanessa + input from Monica on legal wording.

Rafael

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Feb 2028 13:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Internal: Medisynth RFP — credit sizing + guardrails

Rafael — reasonable framing. Approve 150k equivalent but please include the following before we cut the credits:
- Explicit data retention option: 30 days post-pilot unless they request otherwise in writing.
- A short performance addendum: p95 latency target for generation calls under specified seq-length buckets; failure to meet that during their run should trigger a good-faith review and potential top-up (not automatic refund).
- No public head-to-head comparisons without a Redwood review window (30 days) to dispute measurement methodology.

Monica — can you work with Legal to bake the above into the pilot terms and produce a one-page sign-off? Vanessa — if OK I will route for a manager approval and we can finalize credit issuance.

Thanks,
Karthik
VP, Sales Operations

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Peter Chu <peter.chu@medisynth.com>, Alicia Gomez <alicia@medisynth.com>, procurement@medisynth.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Feb 2028 16:05:00 -08:00
Subject: RE: RFP: Medisynth bakeoff — pilot credits & parity ask

Peter / Alicia — thanks for the patience while we coordinated internally. Good news: we can approve a pilot credit package for the bakeoff under the following conditions:

What we are approving:
- 150,000 token-equivalent evaluation credit (includes generation + embedding conversions)
- Evaluation window: 45 days from activation

Pre-activation gating items:
1) Completed SOC 2 questionnaire (we attached our standard form to the pilot SOW).
2) Signed one-page pilot addendum (non-resale, telemetry sharing, 30-day data retention default, 30-day review window for any public head-to-head).
3) Confirmation of the procurement contact and a P.O. or DocuSign sign-off.

Operational notes:
- We will provide the model variants requested for parity testing; we will also add a perf-SLO clause in the addendum (p95 guidance by seq-length) and a discretionary make-good mechanic if an obvious variance is caused by our infra.
- Attachments: Pilot_SOW_draft.docx, MediSynth_RFP.pdf. DocuSign link for pilot addendum: https://redwood.app/docusign/DEAL-2028-0041

Next steps: if you can return the SOC2 questionnaire and sign the addendum, procurement can trigger activation and we will provision the credits and parity model endpoints.

Looking forward — Rafael
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Rafael.mendes@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

---
Attachments (stubs): Pilot_SOW_draft.docx (SOW stub), MediSynth_RFP.pdf (RFP brief)

source.subject: Foresight Health - sudden 429s & latency spike (burst/quota window)
messages:
From: Elena Rossi <elena@foresighthealth.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Cc: support@foresighthealth.com
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Production errors this morning - sudden 429s and latency spike

Marcus —

We started seeing a large fraction of requests returning 429 and end-to-end latency spiking from ~120ms to >1.5s starting 08:45 PT this morning. The impact affected our patient-facing search and some ingestion jobs. Attached is a 5-minute granularity traffic + 429 rate extract (traffic-20271110.png).

We rely on a steady sustained throughput for the search indexer; this window of backpressure caused a backlog that still hasn't fully recovered. Can you confirm whether any rate-limiting or quota changes were rolled out this morning? Our SRE has a timeline if that helps — we can jump on a call.

We need:
- Immediate mitigation to reduce 429s (temporary relaxation if possible)
- Short explanation of root cause and ETA for fix
- Any SLA/credit guidance since this affected production traffic

Thanks — Elena
Elena Rossi
Head of Platform Engineering, Foresight Health

[attachment: traffic-20271110.png]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@foresighthealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2027 09:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Production errors this morning - sudden 429s and latency spike

Elena — thanks for the quick heads up and the graph. I'm escalating to infra and CS immediately.

Short plan:
1) Rafael, can you check burst/quota windows for Foresight's tenant (tenant id: foresight-prod) and confirm if a policy change or a newly-applied compensation rule ran at ~08:40?
2) Laura/Neha: I'll coordinate communications to the account and track SLA/credit options.
3) Elena: we'll propose a temporary relaxation of burst limits and extend the token-window to smooth the spike while we investigate root cause. ETA for a mitigation step: 20–30 minutes.

I will keep you updated here and open a Jira (INF-7892).

—Marcus
Account Lead, Redwood Inference

On Tue, Nov 10, 2027 at 09:12 AM Elena Rossi <elena@foresighthealth.com> wrote:
> Marcus —
> 
> We started seeing a large fraction of requests returning 429 and end-to-end latency spiking from ~120ms to >1.5s starting 08:45 PT this morning....

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2027 09:38:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Production errors this morning - sudden 429s and latency spike

On it — first pass findings:
- No rolling deploys to serving runtime at 08:40 for foresight-prod.
- We did, however, push a policy config change to the global rate engine at 07:50 that added a new "burst_window" override for a small set of tenants. The intent was to give short bursts extra headroom, but the default fallthrough for the token_bucket window used a 10s micro-window AND left the burst_capacity unchanged.
- Combination result: when a sustained high-density burst hit (08:44–08:52) our enforcement engine evaluated the micro-window repeatedly, quickly exhausting per-window tokens and flipping into hard-throttle mode instead of smoothing over a slightly longer 60s window.

Action taken (09:36):
- Temporarily increased burst_capacity for foresight-prod by 3x and extended the token_bucket window from 10s -> 60s.
- Added an immediate rule to prefer windowed accumulation (prefer_longer_windows=true) for the tenant while we validate.

Result: 429s dropped from ~32% -> ~4% over the next 6 minutes; latency returned toward baseline.

Next steps:
- I'll push a config diff to infra repo (PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/456) with a proper default for burst_window fallback and a unit test covering sustained bursts.
- Postmortem and policy hardening planned; I'll need logs from the policy evaluator for the 08:40–09:00 window (will attach).

Rafael
Senior Infra Engineer

[attachment: burst-policy-diff.yml]

Subject: Re: Production errors this morning - sudden 429s and latency spike

Elena — apologies for the interruption. Thank you for the data; Rafael's mitigation worked and we've been monitoring recovery.

A few notes for the account team and docs:
- Root cause (preliminary): a mis-specified burst_window fallback in a global policy coupled with a small micro-window for token accumulation (10s) caused token exhaustion to be detected repeatedly instead of amortized over 60s.
- Impact: request-level throttling for ingest and queries during ~08:44–08:52 PT; queueing/backlog persisted for ~25 minutes after mitigation while worker pools caught up.
- SLA/credits: Marcus and I will put together an initial estimate for downtime vs. SLA terms; we'll also offer a one-off credit for the incident while we complete the postmortem.

I will follow up with the exact timeline and a short note you can share with your execs. We intend to publish a short postmortem and the configuration changes within 72 hours.

Regards,
Laura Bennett

source.subject: Velocity-linked prepay structure for Octavia Health — request for approval
messages:
From: David Jones <david.jones@octaviahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — initial ask

Hi Logan,

Quick note after Friday's exec sync — Octavia is evaluating a 3-year prepay but wants the price to reflect a velocity-based stepdown. They'd prefer an initial year at a higher discount to accommodate onboarding and usage ramp, with built-in protections if usage growth doesn't meet forecast.

High level ask: can Redwood propose a 24–36 month prepay that: 1) frontloads ~40% of committed spend in year 1, 2) offers a stepped discount schedule tied to quarterly usage tiers, and 3) includes a modest mid-contract true-up (no clawbacks beyond agreed escalator). Also need an initial SOW draft and a pricing model we can use for procurement.

We have procurement and legal loops (cc'd in later mails). Timing: they want a proposal by EOD Wed PST.

Appreciate the quick turnaround —
David
Director, Cloud & AI Programs
Octavia Health

---
Attachments: none

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: David Jones <david.jones@octaviahealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — initial ask

David — thanks, and great to hear Octavia is ready to move toward a committed structure. I put together a working model and a draft SOW and attached both. High level from our side:

- Proposed term: 36 months (we can run 24mo variant if you prefer).
- Payment: annual prepay with optional frontload (we modeled 40% year 1 frontload as requested).
- Discount schedule: stepdown quarterly if usage exceeds thresholds; baseline discount applied to committed units.
- True-up: quarterly true-up with a one-time annual true-up cap (no retroactive clawbacks beyond agreed escalator).

Internal note to finance/legal (Kimberly, Neha): can we greenlight a structure that allows a larger Y1 effective discount via frontload while keeping our EAC exposure within acceptable underwriting? The attached pricing model marks a range of Year1 effective discount between 18–28% depending on ramp assumptions. Rafael — FYI for deployment capacity planning.

I need an internal go/no-go by Tue EOD so I can finalize the doc for Octavia Wed EOD.

Thanks —
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: Octavia_prepay_pricing_model_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), Octavia_SOW_draft_v1.pdf (attachment stub)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 08:43:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — initial ask (internal)

Logan — reviewed the model quickly. A few thoughts:

1) Frontload / discount trade: we can accept a higher effective discount in Year 1 if the contract includes a structured stepdown tied to clear usage bands and a modest true-up. Recommend max Year1 effective discount 25% (based on model assumptions in the spreadsheet).

2) Ramp protection: require quarterly reporting cadence + right to convert to usage pricing if actual usage falls below 60% of forecast for two consecutive quarters (this protects runway).

3) Accounting / revenue recognition: finance will need the annual prepay recognized over the committed term — confirm customer is OK with annual invoicing vs single upfront deposit.

4) SLAs & credits: no bespoke SLA credits in Y1 beyond standard SLAs. Any SLA exceptions must be handled as addendum.

If we hold to those guardrails, I'm comfortable approving the pricing band. Neha — if you also sign off on legal language around the true-up cap, Logan can send to Octavia.

-Kimberly
Director, Finance — Commercial Deals

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 10:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — initial ask (internal)

Thanks Kimberly — I reviewed the draft SOW and the pricing worksheet. Legally we can accept the proposed true-up mechanism with the following edits:

- Add explicit definition of "usage" (API tokens + embeddings units) and the measurement window (UTC calendar quarter).
- Add a 30-day notice for any change to billing cadence and confirm Octavia acknowledges annual invoicing.
- Cap our liability for mid-contract adjustments to the delta between committed spend and actual usage for the quarter(s) in question; no retroactive enforcement beyond the quarter.

Once I add these clauses to the SOW I can approve language for Logan to send externally. I will not approve contractual language that allows conversion to monthly bill-at-usage without a separate amendment.

-Neha
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: David Jones <david.jones@octaviahealth.com>
Cc: Octavia Legal <legal@octaviahealth.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 16:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — velocity-linked prepay structure (proposal)

David — thanks for the patience. Per our internal reviews we've finalized a proposal that aligns with Octavia's ask while preserving Redwood's protections. Key points (summary):

- Term: 36 months.
- Payment: annual prepay; modeled option for a 40% Y1 frontload. Octavia can elect 24mo instead; we've included both models in the attached worksheet.
- Discounting: Year 1 effective discount up to 25% (dependent on frontload). Quarterly stepdown schedule tied to usage bands (detailed in spreadsheet).
- True-up: quarterly true-up measured on UTC calendar quarters; cap on retroactive adjustments limited to quarter delta; no retroactive clawbacks beyond agreed escalator.
- Ramp protection: if Octavia consumes <60% of forecast for two consecutive quarters, Redwood reserves the right to transition to pay-as-you-go pricing for subsequent quarters after a 30-day notice.
- SLAs: standard SLAs apply; no bespoke credits in Year 1.

Attachments: updated Octavia_prepay_pricing_model_v2.xlsx, Octavia_SOW_draft_v2.pdf (stubs).

Next steps: if Octavia is good with the guardrails above, we can issue an SOW for signature and draft a short amendment for the billing cadence. Rafael, let me know capacity constraints for a potential October onboarding; Kimberly/Neha, confirm final language and I'll send the redlines to Octavia Legal.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: Octavia_prepay_pricing_model_v2.xlsx, Octavia_SOW_draft_v2.pdf

title: Velocity-linked prepay structure for Octavia Health — request for approval
preview:
From: David Jones <david.jones@octaviahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 12 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — initial ask

Hi Logan,

Quick note after Friday's exec sync — Octavia is evaluating a 3-year prepay but wants the price to reflect a velocity-based stepdown. They'd prefer an initial year at a higher discount to accommodate onboarding and usage ramp, with built-in protections if usage growth doesn't meet forecast.

High level ask: can Redwood propose a 24–36 month prepay that: 1) frontloads ~40% of committed spend in year 1, 2) offers a stepped discount schedule tied to quarterly usage tiers, and 3) includes a modest mid-contract true-up (no clawbacks beyond agreed escalator). Also need an initial SOW draft and a pricing model we can use for procurement.

We have procurement and legal loops (cc'd in later mails). Timing: they want a proposal by EOD Wed PST.

Appreciate the quick turnaround —
David
Director, Cloud & AI Programs
Octavia Health

---
Attachments: none


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: David Jones <david.jones@octaviahealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — initial ask

David — thanks, and great to hear Octavia is ready to move toward a committed structure. I put together a working model and a draft SOW and attached both. High level from our side:

- Proposed term: 36 months (we can run 24mo variant if you prefer).
- Payment: annual prepay with optional frontload (we modeled 40% year 1 frontload as requested).
- Discount schedule: stepdown quarterly if usage exceeds thresholds; baseline discount applied to committed units.
- True-up: quarterly true-up with a one-time annual true-up cap (no retroactive clawbacks beyond agreed escalator).

Internal note to finance/legal (Kimberly, Neha): can we greenlight a structure that allows a larger Y1 effective discount via frontload while keeping our EAC exposure within acceptable underwriting? The attached pricing model marks a range of Year1 effective discount between 18–28% depending on ramp assumptions. Rafael — FYI for deployment capacity planning.

I need an internal go/no-go by Tue EOD so I can finalize the doc for Octavia Wed EOD.

Thanks —
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: Octavia_prepay_pricing_model_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), Octavia_SOW_draft_v1.pdf (attachment stub)


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 08:43:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — initial ask (internal)

Logan — reviewed the model quickly. A few thoughts:

1) Frontload / discount trade: we can accept a higher effective discount in Year 1 if the contract includes a structured stepdown tied to clear usage bands and a modest true-up. Recommend max Year1 effective discount 25% (based on model assumptions in the spreadsheet).

2) Ramp protection: require quarterly reporting cadence + right to convert to usage pricing if actual usage falls below 60% of forecast for two consecutive quarters (this protects runway).

3) Accounting / revenue recognition: finance will need the annual prepay recognized over the committed term — confirm customer is OK with annual invoicing vs single upfront deposit.

4) SLAs & credits: no bespoke SLA credits in Y1 beyond standard SLAs. Any SLA exceptions must be handled as addendum.

If we hold to those guardrails, I'm comfortable approving the pricing band. Neha — if you also sign off on legal language around the true-up cap, Logan can send to Octavia.

-Kimberly
Director, Finance — Commercial Deals


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 10:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — initial ask (internal)

Thanks Kimberly — I reviewed the draft SOW and the pricing worksheet. Legally we can accept the proposed true-up mechanism with the following edits:

- Add explicit definition of \"usage\" (API tokens + embeddings units) and the measurement window (UTC calendar quarter).
- Add a 30-day notice for any change to billing cadence and confirm Octavia acknowledges annual invoicing.
- Cap our liability for mid-contract adjustments to the delta between committed spend and actual usage for the quarter(s) in question; no retroactive enforcement beyond the quarter.

Once I add these clauses to the SOW I can approve language for Logan to send externally. I will not approve contractual language that allows conversion to monthly bill-at-usage without a separate amendment.

-Neha
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: David Jones <david.jones@octaviahealth.com>
Cc: Octavia Legal <legal@octaviahealth.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 16:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Velocity-linked prepay — velocity-linked prepay structure (proposal)

David — thanks for the patience. Per our internal reviews we've finalized a proposal that aligns with Octavia's ask while preserving Redwood's protections. Key points (summary):

- Term: 36 months.
- Payment: annual prepay; modeled option for a 40% Y1 frontload. Octavia can elect 24mo instead; we've included both models in the attached worksheet.
- Discounting: Year 1 effective discount up to 25% (dependent on frontload). Quarterly stepdown schedule tied to usage bands (detailed in spreadsheet).
- True-up: quarterly true-up measured on UTC calendar quarters; cap on retroactive adjustments limited to quarter delta; no retroactive clawbacks beyond agreed escalator.
- Ramp protection: if Octavia consumes <60% of forecast for two consecutive quarters, Redwood reserves the right to transition to pay-as-you-go pricing for subsequent quarters after a 30-day notice.
- SLAs: standard SLAs apply; no bespoke credits in Year 1.

Attachments: updated Octavia_prepay_pricing_model_v2.xlsx, Octavia_SOW_draft_v2.pdf (stubs).

Next steps: if Octavia is good with the guardrails above, we can issue an SOW for signature and draft a short amendment for the billing cadence. Rafael, let me know capacity constraints for a potential October onboarding; Kimberly/Neha, confirm final language and I'll send the redlines to Octavia Legal.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: Octavia_prepay_pricing_model_v2.xlsx, Octavia_SOW_draft_v2.pdf

source.subject: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request
messages:
From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Ben/Laura/Rafael — looping you in early for a Deal Desk path on BrightByte's AWS marketplace private offer. Key facts below and a one-page MAP attached.

- Customer: BrightByte Analytics (AI analytics for retail)
- External contacts: Elaine Park (GT contact), Tom Walker (procurement)
- Ask: 35–40% concession on list price for an AWS Marketplace private offer + a 30-day SLA credit (to be documented in the PO)
- Committed ARR: they are prepared to sign a 24-month private offer for $480,000 TCV (annualized $240k) if we can meet the concession and marketplace terms
- Strategic rationale: brightbyte will co-sell with our AWS partner and is a marquee reference for retail vertical; they plan to deploy across 3 regions and target a larger multi-year expansion pipeline

Why I'm asking for an exception: our standard marketplace concession cap for co-sell autopromos is 25–30% depending on tier. BrightByte is pushing for 40% citing the need to match a competing consolidated SaaS procurement bundle. They want a private offer signed by 9/21 to meet their procurement window.

Attachments: BrightByte_MutualActionPlan.pdf, draft PO (PO_BB-4787.xlsx).

Next steps I want from each of you:
- Ben: cost/margin signoff and final concession limit for marketplace listing
- Laura: partner/marketplace ops feasibility and any listing timing constraints
- Rafael: pricing ops — confirm whether we can document the SLA credit as a one-time onboarding credit vs recurring discount (preferred)

Appreciate a quick thumbs — procurement timeline is tight (Elaine says they need final terms next week).

Thanks,
Mina
Mina Haddad | Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mina.haddad@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0198

---

(Attachment: BrightByte_MutualActionPlan.pdf — Mutual action plan with milestones)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Thanks Mina — quick questions before I escalate to Deal Desk formally:
1) If we grant 40% on list for the private offer, what is the net effective ARR for year 1 (after all credits)? Please paste the math here or attach a workbook (I can open the PO_BB-4787.xlsx).
2) Is BrightByte open to a one-time onboarding credit (dollar amount) instead of making the discount deeper across all months? That helps preserve list price integrity for marketplace metrics.
3) Any partner ISV co-sell incentives needed (we may need to approve a partner referral fee outside of the standard marketplace booking)

I can approve up to 30% for private offers without exec sign-off if the margin hit is acceptable. Anything >30% will require finance + CRO signoff and a short memo explaining strategic benefit.

If procurement needs the PO signed by 9/21 we should try to close the internal review by EOD 9/15.

—Ben
Ben Carter | Head of Marketplace Partnerships | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0177
From: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>, procurement@brightbyte.ai
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 12:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Mina/Ben — thanks for looping us in. A few clarifications from procurement side:
- The $480k TCV is locked in if the private offer reflects a 40% concession against list and we can include a 30-day service credit in the PO for the first 90 days (they want an initial SLA safety net while we ramp).
- BrightByte is prepared to sign a 24-month contract and commit to an implementation milestone schedule in the MAP.
- If you prefer a one-time onboarding credit instead of a recurring price reduction, we would accept a $45k onboarding credit applied at signing, but our CFO has said they prefer the discount reflected in the recurring rate for ledger consolidation.

We do have one constraint: our AWS co-sell team needs to see the marketplace private offer published concurrently with our PO acceptance — otherwise co-sell incentives are at risk. Procurement is on a hard timeline to complete internal approvals by 9/21.

Happy to jump on a short call if helpful — Elaine.

—
Elaine Park | Head of Business Operations | BrightByte Analytics
elaine@brightbyte.ai | +1 212-555-0184
From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 13:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Quick numbers (pulled from the PO draft and our pricing grid):
- List ARR (annualized pre-discount): $400,000
- 35% concession => effective ARR year1 = $260,000 (delta to list = $140,000)
- 40% concession => effective ARR year1 = $240,000 (delta to list = $160,000)
- If we convert the concession into a $45k one-time onboarding credit @ signing + 30% recurring discount, effective year1 ~ $260k (similar to 35% pure discount)

HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/7654321 (contains customer commitment letter and PO draft). MAP attached again.

Laura — can you confirm whether marketplace ops can publish the private offer on the AWS marketplace in parallel with PO signing? That is a hard ask from BrightByte's co-sell leader. Rafael — any immediate flags on the SLA-as-credit vs onboarding credit treatment?

Thanks,
Mina
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Thanks all — operational notes from partner/marketplace side:
- We can publish a private offer concurrently with PO acceptance but we need the finalized pricing terms (exact SKU price after concession) and the legal PO signed. Publishing typically takes 48–72 hours once I get the confirmed SKU mapping and price sheet.
- For the SLA ask: marketplace billing practices don't support recurring negative adjustments on list for co-sell metrics without special handling. A one-time onboarding credit (applied as a credit memo during first invoice) is operationally the cleanest approach and preserves our marketplace metrics.
- Given the strategic co-sell value and the $480k TCV, I'm comfortable recommending an exception up to 35% concession plus a one-time $45k onboarding credit documented in the PO as a separate line item (so BrightByte sees the dollar credit but the recurring SKU price is only 35% off).

Action items I need to proceed:
1) Written Deal Desk approval for concession >30% (formal memo or approval email)
2) Confirmed PO with separate credit line for the $45k onboarding amount, if BrightByte prefers that route
3) A DocuSign link for execution so partner ops can publish the private offer within 72 hours of signature

If Deal Desk signs off, I'll coordinate publishing and notify BrightByte's AWS co-sell rep.

—Laura
Laura Bennett | Partner Ops Manager | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

All — after reviewing the margin impact and strategic upside (co-sell pipeline + reference value), Deal Desk approves the following exception:

- Approved concession: 35% off list for AWS private offer SKUs
- Approved one-time onboarding credit: $45,000 (separate credit line on PO)
- Conditions: 24-month private offer commitment at the confirmed TCV ($480k) and DocuSign-signed PO within 7 calendar days of this approval (i.e., by 2027-09-22)
- Marketplace ops to publish private offer within 72 hours of PO signature and notify AWS co-sell team

Rafael/Laura — please proceed with operational steps and confirm when private offer is published. Mina — please confirm with BrightByte that the credit will be a separate PO line item (per Laura's preference).

Ben Carter | Head of Marketplace Partnerships | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
