Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 13:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Quick numbers (pulled from the PO draft and our pricing grid):
- List ARR (annualized pre-discount): $400,000
- 35% concession => effective ARR year1 = $260,000 (delta to list = $140,000)
- 40% concession => effective ARR year1 = $240,000 (delta to list = $160,000)
- If we convert the concession into a $45k one-time onboarding credit @ signing + 30% recurring discount, effective year1 ~ $260k (similar to 35% pure discount)

HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/7654321 (contains customer commitment letter and PO draft). MAP attached again.

Laura — can you confirm whether marketplace ops can publish the private offer on the AWS marketplace in parallel with PO signing? That is a hard ask from BrightByte's co-sell leader. Rafael — any immediate flags on the SLA-as-credit vs onboarding credit treatment?

Thanks,
Mina


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Thanks all — operational notes from partner/marketplace side:
- We can publish a private offer concurrently with PO acceptance but we need the finalized pricing terms (exact SKU price after concession) and the legal PO signed. Publishing typically takes 48–72 hours once I get the confirmed SKU mapping and price sheet.
- For the SLA ask: marketplace billing practices don't support recurring negative adjustments on list for co-sell metrics without special handling. A one-time onboarding credit (applied as a credit memo during first invoice) is operationally the cleanest approach and preserves our marketplace metrics.
- Given the strategic co-sell value and the $480k TCV, I'm comfortable recommending an exception up to 35% concession plus a one-time $45k onboarding credit documented in the PO as a separate line item (so BrightByte sees the dollar credit but the recurring SKU price is only 35% off).

Action items I need to proceed:
1) Written Deal Desk approval for concession >30% (formal memo or approval email)
2) Confirmed PO with separate credit line for the $45k onboarding amount, if BrightByte prefers that route
3) A DocuSign link for execution so partner ops can publish the private offer within 72 hours of signature

If Deal Desk signs off, I'll coordinate publishing and notify BrightByte's AWS co-sell rep.

—Laura
Laura Bennett | Partner Ops Manager | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

All — after reviewing the margin impact and strategic upside (co-sell pipeline + reference value), Deal Desk approves the following exception:

- Approved concession: 35% off list for AWS private offer SKUs
- Approved one-time onboarding credit: $45,000 (separate credit line on PO)
- Conditions: 24-month private offer commitment at the confirmed TCV ($480k) and DocuSign-signed PO within 7 calendar days of this approval (i.e., by 2027-09-22)
- Marketplace ops to publish private offer within 72 hours of PO signature and notify AWS co-sell team

Rafael/Laura — please proceed with operational steps and confirm when private offer is published. Mina — please confirm with BrightByte that the credit will be a separate PO line item (per Laura's preference).

Ben Carter | Head of Marketplace Partnerships | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
text:
From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Ben/Laura/Rafael — looping you in early for a Deal Desk path on BrightByte's AWS marketplace private offer. Key facts below and a one-page MAP attached.

- Customer: BrightByte Analytics (AI analytics for retail)
- External contacts: Elaine Park (GT contact), Tom Walker (procurement)
- Ask: 35–40% concession on list price for an AWS Marketplace private offer + a 30-day SLA credit (to be documented in the PO)
- Committed ARR: they are prepared to sign a 24-month private offer for $480,000 TCV (annualized $240k) if we can meet the concession and marketplace terms
- Strategic rationale: brightbyte will co-sell with our AWS partner and is a marquee reference for retail vertical; they plan to deploy across 3 regions and target a larger multi-year expansion pipeline

Why I'm asking for an exception: our standard marketplace concession cap for co-sell autopromos is 25–30% depending on tier. BrightByte is pushing for 40% citing the need to match a competing consolidated SaaS procurement bundle. They want a private offer signed by 9/21 to meet their procurement window.

Attachments: BrightByte_MutualActionPlan.pdf, draft PO (PO_BB-4787.xlsx).

Next steps I want from each of you:
- Ben: cost/margin signoff and final concession limit for marketplace listing
- Laura: partner/marketplace ops feasibility and any listing timing constraints
- Rafael: pricing ops — confirm whether we can document the SLA credit as a one-time onboarding credit vs recurring discount (preferred)

Appreciate a quick thumbs — procurement timeline is tight (Elaine says they need final terms next week).

Thanks,
Mina
Mina Haddad | Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mina.haddad@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0198

---

(Attachment: BrightByte_MutualActionPlan.pdf — Mutual action plan with milestones)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>, Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Thanks Mina — quick questions before I escalate to Deal Desk formally:
1) If we grant 40% on list for the private offer, what is the net effective ARR for year 1 (after all credits)? Please paste the math here or attach a workbook (I can open the PO_BB-4787.xlsx).
2) Is BrightByte open to a one-time onboarding credit (dollar amount) instead of making the discount deeper across all months? That helps preserve list price integrity for marketplace metrics.
3) Any partner ISV co-sell incentives needed (we may need to approve a partner referral fee outside of the standard marketplace booking)

I can approve up to 30% for private offers without exec sign-off if the margin hit is acceptable. Anything >30% will require finance + CRO signoff and a short memo explaining strategic benefit.

If procurement needs the PO signed by 9/21 we should try to close the internal review by EOD 9/15.

—Ben
Ben Carter | Head of Marketplace Partnerships | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0177


From: Elaine Park <elaine@brightbyte.ai>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Walker <tom@brightbyte.ai>, procurement@brightbyte.ai
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 12:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightByte - marketplace private offer: co-sell concession & SLA credit request

Mina/Ben — thanks for looping us in. A few clarifications from procurement side:
- The $480k TCV is locked in if the private offer reflects a 40% concession against list and we can include a 30-day service credit in the PO for the first 90 days (they want an initial SLA safety net while we ramp).
- BrightByte is prepared to sign a 24-month contract and commit to an implementation milestone schedule in the MAP.
- If you prefer a one-time onboarding credit instead of a recurring price reduction, we would accept a $45k onboarding credit applied at signing, but our CFO has said they prefer the discount reflected in the recurring rate for ledger consolidation.

We do have one constraint: our AWS co-sell team needs to see the marketplace private offer published concurrently with our PO acceptance — otherwise co-sell incentives are at risk. Procurement is on a hard timeline to complete internal approvals by 9/21.

Happy to jump on a short call if helpful — Elaine.

—
Elaine Park | Head of Business Operations | BrightByte Analytics
elaine@brightbyte.ai | +1 212-555-0184

source.subject: MAP action grid: procurement & legal onboarding (Arcadia Systems)
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, procurement@arcadia.com
Date: Tue, 7 Sep 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP kickoff: procurement & legal action grid for Arcadia Systems

Team —

Per the MAP cadence with Arcadia (kickoff today), I put together an action-grid to keep procurement and legal aligned while we close. High level: MSA redlines inbound from Arcadia Legal, order form still in draft, vendor onboarding requires a short vendor questionnaire + EULA confirmation for their environment.

Quick asks:
- Karthik: can you confirm vendor setup prerequisites (IP allowlist, monitoring access, test account) and ETA?
- Naomi: can you triage the attached Arcadia_MSA_redlines_20270909.pdf once it lands and call out must-haves vs. negotiables?
- Sean: reach out to VendorCo for onboarding window and confirm they can start staging by Sep 21.

I'll host a 30-minute checkpoint Friday to reconcile any outstanding blockers. Attaching the Arcadia_MAP_v3 spreadsheet — please update the owners column with firm dates.

Regards,

Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Arcadia_MAP_v3.xlsx (MAP action grid - edit), vendor-onboarding-checklist.xlsx (checklist)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 8 Sep 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: procurement & legal action grid for Arcadia Systems

Jordan / team —

Vendor setup notes:
- VendorCo confirmed we need a staging VPC and a service account. They provided a short onboarding form (I saved to Drive).
- IP allowlist: Arcadia's infra team will give a CIDR block; we need to confirm intended egress ports (will follow up with their procurement).
- Monitoring: we can provide CloudWatch/Prometheus read-only metrics via a read token. Ops prefers read-only for first 30 days.

Blockers:
- Order form must surface the environment SLA and data retention terms; until legal signs off we can't expose production certs.
- VendorCo requires a signed MSA + Order Form before starting scheduled staging work. Their onboarding window is currently Sep 21–24 but they asked for confirmation this week.

I'll update the MAP sheet with the environment checklist and mark items that need procurement sign-off.

Karthik
Director, Partner Ops

Attachment referenced: vendor-onboarding-checklist.xlsx

From: legal@arcadia.com <legal@arcadia.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Sep 2027 14:20:00 -0700
Subject: Arcadia MSA redlines (initial)

Naomi / Jordan —

Attached are our initial redlines to the MSA. Highlights: limitation of liability cap reduction, stronger data residency language, and a request to add a 30-day notice for changes to the service that materially impact compliance. We flagged a few sections as "non-negotiable" for our board.

We can schedule a 45-minute call to walk through the top five items if that helps.

Thanks,

Evelyn Hart
Senior Counsel, Arcadia Systems

Attachment: Arcadia_MSA_redlines_20270909.pdf

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: legal@arcadia.com, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Sep 2027 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Arcadia MSA redlines (initial) — triage and proposed responses

Evelyn / team —

Thanks for the redlines. I did a first pass and categorized items into: Must-Have (legal/contractual blockers), Important (need internal alignment), and Cosmetic (language we can accept).

Top items I propose we accept with modification:
- Data residency: propose a two-year roadmap with checkpoints; offer contractual language for in-scope data and SLA for deletion requests.
- Liability: propose a tiered cap tied to first-year fees — this is our standard compromise.

Must-haves for Arcadia that we can escalate to Product/Security:
- 30-day notice for material compliance changes — we can commit to communication + mitigation plan; product needs to scope what "material" means.
- Audit rights: Arcadia requests annual audit access; we prefer SOC report + targeted evidence sharing.

Next steps / owners:
- Naomi: draft proposed response doc and redline the PDF with comments (due Sep 13).
- Jordan: confirm deal commercial terms to feed liability cap proposal (due Sep 12).
- Karthik: confirm vendor onboarding availability and push vendor to lock Sep 21 if possible (due Sep 13).

I'll set up a short triage call Tue Sep 14 to align before we push the counter.

Naomi Feldman
Head of Legal (Commercial Contracts)
Redwood Inference

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Cc: alice@vendorco.com, onboarding@vendorco.com
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: VendorCo onboarding status and checklist items

Team —

Quick update from VendorCo call:
- VendorCo confirmed staging slot Sep 21 and initial smoke tests Sep 22. They asked for the signed order form or an exec-level email confirmation to start provisioning.
- Onboarding checklist: staging VPC, service account (IAM role), allowlist IPs from Arcadia, test dataset (anonymized), and access to our metrics endpoint.
- VendorCo asked for an SLA appendix to be attached to the order form; they can operate under a 99.5% staging SLA and negotiate production SLA post-onboarding.

Open asks:
- Jordan: can you provide the one-line executive confirmation if legal can't sign before Sep 21? That will let VendorCo reserve the slot.
- Naomi: confirm if DocuSign placeholder + exec confirmation is acceptable to proceed with staging.

Sean Gallagher
Manager, Customer Success

Attachment: vendor-onboarding-checklist.xlsx

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, legal@arcadia.com, procurement@arcadia.com
Date: Wed, 14 Sep 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Friday checkpoint notes + confirmed action items (MAP action grid)

Team — good checkpoint today. Summary of decisions and owners (action grid updated in Drive):

1) MSA redlines: Naomi to produce a counter-redline PDF with inline comments for the top five items. Owner: Naomi. Due: 2027-09-17.
2) Liability cap: Jordan to propose tiered cap (first-year fees basis) and confirm with Arcadia commercial. Owner: Jordan. Due: 2027-09-12 (updated in sheet as completed).
3) Order form: Rafael to produce v2 of the Order Form with SLA appendix placeholder. Owner: Rafael Mendes. Due: 2027-09-15.
4) Vendor onboarding: Karthik to confirm staging prerequisites; Sean to confirm VendorCo slot. Both to confirm whether exec confirmation is required to hold Sep 21. Owners: Karthik Iyer / Sean Gallagher. Due: 2027-09-16.
5) Audit evidence: Naomi to propose SOC + targeted evidence approach as an alternative to annual audit access. Owner: Naomi Feldman. Due: 2027-09-20.

Notes:
- Rafael confirmed he will upload the OrderForm_v2.draft.docx to Drive and create a DocuSign placeholder (link in MAP).
- If Arcadia Legal requires the staging slot held by a signed order form, Jordan will provide an exec-level confirmation email to VendorCo while we continue negotiations.

Next checkpoint: Tue Sep 21 (10:00 PT) — short sync before staging. Please update the action grid before EOD Monday so I can circulate the pre-meeting read.

Thanks all,

Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

Related links: https://drive.company/drive/folders/arcadia-onboarding-2027 (MAP sheet, order form drafts, vendor checklist)
notes: Thread contains MAP action grid, MSA redline triage, order form versioning, and vendor onboarding coordination.
All internal participants are Redwood employees in the sales/procurement/legal/org.
External participants: Arcadia Legal and VendorCo contacts.

2) Liability cap: Jordan to propose tiered cap (first-year fees basis) and confirm with Arcadia commercial. Owner: Jordan. Due: 2027-09-12 (updated in sheet as completed).
3) Order form: Rafael to produce v2 of the Order Form with SLA appendix placeholder. Owner: Rafael Mendes. Due: 2027-09-15.
4) Vendor onboarding: Karthik to confirm staging prerequisites; Sean to confirm VendorCo slot. Both to confirm whether exec confirmation is required to hold Sep 21. Owners: Karthik Iyer / Sean Gallagher. Due: 2027-09-16.
5) Audit evidence: Naomi to propose SOC + targeted evidence approach as an alternative to annual audit access. Owner: Naomi Feldman. Due: 2027-09-20.

Notes:
- Rafael confirmed he will upload the OrderForm_v2.draft.docx to Drive and create a DocuSign placeholder (link in MAP).
- If Arcadia Legal requires the staging slot held by a signed order form, Jordan will provide an exec-level confirmation email to VendorCo while we continue negotiations.

Next checkpoint: Tue Sep 21 (10:00 PT) — short sync before staging. Please update the action grid before EOD Monday so I can circulate the pre-meeting read.

Thanks all,

Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

text:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, procurement@arcadia.com
Date: Tue, 7 Sep 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP kickoff: procurement & legal action grid for Arcadia Systems

Team —

Per the MAP cadence with Arcadia (kickoff today), I put together an action-grid to keep procurement and legal aligned while we close. High level: MSA redlines inbound from Arcadia Legal, order form still in draft, vendor onboarding requires a short vendor questionnaire + EULA confirmation for their environment.

Quick asks:
- Karthik: can you confirm vendor setup prerequisites (IP allowlist, monitoring access, test account) and ETA?
- Naomi: can you triage the attached Arcadia_MSA_redlines_20270909.pdf once it lands and call out must-haves vs. negotiables?
- Sean: reach out to VendorCo for onboarding window and confirm they can start staging by Sep 21.

I'll host a 30-minute checkpoint Friday to reconcile any outstanding blockers. Attaching the Arcadia_MAP_v3 spreadsheet — please update the owners column with firm dates.

Regards,

Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Arcadia_MAP_v3.xlsx (MAP action grid - edit), vendor-onboarding-checklist.xlsx (checklist)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 8 Sep 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: procurement & legal action grid for Arcadia Systems

Jordan / team —

Vendor setup notes:
- VendorCo confirmed we need a staging VPC and a service account. They provided a short onboarding form (I saved to Drive).
- IP allowlist: Arcadia's infra team will give a CIDR block; we need to confirm intended egress ports (will follow up with their procurement).
- Monitoring: we can provide CloudWatch/Prometheus read-only metrics via a read token. Ops prefers read-only for first 30 days.

Blockers:
- Order form must surface the environment SLA and data retention terms; until legal signs off we can't expose production certs.
- VendorCo requires a signed MSA + Order Form before starting scheduled staging work. Their onboarding window is currently Sep 21–24 but they asked for confirmation this week.

I'll update the MAP sheet with the environment checklist and mark items that need procurement sign-off.

Karthik
Director, Partner Ops

Attachment referenced: vendor-onboarding-checklist.xlsx

From: legal@arcadia.com <legal@arcadia.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Sep 2027 14:20:00 -0700
Subject: Arcadia MSA redlines (initial)

Naomi / Jordan —

Attached are our initial redlines to the MSA. Highlights: limitation of liability cap reduction, stronger data residency language, and a request to add a 30-day notice for changes to the service that materially impact compliance. We flagged a few sections as \"non-negotiable\" for our board.

We can schedule a 45-minute call to walk through the top five items if that helps.

Thanks,

Evelyn Hart
Senior Counsel, Arcadia Systems

Attachment: Arcadia_MSA_redlines_20270909.pdf

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: legal@arcadia.com, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Sep 2027 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Arcadia MSA redlines (initial) — triage and proposed responses

Evelyn / team —

Thanks for the redlines. I did a first pass and categorized items into: Must-Have (legal/contractual blockers), Important (need internal alignment), and Cosmetic (language we can accept).

Top items I propose we accept with modification:
- Data residency: propose a two-year roadmap with checkpoints; offer contractual language for in-scope data and SLA for deletion requests.
- Liability: propose a tiered cap tied to first-year fees — this is our standard compromise.

Must-haves for Arcadia that we can escalate to Product/Security:
- 30-day notice for material compliance changes — we can commit to communication + mitigation plan; product needs to scope what \"material\" means.
- Audit rights: Arcadia requests annual audit access; we prefer SOC report + targeted evidence sharing.

Next steps / owners:
- Naomi: draft proposed response doc and redline the PDF with comments (due Sep 13).
- Jordan: confirm deal commercial terms to feed liability cap proposal (due Sep 12).
- Karthik: confirm vendor onboarding availability and push vendor to lock Sep 21 if possible (due Sep 13).

I'll set up a short triage call Tue Sep 14 to align before we push the counter.

Naomi Feldman
Head of Legal (Commercial Contracts)
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Split-invoice payment: EUR/USD routing, intermediary fees & VAT treatment
source.body:
From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@globex.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@globex.com
Date: Mon, 9 Aug 2027 16:12:00 +0100
Subject: Split-invoice payment: routing question

Hi Jonas,

We received Invoice 12345 for Q3 usage. Our treasury prefers to pay part in EUR and part in USD to match our hedging positions. Can you confirm whether Redwood can accept a split wire and provide separate beneficiary instructions for EUR (SEPA) and USD (SWIFT/CHIPS)? Also, we need clarity on how intermediary bank fees are handled so we can book the payment gross vs net for VAT reclaim.

Quick answers we need:
- Separate bank details for EUR and USD?
- Who bears intermediary/correspondent bank charges by default (our side or Redwood)?
- Any reason we should route everything to a single nostro to avoid correspondence fees?

Thanks,
Elena
Finance Operations
Globex Corporation

---

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@globex.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, accounts-payable@globex.com
Date: Mon, 9 Aug 2027 16:32:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Split-invoice payment: routing question

Hi Elena — thanks for the note. Short answer: we can accept a split payment but there are trade-offs for VAT reporting and intermediary fees. Full details below and I attached a template with the recommended routing options (wire_instructions_template.xlsx).

Summary:
- We can accept two legs: EUR (SEPA to our EUR collection account) and USD (SWIFT to our USD correspondent).
- Default fees: our standard terms are "sender bears all charges" (OUR). If you choose BEN or SHA, intermediary/correspondent fees may be deducted in transit and will reduce the amount we receive.
- VAT: for EU VAT reclaim you need a full gross invoice amount reflected in your remittance. If fees are deducted by an intermediary (i.e., we receive less than invoice total), that can complicate reclaim depending on your local tax authority.

Recommendation:
1) If you want clean VAT treatment, set both wires to OUR so Redwood receives the full invoice amounts and there are no deductions for intermediary banks.
2) Alternatively, instruct SHA but add a small margin on the USD leg to cover expected correspondent fees — we can provide typical ranges for USD-SWIFT routing.
3) If you prefer a single nostro to avoid multiple correspondent legs, we can accept a single USD wire to our NYC correspondent and internally convert to EUR; note this may affect the FX rate and Redwood will pass through conversion at our published mid-market + spread.

I've looped in Marissa (Finance) and Rafael (Payments) for fee estimates and exact account numbers.

Best,
Jonas
Head of GTM Partnerships
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood_Invoice_12345.pdf (PDF, 98KB)]
[Attachment: wire_instructions_template.xlsx (Excel, 12KB)]

---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@globex.com>, accounts-payable@globex.com
Date: Mon, 9 Aug 2027 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Split-invoice payment: routing question

Hi all — adding the finance perspective. We prefer to receive the full invoice total (OUR) to avoid reconciliation overhead and VAT shortfalls. Practical notes:

- EUR leg (SEPA): send to our EUR IBAN, always credited in full with negligible intermediary fees if SEPA-to-SEPA. Recommended: EUR leg for the exact EUR portion of invoice; put invoice reference in remittance.

- USD leg (SWIFT): common path is payer bank -> corres bank (e.g., Bank A) -> Redwood nostro. Intermediary fees vary: typical range USD 5–40 depending on chains. If you select SHA, those fees will be split and often deducted by an intermediary beyond either party's control.

- VAT accounting: if you want to reclaim VAT in Italy/Spain/France, tax authorities typically expect the supplier invoice (gross amount) and evidence of full payment. If the USD leg is debited net, you may need a statement from Redwood confirming shortfall and we can issue a credit memo, but that delays reclaim.

Operational options:
A) Two-leg OUR (recommended): you send EUR as OUR to EUR IBAN, USD as OUR to USD SWIFT — Redwood bears no deduction risk.
B) Single USD OUR with internal conversion (lower bank hops but exposes Globex to Redwood's FX spread).
C) SHA with agreed fee uplift on the USD amount to anticipate deducted correspondents (we can propose +0.25% or a flat $25 buffer).

Rafael — can you confirm exact account numbers and the usual intermediary fee estimate for CHIPS/SWIFT into our NYC desk?

Thanks,
Marissa

Redwood Inference

title: Split-invoice payment: EUR/USD routing, intermediary fees & VAT treatment
preview:
From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@globex.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@globex.com
Date: Mon, 9 Aug 2027 16:12:00 +0100
Subject: Split-invoice payment: routing question

Hi Jonas,

We received Invoice 12345 for Q3 usage. Our treasury prefers to pay part in EUR and part in USD to match our hedging positions. Can you confirm whether Redwood can accept a split wire and provide separate beneficiary instructions for EUR (SEPA) and USD (SWIFT/CHIPS)? Also, we need clarity on how intermediary bank fees are handled so we can book the payment gross vs net for VAT reclaim.

Quick answers we need:
- Separate bank details for EUR and USD?
- Who bears intermediary/correspondent bank charges by default (our side or Redwood)?
- Any reason we should route everything to a single nostro to avoid correspondence fees?

Thanks,
Elena
Finance Operations
Globex Corporation

---

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@globex.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, accounts-payable@globex.com
Date: Mon, 9 Aug 2027 16:32:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Split-invoice payment: routing question

Hi Elena — thanks for the note. Short answer: we can accept a split payment but there are trade-offs for VAT reporting and intermediary fees. Full details below and I attached a template with the recommended routing options (wire_instructions_template.xlsx).

Summary:
- We can accept two legs: EUR (SEPA to our EUR collection account) and USD (SWIFT to our USD correspondent).
- Default fees: our standard terms are "sender bears all charges" (OUR). If you choose BEN or SHA, intermediary/correspondent fees may be deducted in transit and will reduce the amount we receive.
- VAT: for EU VAT reclaim you need a full gross invoice amount reflected in your remittance. If fees are deducted by an intermediary (i.e., we receive less than invoice total), that can complicate reclaim depending on your local tax authority.

Recommendation:
1) If you want clean VAT treatment, set both wires to OUR so Redwood receives the full invoice amounts and there are no deductions for intermediary banks.
2) Alternatively, instruct SHA but add a small margin on the USD leg to cover expected correspondent fees — we can provide typical ranges for USD-SWIFT routing.
3) If you prefer a single nostro to avoid multiple correspondent legs, we can accept a single USD wire to our NYC correspondent and internally convert to EUR; note this may affect the FX rate and Redwood will pass through conversion at our published mid-market + spread.

I've looped in Marissa (Finance) and Rafael (Payments) for fee estimates and exact account numbers.

Best,
Jonas
Head of GTM Partnerships
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Redwood_Invoice_12345.pdf (PDF, 98KB)]
[Attachment: wire_instructions_template.xlsx (Excel, 12KB)]

---

source.subject: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW
messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: renewals@datadog.com, Evan Liu <evan.liu@datadog.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW

Hi Evan / Datadog renewals team,

We have our Datadog contract up for amendment focused on log retention and indexing (current term ends 2028-01-31). Usage has spiked and the renewal quote we received lists a ~38% uplift due to added ingestion and retention add-ons. Before we escalate to execs, can you provide: 

- A compact comparison of three alternatives: (1) 30-day hot retention with archive-to-s3 for 1 year, (2) 60-day hot retention with selective indexing, (3) current 90-day hot retention but with a committed usage discount. Include per-GB and monthly costs for each (with and without standard 12-mo commit).
- Any migration mechanics or SOW items we should expect (e.g., agent config changes, reindexing windows).
- If there are feature tradeoffs (saved queries, alerts, metric derivation) with selective indexing.

Cc'ing Rafael (sales ops), Laura (finance) and Dylan (infra) to keep stakeholders aligned. We're particularly sensitive to: reducing monthly unit cost while retaining ability to run long-tail log searches for incident debug and retaining audit logs for 1 year for compliance.

I've attached the renewal quote Datadog sent and our draft SOW template for retention work so you can mark where changes would land. Goal: get a decision path this week so procurement can issue PO and we can sign the amendment before EoQ.

Thanks,
Camila

--
Camila Reyes
Sr. Procurement Manager | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.com
(415) 555-0192

Attachments: Datadog_renewal_quote_July2027.pdf, SOW-logging-retention-amendment.docx
From: Evan Liu <evan.liu@datadog.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: renewals@datadog.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW

Hi Camila — thanks for the clear ask and the SOW. Quick summary below; attaching an annotated quote and a short SOW redline.

Option A — 30-day hot + 11-mo archive-to-S3:
- Hot retention: 30 days for indexed logs; archival storage in S3 for up to 12 months (restore via on-demand retrieval).
- Cost: $0.018/GB hot + $0.0025/GB/month archive (example blended monthly for your current 25TB/month: ~$6.75k hot + ~$1.56k archive = ~$8.3k/mo).
- Tradeoffs: longer restore latency for older logs; saved views remain but archived logs not searchable until restored.

Option B — 60-day hot with selective indexing:
- Hot retention: 60 days for fully indexed logs, plus selective indexing for noisy sources (sampled or non-indexed raw stored cheaper).
- Cost: $0.028/GB hot if fully indexed; selective indexing reduces effective bill by ~25-40% depending on sources. For your telemetry mix, we estimate ~$11k/mo without commit; with a 12-mo 15% commit discount estimate ~$9.35k/mo.

Option C — 90-day hot with committed capacity:
- Keep current experience; 12-mo commit pricing could bring the uplift down to ~+10% YoY vs quoted non-committed price.
- Cost: ~13% higher than your current invoice without commit; with commit approx +6% relative to current.

Other notes:
- One-time migration SOW: ~2.5 engineer-days for agent config and pipeline updates plus up to 8 hours for QA and runbook updates. Redline attached.
- We can provide a complimentary 30-day reduce-invoice credit if you sign the 12-mo commit this quarter (must be on docuSign by 31-Jul-2027).

I've attached: annotated_quote.pdf and SOW_redline.docx. Happy to hop on a 30-min call with Rafael/Dylan/Laura to walk through numbers.

Best,
Evan
Senior Renewals Manager — Datadog
evan.liu@datadog.com

Attachments: annotated_quote.pdf, SOW_redline.docx
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Evan Liu <evan.liu@datadog.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2027 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW

Thanks Evan — helpful level of detail. Internal quick reactions below (reply-all to keep alignment):

- Rafael: Option B looks promising from a product/eng perspective (keeps searchability for 60 days). Can you quantify which sources to selectively index? I think we can sample noisy infra logs (k8s kubelet, node-exporter) but keep app logs fully indexed.
- Laura: the 12-mo commit + 15% discount + your 30-day signing credit could be the least friction to procurement if the overall ARR delta is acceptable. Can you confirm if the commit requires an upfront payment or monthly amortization?
- Dylan: migration SOW of 2.5 eng-days looks reasonable; we may prefer to own the agent changes but want Datadog to support rollout verification and QA.

Action items: Evan, please confirm commit billing cadence and share the annotated quote as a named PDF (we referenced a generic PDF but need the formal quote for PO). Rafael, Laura and Dylan — if you can OK the direction today I’ll ask procurement to prepare the PO and route for signature. We should also keep the SOW with minor redlines and send to legal for a quick review.

Camila

On Mon, Jul 12, 2027 at 11:02 AM Evan Liu <evan.liu@datadog.com> wrote:
> Hi Camila — thanks for the clear ask and the SOW. Quick summary below; attaching an annotated quote and a short SOW redline.
> 
> [snip quoted options A/B/C]
> 
> Best,
> Evan
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Jul 2027 15:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Datadog log retention addendum — options, pricing, and SOW

All — quick procurement summary and next steps so we can close this week:

Decision: move forward with Option B (60-day hot + selective indexing) with a 12-month commit at the 15% discount, subject to confirmation from Evan on billing cadence (monthly amortization preferred).

Budget/PO: Laura — request a PO for $112,200 ARR (this reflects the committed price in Evan's estimate). Please route to AP once the DocuSign agreement is received.

SOW & implementation: Dylan, you'll own the agent config changes; Evan to provide the SOW redline and a 2-hr onboarding session. We keep 90-day archive for audit logs in S3 (in case legal requests).

Signatures: Camila to coordinate DocuSign; Evan indicated a 31-Jul signing window for the 30-day credit.

Rafael
Head of Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.com

> On Mon, Jul 12, 2027 at 13:05 Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Thanks Evan — helpful level of detail. Internal quick reactions below (reply-all to keep alignment):
> > [snip]

- Cost: $0.018/GB hot + $0.0025/GB/month archive (example blended monthly for your current 25TB/month: ~$6.75k hot + ~$1.56k archive = ~$8.3k/mo).
- Tradeoffs: longer restore latency for older logs; saved views remain but archived logs not searchable until restored.

Option B — 60-day hot with selective indexing:
- Hot retention: 60 days for fully indexed logs, plus selective indexing for noisy sources (sampled or non-indexed raw stored cheaper).
- Cost: $0.028/GB hot if fully indexed; selective indexing reduces effective bill by ~25-40% depending on sources. For your telemetry mix, we estimate ~$11k/mo without commit; with a 12-mo 15% commit discount estimate ~$9.35k/mo.

Option C — 90-day hot with committed capacity:
- Keep current experience; 12-mo commit pricing could bring the uplift down to ~+10% YoY vs quoted non-committed price.
- Cost: ~13% higher than your current invoice without commit; with commit approx +6% relative to current.

Other notes:
- One-time migration SOW: ~2.5 engineer-days for agent config and pipeline updates plus up to 8 hours for QA and runbook updates. Redline attached.
- We can provide a complimentary 30-day reduce-invoice credit if you sign the 12-mo commit this quarter (must be on docuSign by 31-Jul-2027).

I've attached: annotated_quote.pdf and SOW_redline.docx. Happy to hop on a 30-min call with Rafael/Dylan/Laura to walk through numbers.

Best,
Evan
Senior Renewals Manager — Datadog
evan.liu@datadog.com

Attachments: annotated_quote.pdf, SOW_redline.docx

source.subject: Invoice originator and delivery routing for Brightline AP onboarding
messages:
From: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Keller <tkeller@brightlinehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jul 2027 09:15:00 -0400
Subject: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the walkthrough last week. We're ready to lock down how Redwood will deliver invoices to Brightline so our AP team can process payments without delays. A couple of questions to confirm requirements before we onboard:

- Do you support sending invoices via a hosted portal (we can pull) or do you e-mail PDFs to our AP address? Which path is the recommended default?
- We have a PO policy: PO must appear on the invoice in 1 of 3 formats (BR-##### or BR-INV-##### or numeric only). Can you confirm where the PO should be placed (invoice header vs line-level)?
- For e-invoicing: do you support XML payloads/SFTP transfer? If so, can you share a sample schema we can validate against?
- Tax/VAT: we operate in multiple states; if we need to supply an EIN or VAT id, what evidence do you require and how is that stored?

I'll attach our typical invoice sample so you can see our PO layout. Please loop in whoever owns billing for Redwood and finance for VAT checks.

Thanks,
Sara Bradley
Director, Revenue Operations
Brightline Health

Attachment: brightline_invoice_sample.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jul 2027 11:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Sara — great to hear you're ready to proceed. Short answer: we can support both routes, with a few tradeoffs. Summary and recommended next steps below.

1) Delivery options (pros/cons)
- Portal (recommended for recurring, high-volume): we post invoices to a secure Brightline tenant you can pull or we can push via API. Pros: reliable metadata, less PDF parsing, supports XML/e-invoice. Cons: 1–2 business days to onboard tenant and map PO fields.
- Email PDF (fallback or low-volume): we can send a human-readable PDF to ap@brightlinehealth.com and cc any internal contacts. Pros: immediate, low lift. Cons: requires AP to manually match POs unless we also send structured metadata.

2) PO placement and required fields
- Preferred: top-level invoice fields. We'll accept PO in header or line-level if you include a canonical PO tag in the payload. For the portal/XML route, map to field: invoice.po_number. For PDF-only deliveries, include PO in the header and the first line of the PDF.

3) E-invoice options
- We support XML payloads over SFTP, as well as an HTTPS API to push invoices. Attached is our sample e-invoice schema (redwood_einvoice_schema.xml) and a quick portal onboarding checklist (portal_onboarding_instructions.pdf). If you want SFTP instead of portal, that is an option — provide host/key details and we'll coordinate with infra.

4) Tax / VAT evidence
- Looping in Neha (finance ops) for the exact evidence we need. In general we store EIN or VAT ids in our billing profile and attach submitted evidence to the account for audit.

Suggested next steps:
- You confirm preferred delivery: Portal (with email CC to ap@brightlinehealth.com) or SFTP + email copies.
- Brightline confirms PO format to map in our onboarding form (BR-##### vs BR-INV-#####).
- Brightline completes the onboarding form and uploads tax evidence (if applicable).

I'll leave Neha and Rafael to confirm the tax checklist and any contract amendment for e-invoicing if necessary.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Revenue
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_einvoice_schema.xml, portal_onboarding_instructions.pdf

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 10:30:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Sara — quick note from finance on the evidence and VAT handling.

- US customers: we require an EIN for tax recordkeeping if you're requesting any withholding exemptions. Please attach a copy of your W-9 or a scanned EIN letter (or provide the EIN and we will request formal documentation).
- EU customers / cross-border VAT: if you claim reverse charge, provide your VAT registration certificate (pdf). We will store this in our billing profile and reference it on invoices where applicable.

Checklist attached (tax_evidence_checklist.pdf). Once we have the docs we will:
- Mark your billing profile to show the preferred tax treatment (exempt/reverse charge/standard)
- Ensure the invoice has the required tax lines and references for your AP team

Notes on payment collection: our default payment terms for self-serve/portal customers are NET30. For contractually negotiated terms we record payment SLA in the billing profile. Rafael — is there any scenario where Brightline's PO policy requires a contract amendment before we enable e-invoicing?

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
Manager, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: tax_evidence_checklist.pdf

From: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 13:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Thanks Ava and Neha — Brightline prefers the portal as the main delivery channel, but we need a copy of the PDF emailed to ap@brightlinehealth.com for our internal scan/import. SFTP is acceptable if you prefer to push XML there; either way, please make sure the PDF copy contains the PO header in BR-INV-##### format.

We can provide a scanned W-9 (attached below) and will upload any VAT certs if needed. One more ask: can Redwood include a single-line summary field called "billing_reference" that our ERP uses for remittance matching?

Also, for legal: we typically require a one-paragraph amendment to the MSA allowing e-invoice delivery as formal delivery of invoices. If you could loop legal or send a draft amendment, we can sign quickly.

Best,
Sara

> On Mon, Jul 5, 2027 at 11:05 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Hi Sara — great to hear you're ready to proceed. Short answer: we can support both routes, with a few tradeoffs. Summary and recommended next steps below.

Attachment: brightline_w9_scanned.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>, Accounts Payable <ap@brightlinehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 13:45:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Thanks — this is helpful and straightforward. Confirming next steps and owners:

1) Delivery path: Portal will be enabled for Brightline tenant. We will also send an emailed PDF copy to ap@brightlinehealth.com on the same cadence as the portal posting. Rafael will coordinate the onboarding schedule with your infra lead and will collect SFTP details if you choose that option.

2) PO mapping: We'll map BR-INV-##### to invoice.po_number and include a top-level "billing_reference" field in the XML and portal metadata to match your ERP. Please confirm the exact sample value format when you complete the onboarding form.

3) Tax evidence: Neha will ingest your W-9 and mark the profile accordingly. If you later provide VAT certificates they will be attached to the profile for EU/cross-border treatments.

4) Legal amendment: Rafael will circulate a one-paragraph amendment to the MSA to explicitly allow e-invoice delivery as formal invoice delivery. Rafael — assigning to you to draft and send via DocuSign.

I'll kick off the portal tenant creation today and share the onboarding link. Rafael — attaching a draft amendment for editing.

Next actions:
- Brightline: finish onboarding form with sample PO value + upload any tax docs (Sara).
- Redwood (Rafael): send amendment-draft-v1.pdf to legal for DocuSign, schedule portal tenant setup, or request SFTP credentials if preferred.
- Redwood (Neha): mark billing profile on receipt of W-9.

Thanks all,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Revenue
Redwood Inference

Attachments: amendment-draft-v1.pdf
Related link to onboarding: https://redwoodinference.app/onboarding/brightline

text:
From: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Keller <tkeller@brightlinehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jul 2027 09:15:00 -0400
Subject: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the walkthrough last week. We're ready to lock down how Redwood will deliver invoices to Brightline so our AP team can process payments without delays. A couple of questions to confirm requirements before we onboard:

- Do you support sending invoices via a hosted portal (we can pull) or do you e-mail PDFs to our AP address? Which path is the recommended default?
- We have a PO policy: PO must appear on the invoice in 1 of 3 formats (BR-##### or BR-INV-##### or numeric only). Can you confirm where the PO should be placed (invoice header vs line-level)?
- For e-invoicing: do you support XML payloads/SFTP transfer? If so, can you share a sample schema we can validate against?
- Tax/VAT: we operate in multiple states; if we need to supply an EIN or VAT id, what evidence do you require and how is that stored?

I'll attach our typical invoice sample so you can see our PO layout. Please loop in whoever owns billing for Redwood and finance for VAT checks.

Thanks,
Sara Bradley
Director, Revenue Operations
Brightline Health

Attachment: brightline_invoice_sample.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Jul 2027 11:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Sara — great to hear you're ready to proceed. Short answer: we can support both routes, with a few tradeoffs. Summary and recommended next steps below.

1) Delivery options (pros/cons)
- Portal (recommended for recurring, high-volume): we post invoices to a secure Brightline tenant you can pull or we can push via API. Pros: reliable metadata, less PDF parsing, supports XML/e-invoice. Cons: 1–2 business days to onboard tenant and map PO fields.
- Email PDF (fallback or low-volume): we can send a human-readable PDF to ap@brightlinehealth.com and cc any internal contacts. Pros: immediate, low lift. Cons: requires AP to manually match POs unless we also send structured metadata.

2) PO placement and required fields
- Preferred: top-level invoice fields. We'll accept PO in header or line-level if you include a canonical PO tag in the payload. For the portal/XML route, map to field: invoice.po_number. For PDF-only deliveries, include PO in the header and the first line of the PDF.

3) E-invoice options
- We support XML payloads over SFTP, as well as an HTTPS API to push invoices. Attached is our sample e-invoice schema (redwood_einvoice_schema.xml) and a quick portal onboarding checklist (portal_onboarding_instructions.pdf). If you want SFTP instead of portal, that is an option — provide host/key details and we'll coordinate with infra.

4) Tax / VAT evidence
- Looping in Neha (finance ops) for the exact evidence we need. In general we store EIN or VAT ids in our billing profile and attach submitted evidence to the account for audit.

Suggested next steps:
- You confirm preferred delivery: Portal (with email CC to ap@brightlinehealth.com) or SFTP + email copies.
- Brightline confirms PO format to map in our onboarding form (BR-##### vs BR-INV-#####).
- Brightline completes the onboarding form and uploads tax evidence (if applicable).

I'll leave Neha and Rafael to confirm the tax checklist and any contract amendment for e-invoicing if necessary.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Revenue
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_einvoice_schema.xml, portal_onboarding_instructions.pdf

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 10:30:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Hi Sara — quick note from finance on the evidence and VAT handling.

- US customers: we require an EIN for tax recordkeeping if you're requesting any withholding exemptions. Please attach a copy of your W-9 or a scanned EIN letter (or provide the EIN and we will request formal documentation).
- EU customers / cross-border VAT: if you claim reverse charge, provide your VAT registration certificate (pdf). We will store this in our billing profile and reference it on invoices where applicable.

Checklist attached (tax_evidence_checklist.pdf). Once we have the docs we will:
- Mark your billing profile to show the preferred tax treatment (exempt/reverse charge/standard)
- Ensure the invoice has the required tax lines and references for your AP team

Notes on payment collection: our default payment terms for self-serve/portal customers are NET30. For contractually negotiated terms we record payment SLA in the billing profile. Rafael — is there any scenario where Brightline's PO policy requires a contract amendment before we enable e-invoicing?

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
Manager, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: tax_evidence_checklist.pdf

From: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 13:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Thanks Ava and Neha — Brightline prefers the portal as the main delivery channel, but we need a copy of the PDF emailed to ap@brightlinehealth.com for our internal scan/import. SFTP is acceptable if you prefer to push XML there; either way, please make sure the PDF copy contains the PO header in BR-INV-##### format.

We can provide a scanned W-9 (attached below) and will upload any VAT certs if needed. One more ask: can Redwood include a single-line summary field called "billing_reference" that our ERP uses for remittance matching?

Also, for legal: we typically require a one-paragraph amendment to the MSA allowing e-invoice delivery as formal delivery of invoices. If you could loop legal or send a draft amendment, we can sign quickly.

Best,
Sara

> On Mon, Jul 5, 2027 at 11:05 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Hi Sara — great to hear you're ready to proceed. Short answer: we can support both routes, with a few tradeoffs. Summary and recommended next steps below.

Attachment: brightline_w9_scanned.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Bradley <sara.bradley@brightlinehealth.com>, Accounts Payable <ap@brightlinehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Jul 2027 13:45:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice originator & delivery — Brightline AP onboarding

Thanks — this is helpful and straightforward. Confirming next steps and owners:

1) Delivery path: Portal will be enabled for Brightline tenant. We will also send an emailed PDF copy to ap@brightlinehealth.com on the same cadence as the portal posting. Rafael will coordinate the onboarding schedule with your infra lead and will collect SFTP details if you choose that option.

2) PO mapping: We'll map BR-INV-##### to invoice.po_number and include a top-level "billing_reference" field in the XML and portal metadata to match your ERP. Please confirm the exact sample value format when you complete the onboarding form.

3) Tax evidence: Neha will ingest your W-9 and mark the profile accordingly. If you later provide VAT certificates they will be attached to the profile for EU/cross-border treatments.

4) Legal amendment: Rafael will circulate a one-paragraph amendment to the MSA to explicitly allow e-invoice delivery as formal invoice delivery. Rafael — assigning to you to draft and send via DocuSign.

I'll kick off the portal tenant creation today and share the onboarding link. Rafael — attaching a draft amendment for editing.

Next actions:
- Brightline: finish onboarding form with sample PO value + upload any tax docs (Sara).
- Redwood (Rafael): send amendment-draft-v1.pdf to legal for DocuSign, schedule portal tenant setup, or request SFTP credentials if preferred.
- Redwood (Neha): mark billing profile on receipt of W-9.

Thanks all,
Ava Chen
Account Lead, Revenue
Redwood Inference

Attachments: amendment-draft-v1.pdf
Related link to onboarding: https://redwoodinference.app/onboarding/brightline

messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Katherine Morales <katherine.morales@novexhealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-25T14:06:00-07:00
Subject: Novex MAP: responsibilities, blockers, and revised timeline

Hi Katherine —

Great to meet earlier. Below is a concise recap from our discovery and an initial MAP draft to align on milestones and owners. I attached Novex_MAP_v1.xlsx for easy comments. Please confirm or flag any changes.

Key decisions from call:
- Target integration scope: embeddings + session-routing for secure patient messages
- Success metric: sub-200ms median generation for core flows; cost target under $0.02/1000 tokens avg over pilot
- Data requirements: kept in VPC; audit logs required for SOC2 evidence

Proposed MAP (high level):
1) Pilot setup (owner: Redwood infra) — Redwood to provision dedicated capacity and baseline perf tests — target date: 2027-07-15
2) Data ingestion & privacy review (owner: Novex data team) — schema mapping + sample dataset — target date: 2027-07-22
3) Compliance evidence handoff (owner: Evelyn Hart / Redwood) — SOC2 logs and retention policy — target date: 2027-07-29
4) UAT & perf tuning (owner: Novex dev leads + Redwood) — target date: 2027-08-05
5) Commercial sign-off (owner: Katherine Morales / Novex) — target date: 2027-08-12

Dependencies and blockers we flagged:
- SOC2 evidence (Evelyn to provide specifics and retention window)
- Procurement timeline — Katherine to confirm procurement contact and typical approval lead times

Immediate asks / next steps:
- Katherine: confirm owners and the procurement contact (copy to procurement if possible)
- Rafael: I’ll loop you in for capacity planning and a perf baseline next week
- I will send a revised MAP with updated dates based on your procurement cadence by Friday (6/30).

Attachments: Novex_MAP_v1.xlsx (draft)

Thanks — looking forward to aligning next steps.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0148
From: Katherine Morales <katherine.morales@novexhealth.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-25T16:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Novex MAP: responsibilities, blockers, and revised timeline

Camila —

Thanks for the clear recap and draft. That aligns with what we discussed. A couple quick notes:
- Procurement lead is Aaron Li (aaron.li@novexhealth.com); Im copying him on this chain.
- We typically run procurement windows mid-month, so the commercial sign-off may slide ~2 weeks. Can you reflect that in the MAP?
- SOC2 evidence: I will coordinate with our security team but expect questions on log retention and encryption-at-rest — Ill loop Evelyn on details.

Please go ahead and adjust the dates by two weeks for now; well confirm earlier if we can. Rafael, can you confirm Redwood's baseline capacity for the pilot?

Katherine
VP Product, Novex Health
katherine.morales@novexhealth.com
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Katherine Morales <katherine.morales@novexhealth.com>, Aaron Li <aaron.li@novexhealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-06-30T09:07:00-07:00
Subject: Updated MAP (v2) + SOW draft — milestone owners & weekly checkpoint cadence

Katherine / Aaron —

Per your note, attached is Novex_MAP_v2.xlsx reflecting the two-week shift in the commercial milestone and clarified owners. I also attached Novex_SOW_v0.3.pdf for initial procurement review. Highlights in v2:
- Pilot setup -> target 2027-07-29 (owner: Redwood infra / Rafael Mendes)
- Data ingestion -> target 2027-08-05 (owner: Novex data team)
- Compliance evidence handoff -> target 2027-08-12 (owner: Evelyn Hart / Redwood)
- UAT & perf tuning -> target 2027-08-19 (joint)
- Commercial sign-off -> target 2027-08-26

Action items (week 1 checkpoint):
- Rafael: provide perf baseline numbers and estimate of dedicated GPU hours required (due 2027-07-02)
- Katherine: confirm Novex dev lead for integration (due 2027-07-06)
- Aaron / Procurement: confirm any vendor approval forms needed and expected procurement lead time (due 2027-07-09)
- Evelyn: confirm SOC2 log retention window and point of contact for evidence (due 2027-07-09)

I propose a weekly 30-min MAP checkpoint on Fridays at 10am PT starting 2027-07-02 — unless you prefer a different cadence. Ive highlighted overdue/at-risk tasks in the MAP v2 sheet and added a comment column for each owner to update progress.

Attachments: Novex_MAP_v2.xlsx (updated MAP), Novex_SOW_v0.3.pdf (procurement draft)

Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0148

On 2027-06-25T14:06:00-07:00 Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Great to meet earlier. Below is a concise recap from our discovery and an initial MAP draft to align on milestones and owners. I attached Novex_MAP_v1.xlsx for easy comments. Please confirm or flag any changes.
> 
> [original recap omitted]
From: Aaron Li <aaron.li@novexhealth.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Katherine Morales <katherine.morales@novexhealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Novex Procurement <procurement@novexhealth.com>
Date: 2027-07-02T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Updated MAP (v2) + SOW draft — milestone owners & weekly checkpoint cadence

Camila / Katherine —

Thanks for the update and the SOW. Procurement has scheduled an internal intake on 2027-07-09 to review vendor docs. Two quick asks before that meeting:
1) Can you provide a simple cost breakdown by pilot month and an expected monthly committed capacity (GPU hours)?
2) Please share the SOW as a DocuSign link when youre ready (procurement requires an e-sign workflow).

We can join the weekly MAP check-ins; 10am PT Fridays works. Ive copied procurement and Rafael for capacity + cost figures.

Aaron Li
Contracts Manager, Novex Health
procurement@novexhealth.com

On 2027-06-30T09:07:00-07:00 Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:

source.subject: Deployment pathway scenarios & sizing proforma
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena@novapay.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap + proforma request (hosted vs dedicated vs private)

Hi Elena — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and next steps so we're aligned on sizing and deployment pathway options: 

1) What we heard from NovaPay:
- Primary use: tokenization + conversational support inside your merchant dashboard for fraud triage and dispute summaries.
- Peak TPS target: ~250 short-completion calls per second during batch settlement windows (peaks last ~10–15 min). Off-peak ~30 TPS.
- Token mix: average request ~220 input tokens, 140 output tokens; 95thpct requests up to 1,200 tokens (long receipts + context).
- Latency target: p95 < 350ms for short completions, p95 < 1.2s for long-context ops.
- Data residency: US-only; SOC2 evidence required.

2) Deployment pathways we discussed:
- Hosted API: fastest to production but shared pool; recommend only for non-sensitive early rollout.
- Dedicated capacity: reserved GPU pools (best predictability for your TPS bursts), supports KV/prefix caching to lower cost on repeated contexts.
- Private VPC/on-prem: for eventual regulated workflows; longer lead time and integration work.

3) Sizing asks we need from you to produce a proforma (we added a basic worksheet attached):
- Clarify percent of calls that are short (<400 tokens) vs long (>400 tokens) during peak.
- Any expected concurrency from your frontend (concurrent open sessions interacting with chat/generate API)?
- Confirmation if 99% of requests will be within US region.

Next steps: if you can confirm the short/long mix and typical concurrency by Friday, we'll return a dedicated-capacity proforma (GPU mix options + cost/latency tradeoffs) and a hosted fall-back plan.

Attachments: I attached the blank proforma and our SOC2 summary for procurement.

Cheers,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

--
Ava Chen
Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
(415) 555-0192
From: Elena Park <elena@novapay.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T10:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proforma request (hosted vs dedicated vs private)

Hi Ava — thanks for the tidy recap. Confirming the numbers from our end: 

- Peak TPS window: 250 TPS (as discussed). We expect this to be concentrated in three 12–15 minute windows per day.
- Token mix estimate (rough): ~65% short (<=400 tokens), ~30% medium (401–800), ~5% long (>800).
- Average concurrency: frontend keeps ~1.8k concurrent open sessions during peak; estimate ~15% of those actively sending requests within any 30s window. That yields roughly 270 concurrent active calls during peaks (customer-side estimate).
- Residency: US-only. SOC2 path okay; will request SOC2 + encryption attestation from procurement.

I've uploaded our short transcript logs to the Drive folder you shared; feel free to use those for more accurate token profiling.

Elena
Director, Engineering Ops, NovaPay
ops@novapay.com
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: sizing numbers from NovaPay — asking for GPU recommendations and proforma inputs

Rafael / Marcus — forwarding Elena's confirmation above. Key constraints: 250 TPS peak, token mix ~65/30/5, ~270 concurrent active calls in peak window, p95 latency goals. Can you propose: 

- 2–3 dedicated GPU SKU options (e.g., H100-heavy vs A100 mixed vs L4 for short inference) with expected token/s GPU TPS and expected p95 latency for short vs long requests. Please include KV cache sizing assumptions and whether multi-node sharding or model variants (quantized) are recommended.
- Recommended batching/concurrency (tokens-in-flight) defaults we would set in the proforma to meet p95 SLA targets while minimizing cost.
- Any redlines on feasibility for 1.2s for long-context ops at 250 TPS peak.

I'll combine your inputs into the attached proforma and send back to Elena with cost/latency tradeoffs and timeline for Dedicated vs Hosted pilot.

Thanks,
Ava
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T13:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: sizing numbers from NovaPay — GPU recommendations

Quick pass from infra: high level guidance below; we can turn these into the proforma numbers once Marcus confirms cost model tweaks.

Option A — H100-focused (lowest latency for long context):
- Single H100 (80GB) perf note: ~8–11 short-completion TPS at 1/seq=140/resp=140 with aggressive kernel selection; with batching/prefetch we can push effective short-completion throughput closer to 18–20 TPS per GPU for highly quantized 8-bit models.
- For NovaPay peak (250 TPS short-heavy), estimate 12–16 H100s if we run mixed batching and reserve headroom for KV cache hot keys. Long requests (1,200 tokens) gravitate to 2–3x higher GPU time; plan for additional concurrency headroom (estimate +30% GPUs to keep p95 under 1.2s).
- KV/cache: dedicating ~200GB per pool for prefix/KV cache recommended (fast SSD-backed local cache across nodes).

Option B — A100-mix (cost / latency balance):
- A100 40GB: ~6–9 effective TPS for medium short requests, but denser packing and cheaper per-hour. For your mix, ~20 A100s gives predictable behavior and lower $/token than H100 option but slightly higher p95 on long runs.

Option C — Hybrid with L4 for short completions + H100 for long context:
- Offload >60% of short requests to L4 instances (cheaper), and reserve H100s for long-context and fallback. This reduces cost significantly but adds routing complexity and model-variant validation.

Batching/course defaults: 12–20ms target batching window for short completions; limit dynamic-batch size to keep p95 < 350ms. Recommend adaptive batching that tightens when queue latency approaches threshold.

Feasibility note: hitting 1.2s p95 for 1,200-token requests at 250 TPS is possible but expensive if all requests are long — our sizing assumes only ~5% long requests as you reported. If long-request % increases, GPU counts scale superlinearly.

I'll prepare precise GPU counts and cost delta for A/B variants (H100-only, A100 mix, hybrid) and drop into the proforma.

- Rafael
Senior Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T15:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: sizing numbers from NovaPay — GPU recommendations

Adding pricing and procurement timeline notes for the proforma: 

- Ballpark monthly committed pricing (pre-negotiation): H100 cluster (16 nodes) estimated $XX,XXX/mo; A100 cluster (20 nodes) estimated $YY,YYY/mo; Hybrid (L4 + H100 reserve) ~midpoint. I'll populate exact $/token and committed vs on-demand deltas in the spreadsheet.
- Lead times: Dedicated pool provisioning (US region) ~4–6 weeks once contract signed (shorter if we can use reserved infra we have queued). Private VPC onboarding ~8–12 weeks (adds network review + KMS integration).
- Procurement ask: include SOC2 docs and an initial PO estimate to unblock infra procurement.

I'll update nova_capacity_proforma.xlsx with node counts, expected headroom, and per-node throughput assumptions and send a versioned copy back to you. Ava — once you get Rafael's final numbers, please attach the proforma and a DoR checklist for NovaPay procurement; we should ask for a soft commit on timeline in the reply.

Marcus
Sales Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference

- Any redlines on feasibility for 1.2s for long-context ops at 250 TPS peak.

I'll combine your inputs into the attached proforma and send back to Elena with cost/latency tradeoffs and timeline for Dedicated vs Hosted pilot.

Thanks,
Ava


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T13:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: sizing numbers from NovaPay — GPU recommendations

Quick pass from infra: high level guidance below; we can turn these into the proforma numbers once Marcus confirms cost model tweaks.

Option A — H100-focused (lowest latency for long context):
- Single H100 (80GB) perf note: ~8–11 short-completion TPS at 1/seq=140/resp=140 with aggressive kernel selection; with batching/prefetch we can push effective short-completion throughput closer to 18–20 TPS per GPU for highly quantized 8-bit models.
- For NovaPay peak (250 TPS short-heavy), estimate 12–16 H100s if we run mixed batching and reserve headroom for KV cache hot keys. Long requests (1,200 tokens) gravitate to 2–3x higher GPU time; plan for additional concurrency headroom (estimate +30% GPUs to keep p95 under 1.2s).
- KV/cache: dedicating ~200GB per pool for prefix/KV cache recommended (fast SSD-backed local cache across nodes).

Option B — A100-mix (cost / latency balance):
- A100 40GB: ~6–9 effective TPS for medium short requests, but denser packing and cheaper per-hour. For your mix, ~20 A100s gives predictable behavior and lower $/token than H100 option but slightly higher p95 on long runs.

Option C — Hybrid with L4 for short completions + H100 for long context:
- Offload >60% of short requests to L4 instances (cheaper), and reserve H100s for long-context and fallback. This reduces cost significantly but adds routing complexity and model-variant validation.

Batching/course defaults: 12–20ms target batching window for short completions; limit dynamic-batch size to keep p95 < 350ms. Recommend adaptive batching that tightens when queue latency approaches threshold.

Feasibility note: hitting 1.2s p95 for 1,200-token requests at 250 TPS is possible but expensive if all requests are long — our sizing assumes only ~5% long requests as you reported. If long-request % increases, GPU counts scale superlinearly.

I'll prepare precise GPU counts and cost delta for A/B variants (H100-only, A100 mix, hybrid) and drop into the proforma.

- Rafael
Senior Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T15:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: sizing numbers from NovaPay — GPU recommendations

Adding pricing and procurement timeline notes for the proforma: 

- Ballpark monthly committed pricing (pre-negotiation): H100 cluster (16 nodes) estimated $XX,XXX/mo; A100 cluster (20 nodes) estimated $YY,YYY/mo; Hybrid (L4 + H100 reserve) ~midpoint. I'll populate exact $/token and committed vs on-demand deltas in the spreadsheet.
- Lead times: Dedicated pool provisioning (US region) ~4–6 weeks once contract signed (shorter if we can use reserved infra we have queued). Private VPC onboarding ~8–12 weeks (adds network review + KMS integration).
- Procurement ask: include SOC2 docs and an initial PO estimate to unblock infra procurement.

I'll update nova_capacity_proforma.xlsx with node counts, expected headroom, and per-node throughput assumptions and send a versioned copy back to you. Ava — once you get Rafael's final numbers, please attach the proforma and a DoR checklist for NovaPay procurement; we should ask for a soft commit on timeline in the reply.

Marcus
Sales Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference
text:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena@novapay.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap + proforma request (hosted vs dedicated vs private)

Hi Elena — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and next steps so we're aligned on sizing and deployment pathway options: 

1) What we heard from NovaPay:
- Primary use: tokenization + conversational support inside your merchant dashboard for fraud triage and dispute summaries.
- Peak TPS target: ~250 short-completion calls per second during batch settlement windows (peaks last ~10–15 min). Off-peak ~30 TPS.
- Token mix: average request ~220 input tokens, 140 output tokens; 95thpct requests up to 1,200 tokens (long receipts + context).
- Latency target: p95 < 350ms for short completions, p95 < 1.2s for long-context ops.
- Data residency: US-only; SOC2 evidence required.

2) Deployment pathways we discussed:
- Hosted API: fastest to production but shared pool; recommend only for non-sensitive early rollout.
- Dedicated capacity: reserved GPU pools (best predictability for your TPS bursts), supports KV/prefix caching to lower cost on repeated contexts.
- Private VPC/on-prem: for eventual regulated workflows; longer lead time and integration work.

3) Sizing asks we need from you to produce a proforma (we added a basic worksheet attached):
- Clarify percent of calls that are short (<400 tokens) vs long (>400 tokens) during peak.
- Any expected concurrency from your frontend (concurrent open sessions interacting with chat/generate API)?
- Confirmation if 99% of requests will be within US region.

Next steps: if you can confirm the short/long mix and typical concurrency by Friday, we'll return a dedicated-capacity proforma (GPU mix options + cost/latency tradeoffs) and a hosted fall-back plan.

Attachments: I attached the blank proforma and our SOC2 summary for procurement.

Cheers,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

--
Ava Chen
Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
(415) 555-0192


From: Elena Park <elena@novapay.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-06-10T10:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proforma request (hosted vs dedicated vs private)

Hi Ava — thanks for the tidy recap. Confirming the numbers from our end: 

- Peak TPS window: 250 TPS (as discussed). We expect this to be concentrated in three 12–15 minute windows per day.
- Token mix estimate (rough): ~65% short (<=400 tokens), ~30% medium (401–800), ~5% long (>800).
- Average concurrency: frontend keeps ~1.8k concurrent open sessions during peak; estimate ~15% of those actively sending requests within any 30s window. That yields roughly 270 concurrent active calls during peaks (customer-side estimate).
- Residency: US-only. SOC2 path okay; will request SOC2 + encryption attestation from procurement.

I've uploaded our short transcript logs to the Drive folder you shared; feel free to use those for more accurate token profiling.

Elena
Director, Engineering Ops, NovaPay
ops@novapay.com

- Recommended batching/concurrency (tokens-in-flight) defaults we would set in the proforma to meet p95 SLA targets while minimizing cost.
- Any redlines on feasibility for 1.2s for long-context ops at 250 TPS peak.

I'll combine your inputs into the attached proforma and send back to Elena with cost/latency tradeoffs and timeline for Dedicated vs Hosted pilot.

Thanks,
Ava
