messages:
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khan <samir.khan@acme-retail.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Papertrail — approvals, signature timing, and start date for Acme MAP

Hi Samir —

Thanks again for the productive eval cycle. Per our MAP and the evaluation notes attached, we have a narrow window to complete approvals and get signatures in place to meet the proposed July 1 onboarding start. Below is the recommended paper process and ask from Acme:

- Approvals: Legal review complete (Acme legal), commercial approval (Samir), and internal finance sign-off for PO.
- Signature timing: we need signatures on the SOW and Master Services Agreement (MSA) by June 18 to hit a July 1 start (allowing two weeks for setup + onboarding prep).
- Start date: Target commencement: 2027-07-01. If signatures slip beyond June 25, we should plan a phased ramp (reduced throughput first week).

Attachments included: MAP_Acme_v3.pdf (final MAP), SOW_Acme_v1_redlines.docx (latest redlines), signature-matrix.csv (recommended signer windows). I've updated the signature windows to accommodate Acme's CFO availability (see matrix).

Action items for this thread:
1) Samir — confirm whether Acme Legal can sign by June 18 or if you need additional time.
2) Laura / Vivek — confirm Redwood's final approval & signer availability. Rafael — confirm invoicing cadence once DocuSign completes.
3) If we get green by June 10, I'll push DocuSign envelopes same day and schedule the kickoff for July 1.

DocuSign prep link (draft): https://docusign.example.com/prepare/ACME-4701
HubSpot record: https://hubspot.example.com/deal/ACME-4701

Thanks — trying to keep this tight so procurement and onboarding teams can plan.

— Jasmine Liu
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
jasmine.liu@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0123

Attachments: MAP_Acme_v3.pdf, SOW_Acme_v1_redlines.docx, signature-matrix.csv
From: Samir Khan <samir.khan@acme-retail.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Legal <legal@acme-retail.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Jun 2027 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Papertrail — approvals, signature timing, and start date for Acme MAP

Jasmine — thanks for the clear plan and the docs. Quick update from our side:

- Legal has the redlines and expects to finish the review by June 9; they flagged two minor items on data retention and audit log export that we asked Redwood to confirm in writing.
- Procurement needs a final SOW with the pricing table locked by June 14 to issue the PO. Can Redwood confirm the pricing freeze?
- On start date: July 1 works, but our internal ops team needs clarity on the first-week throughput and a single point of contact for cutover.

If legal clears the items and procurement can get the PO to you by June 18, we should be OK. I'm looping Cynthia from Ops (cynthia.ops@acme-retail.com) to coordinate the kickoff logistics — she may request a brief UAT window before full traffic.

Samir
Director, Platform Partnerships
Acme Retail
samir.khan@acme-retail.com
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>, Samir Khan <samir.khan@acme-retail.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Jun 2027 10:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Papertrail — approvals, signature timing, and start date for Acme MAP

Looping in final approvals from my side — short answers:

- Redwood confirms pricing freeze for the SOW as attached (SOW_Acme_v1_redlines.docx). No additional commercial changes expected if Acme accepts current redlines.
- Legal flag on retention: Redwood will add a short addendum confirming 90-day export availability and retention default; I'll drop a one-paragraph addendum into the DocuSign packet today.
- Signature windows: per signature-matrix.csv, our authorized signers (VP Sales and General Counsel) are available June 15–22 in the morning PT. Rafael will handle invoicing: 30 days net from signature date unless Acme requests different terms in writing.

Rafael — please confirm invoice recipient and whether we should send an initial pro forma to speed PO processing.

Samir — if you can get procurement to commit to a June 18 PO, we will move DocuSign the same day. If legal needs an extra day for the addendum, we can still land signatures by June 20.

Laura Bennett
Head of Commercial Operations, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com

> Hi Samir —
> 
> Thanks again for the productive eval cycle. Per our MAP and the evaluation notes attached...

To: Samir Khan <samir.khan@acme-retail.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>

Date: Fri, 4 Jun 2027 16:23:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Papertrail — approvals, signature timing, and start date for Acme MAP

Thanks all — quick final note and confirm next steps:

1) Go/no-go meeting: I'll run a 20-minute sync on Monday, June 14 at 09:00 PT to confirm final signers, any outstanding redlines, and DocuSign readiness. Invite sent separately.
2) DocuSign: Laura will add the 1-paragraph retention addendum today; once that's in, I'll prepare envelopes and send to Samir + Acme Legal. Target send date: June 14 (immediately after the go/no-go).
3) Signature windows & start date: per Laura, our signers are available June 15–22 mornings PT. If signatures land by June 18 and PO is issued the same day, onboarding start remains July 1 with a standard ramp (week 1: limited throughput + dedicated onboarding session).
4) Rafael — please create pro forma invoice and cc procurement@acme-retail.com. Rafael, confirm when done so we can attach to the DocuSign packet.

Samir — appreciate if you can confirm by end of day Friday (June 11) whether procurement will commit to the PO timeline. If anything shifts, call out preferred fallback start date (July 8 recommended).

Forwarding the latest attachments again: MAP_Acme_v3.pdf (final MAP), SOW_Acme_v1_redlines.docx (with Laura's forthcoming addendum), signature-matrix.csv.

Best —

If legal clears the items and procurement can get the PO to you by June 18, we should be OK. I'm looping Cynthia from Ops (cynthia.ops@acme-retail.com) to coordinate the kickoff logistics — she may request a brief UAT window before full traffic.

Samir

Acme Retail
samir.khan@acme-retail.com


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>, Samir Khan <samir.khan@acme-retail.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 3 Jun 2027 10:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Papertrail — approvals, signature timing, and start date for Acme MAP

Looping in final approvals from my side — short answers:

- Redwood confirms pricing freeze for the SOW as attached (SOW_Acme_v1_redlines.docx). No additional commercial changes expected if Acme accepts current redlines.
- Legal flag on retention: Redwood will add a short addendum confirming 90-day export availability and retention default; I'll drop a one-paragraph addendum into the DocuSign packet today.
- Signature windows: per signature-matrix.csv, our authorized signers (VP Sales and General Counsel) are available June 15–22 in the morning PT. Rafael will handle invoicing: 30 days net from signature date unless Acme requests different terms in writing.

Rafael — please confirm invoice recipient and whether we should send an initial pro forma to speed PO processing.

Samir — if you can get procurement to commit to a June 18 PO, we will move DocuSign the same day. If legal needs an extra day for the addendum, we can still land signatures by June 20.

Laura Bennett
Head of Commercial Operations, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com

2) DocuSign: Laura will add the 1-paragraph retention addendum today; once that's in, I'll prepare envelopes and send to Samir + Acme Legal. Target send date: June 14 (immediately after the go/no-go).
3) Signature windows & start date: per Laura, our signers are available June 15–22 mornings PT. If signatures land by June 18 and PO is issued the same day, onboarding start remains July 1 with a standard ramp (week 1: limited throughput + dedicated onboarding session).
4) Rafael — please create pro forma invoice and cc procurement@acme-retail.com. Rafael, confirm when done so we can attach to the DocuSign packet.

Samir — appreciate if you can confirm by end of day Friday (June 11) whether procurement will commit to the PO timeline. If anything shifts, call out preferred fallback start date (July 8 recommended).

Forwarding the latest attachments again: MAP_Acme_v3.pdf (final MAP), SOW_Acme_v1_redlines.docx (with Laura's forthcoming addendum), signature-matrix.csv.

messages:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Emily Stone <emily@acme.io>, procurement@acme.io
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 24 May 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal proposal: scale to 16-GPU reserved pool + throughput & SLA guardrails

Hi Emily / Acme procurement team,

Hope you had a good weekend — putting this thread together to formalize the renewal conversation we started last quarter. Based on usage trends and your roadmap to ship higher-throughput inference, we recommend moving from your current 8-GPU reserved pool to a 16-GPU reserved pool for the upcoming 12-month renewal. Highlights in the attached SOW and pricing sheet: 

- Monthly commit: 16 GPUs (reserved) with optional burst credits for spikes
- Throughput target: configuration guidance for sustained throughput; we’ll size burst pool for short peaks
- Canary: 30-day phased validation window post-deployment with roll-back option
- SLA: updated p99 latency target and credit schedule (draft below)

Attachments: Acme_Renewal_SOW_v2.pdf; capacity_pricing_sheet.xlsx (pricing table + example amortized unit costs)

Next steps I propose:
1) Confirm target monthly commit and any preferred billing cadence.
2) Share expected average seq-length and QPS profile so Rafael can validate the throughput / latency SLOs.
3) If comfortable, we’ll issue a revised SOW (v3) and a DocuSign link for signature.

If helpful, I can schedule a 30-minute call with Rafael and Laura this week to review technical constraints and timeline.

Thanks,
Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0127

---
Attachment stubs: Acme_Renewal_SOW_v2.pdf (pdf, 320KB); capacity_pricing_sheet.xlsx (xlsx, 48KB)

From: Emily Stone <emily@acme.io>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@acme.io
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal proposal: scale to 16-GPU reserved pool + throughput & SLA guardrails

Karthik,

Thanks for the proposal and the docs — appreciate the clarity. A few immediate questions from procurement and engineering: 

- Monthly commit: can we do a phased increase (8->12->16) over the first 60 days instead of a hard 16 commit up front?
- Canary / validation: we need a clear acceptance test and rollback criteria (we typically require a two-week canary with traffic mirroring before full cutover).
- SLA credits: please confirm how credits are calculated and whether we can get a cap on exposure.

I've attached our standard procurement checklist for renewals so you see the items we will push on legal/compliance.

Emily Stone
Head of Procurement, Acme Robotics
emily@acme.io

---
Attachment stub: Acme_internal_requirements.docx (docx, 72KB)

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Renewal proposal: scale to 16-GPU reserved pool + throughput & SLA guardrails

Quick internal sync — forwarding Emily’s reply. Key asks we should address in the counterproposal:

- Phased increase option (8 -> 12 -> 16) with clear milestones and a price schedule for each step.
- Canary: they want traffic mirroring + two-week canary; we previously offered 30-day, we can propose 30-day with an optional two-week mirrored validation followed by full cutover.
- SLA credit cap: recommend we offer a sliding credit (5% for single incident up to max 30% per quarter).

Rafael — can you confirm technical feasibility and lead time on provisioning additional reserved GPUs? Also any constraints around p99 latency claims if their avg sequence length jumps?

Laura Bennett
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
laura@redwood.com

---
(Internal only)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 26 May 2027 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Renewal proposal: scale to 16-GPU reserved pool + throughput & SLA guardrails

Laura / Karthik,

Confirmed — we can support a phased ramp (8 -> 12 -> 16) but with the following engineering caveats: 

- Lead time: 48–72 hours to allocate the additional reserved instances once the purchase order hits our provisioning queue (longer if a specific region/hardware SKU is requested).
- Canary approach: we recommend a two-stage validation — 14-day mirrored traffic window (no cutover) followed by a 30-day production validation after cutover.
- Throughput assumptions: for the models you’re running, budget ~180–220 tokens/sec per GPU for sequence lengths <=512 (p99 latency target at that density is achievable). If average seq length >512 the effective throughput drops ~30% (we need to size accordingly).
- Fallback: we can hold a 4-GPU warm standby (no charge for first month) to minimize risk of immediate capacity shortfall during cutover.

I’ve attached a short test-run summary from our bench (throughput_test_results.txt) and flagged areas legal / product should note for the SLO language.

Rafael Mendes
Solutions Engineering Lead | Redwood Inference
rafael@redwood.com

---
Attachment stub: throughput_test_results.txt (txt, 4KB)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Emily Stone <emily@acme.io>, procurement@acme.io
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 27 May 2027 09:00:00 -0700
Subject: Counterproposal: 16-GPU reserved pool — phased ramp + canary & SLA (ACME-2027-Q2-DED-108)

Emily / Acme team,

Thanks for the prompt feedback — based on Rafael’s notes we’ve put together a counterproposal that addresses the phased ramp and validation requirements: 

Proposal summary (high level):
- Term: 12 months starting on activation
- Billing: monthly with invoice net-30
- Commit: 16 GPUs reserved; phased billing schedule available: 8 GPUs (month 1), 12 GPUs (month 2), 16 GPUs (month 3 onward) if you choose the ramp option.
- Canary: 14-day mirrored traffic window (no production cutover) followed by a 30-day validation after production activation. Either party may trigger rollback during the mirrored window.
- SLA: p99 latency target tailored to avg seq length <=512; credit schedule: 5% credit for a single missed event, escalating up to 30% per quarter cap.
- Add-ons: 4-GPU warm-standby for first 30 days at no charge (to reduce cutover risk).

Pricing (summary):
- Effective monthly commit price (16 GPUs, amortized) — see attached ACME_SOW_v3.pdf for full line items and DocuSign link.

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/signer?transaction=0A1B2C3D

If that structure looks acceptable, we’ll issue the final SOW and get the DocuSign routed to your legal / procurement. If you prefer the hard commit to 16 immediately we can also update for that cadence (price delta shown in the attached SOW).

Would you like a short call tomorrow to walk the SOW and the mirrored validation plan through with Rafael?

Best,
Karthik
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

---
Attachment stub: ACME_SOW_v3.pdf (pdf, 410KB)

From: Emily Stone <emily@acme.io>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, procurement@acme.io
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 May 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Counterproposal: 16-GPU reserved pool — phased ramp + canary & SLA (ACME-2027-Q2-DED-108)

Karthik, Laura, Rafael,

Appreciate the fast turnaround and the detailed counterproposal. We’re aligned with the phased ramp and mirrored validation approach you outlined. Two final asks before we sign: 

1) A two-month price lock on the committed rate for the first six months (to ease our budgeting during ramp).
2) A short data residency clause in the SOW noting that inference logs and any telemetry for Acme customers will reside in the US region unless otherwise requested and documented.

If you can add those two items into the final SOW (ACME_SOW_v3.pdf) we can route the DocuSign through legal and sign by EOD Wednesday.

Emily Stone
Head of Procurement, Acme Robotics
emily@acme.io

---

1) A two-month price lock on the committed rate for the first six months (to ease our budgeting during ramp).
2) A short data residency clause in the SOW noting that inference logs and any telemetry for Acme customers will reside in the US region unless otherwise requested and documented.

If you can add those two items into the final SOW (ACME_SOW_v3.pdf) we can route the DocuSign through legal and sign by EOD Wednesday.

Emily Stone
Head of Procurement, Acme Robotics
emily@acme.io

---
Procurement: procurement@acme.io

source.subject: Post-call: concurrency & streaming SLOs for Clearwave POC
messages:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Li <sara.l@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: concurrency & streaming SLOs for Clearwave POC

Hi Sara — thanks again for the time earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our discovery on Clearwave’s POC requirements: 

- Goal: real-time insights pipeline integrating Redwood streaming generation for anomaly explanations.
- Traffic profile asked for: primarily bursty daytime traffic with predictable nightly batch enrichment.
- Key SLO asks from your side: 95th pct latency target (chat-style responses), parallelism for concurrent user sessions, and ability to stream partial results for long analyses.

Can you confirm the following so we can nail down quota options for the POC?
1) Typical concurrent active sessions during peak (approximate).
2) Average tokens per response and max tokens for long analyses.
3) Expected RPS (requests/sec) during peak bursts and sustained baseline.

Tentative Redwood starting point (we will formalize after your numbers):
- Provisionally propose 80 concurrent requests (per model endpoint) with connection pool support, burst capacity to 250 concurrent requests for up to 60s, and a 600 RPS soft limit.
- Streaming: support chunked responses with 200ms max inter-chunk latency and up to 30s streaming windows for long analysis.

If that matches your expectations, Rafael will prepare a quick worksheet with concrete numbers and a short quote. Could you send the traffic sample you mentioned?

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

--
Attachment: redwood_conn_workbook_v1.xlsx (will attach after internal sizing)

From: Sara Li <sara.l@clearwave.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 16:10:14 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call: concurrency & streaming SLOs for Clearwave POC

Hi Neha — thanks for the clear notes. A few clarifications and the sample attached.

- Peak concurrent interactive analysts: ~45 concurrent users (each may maintain a session that issues 1–3 in-flight requests).
- Burst behavior: scheduled batch jobs can kick off parallel runs; bursts up to ~220 concurrent requests for short windows (30–120s).
- Typical response size: 150 tokens average, 900 tokens max for full analysis.
- Baseline sustained RPS: ~180 RPS during daytime, with spikes to ~700 RPS during heavy repro runs.

Attaching sample traffic file: clearwave_traffic_sample.json (contains 72-hour synthetic distribution).

Also: procurement asks for a 95th percentile latency target at or below 1s for interactive flows. Can you confirm whether your provisional burst and connection-pooling plan would support that?

Thanks — Sara
Sara Li
Head of Integrations, Clearwave Analytics
sara.l@clearwave.ai
Attachment: clearwave_traffic_sample.json (json)

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 26 May 2027 09:23:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call: concurrency & streaming SLOs for Clearwave POC (internal sizing)

Neha / Jonas — I sketched a sizing worksheet and a few technical options after looking at the sample traffic. Attaching workbook. Key points: 

- Concurrency model: recommend hybrid pooling — persistent connections with multiplexing (HTTP/2) to reduce TCP churn + per-connection request queueing. This gives predictable latency under burst.
- Endpoint sizing (provisional): to hit 95th <= 1s for 150-token responses, we need roughly 6 g4dn-equivalent replicas on our standard quantized model (assumes batching window 10–20ms, KV cache hit rate 65%). That supports sustained ~600 RPS and burst headroom to ~1200 RPS across the cluster.
- Concurrency quotas: suggest tiered quota for the POC — base 80 concurrent, bump to 250 concurrent during scheduled bursts; we can enable short burst tokens for sudden spikes (60–120s windows).
- Streaming: 200ms inter-chunk latency target is reasonable; increase keepalive window for connections to 90s to avoid rehandshake during long analyses.

Open questions for product/legal when we formalize:
- Are Clearwave engineers comfortable with short auto-scaling cool-downs (30–60s) for the POC?
- Any required extra auditability for streaming (chunk IDs, sequence numbers) to aid replay?

I attached redwood_conn_workbook_v1.xlsx with per-scenario numbers.

Rafael
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
--
Attachment: redwood_conn_workbook_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Li <sara.l@clearwave.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 26 May 2027 10:00:45 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call: concurrency & streaming SLOs for Clearwave POC

Sara — thanks for the sample and the latency target. Based on Rafael’s sizing here’s a concise POC quota proposal for you to review: 

POC quota (30-day POC):
- Base concurrency: 80 parallel requests (per endpoint)
- Burst concurrency: up to 250 parallel requests for up to 90s windows (manual or scheduled)
- Sustained baseline throughput: 600 RPS cap across provisioned replicas
- Burst RPS limit: 1200 RPS soft cap with automatic throttling fallback
- Streaming: 200ms inter-chunk latency target; max streaming window 30s; keepalive 90s
- SLA ask: Redwood will aim for 95th percentile <= 1s for interactive flows under provisioned capacity; formal SLA to be included in quote.

If Clearwave is comfortable with these starting numbers, Rafael will produce a short technical appendix and I’ll loop in procurement for a formal quote. Action items: 
- Sara: confirm peak concurrent user estimate (you gave ~45 users; want to confirm that each truly maps to 1–3 in-flight requests).
- Sara: confirm whether scheduled batch runs should be treated as separate burst windows or folded into daily baseline.

I’ve also added a low-level change request to allow temporary burst capacity toggles from the console — Jonas, Rafael, please watch for the ticket.

Cheers,
Neha
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
--
On Wed, May 26, 2027 at 9:23 AM Rafael Mendes <rafael.m@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Neha / Jonas — I sketched a sizing worksheet and a few technical options after looking at the sample traffic. Attaching workbook. Key points: 
> [quoted summary omitted]

From: Sara Li <sara.l@clearwave.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 27 May 2027 08:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call: concurrency & streaming SLOs for Clearwave POC

Neha — that sounds reasonable and in line with our expectations. A few procurement notes before Rafael finalizes numbers: 

- Procurement will want a line-item for concurrency (base + burst) and a clear definition of burst windows and automatic throttling behavior.
- The 95th percentile <= 1s requirement is firm for interactive flows. If you can include a test plan for how you validate the 95th under the POC we can sign off faster.
- For long analyses (900 token responses), we accept streaming with chunked partial results — procurement wants an upper bound on the number of chunks per response and the expected inter-chunk latency.

Please go ahead and send the formal quote + SOW. We can accelerate procurement if the worksheet shows clear run-rate cost and a short test plan.

Thanks,
Sara
Head of Integrations, Clearwave Analytics

On Tue, May 25, 2027 at 4:10 PM Sara Li <sara.l@clearwave.ai> wrote:
> Hi Neha — thanks for the clear notes. A few clarifications and the sample attached.

Date: Thu, 27 May 2027 09:41:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call: concurrency & streaming SLOs for Clearwave POC (next steps)

All — thanks for the quick confirmation, Sara. Rafael/Jonas, per procurement asks please: 

1) Rafael — add a short test plan into the workbook showing how we will validate 95th pct latency <= 1s for interactive flows (include traffic profile and measurement windows).
2) Jonas — add the burst-window definition and the console toggle instructions to the API appendix.
3) Neha (me) — I will draft the commercial quote and SOW and loop in procurement contact from Clearwave once the workbook is updated.

Sara — I’ll aim to get the formal quote over by EOD Friday. Would you be available for a 30-min follow-up on Monday morning to review the quote and the test plan? Proposed calendar: Mon, May 31, 2027 09:30–10:00 PT.

Best,
Neha
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com

source.subject: Scaling commitment: blended uplift & co-term pathways for Cortex Health
messages:
From: Evan Park <evan.park@cortexhealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-18T09:12:00Z
Subject: Capacity bump mid-term + co-term question

Hi Marissa,

Quick question on behalf of Cortex Health — our usage is ramping faster than forecast and we need to add ~1.5x GPU capacity starting June 1. We're currently mid-contract (ends 2028-01-15) and would prefer to co-term the new capacity to our existing renewal if possible.

Can you share: 1) a rough uplift % for adding capacity mid-contract (we'll do committed flat increase, not burst), 2) whether we can blend/annualize to co-term and how that affects the effective price, and 3) any one-time credits or pro-rata treatment you typically apply?

We'd like a ballpark this week to take to procurement.

Thanks,
Evan Park
Head of Cloud Ops
Cortex Health
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Evan Park <evan.park@cortexhealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-19T14:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Capacity bump mid-term + co-term question

Evan — thanks for the note and congrats on the accelerated uptake. High-level options (ballpark figures, subject to finance approval):

Option A — Immediate add-on (mid-contract uplift):
- 12% uplift on the unit price for the added capacity, billed pro-rata from the add date (no change to original term).
- Credit: existing unused monthly credits (if any) applied first; no separate one-time credit.
- Fastest to implement; preserves current renewal as-is.

source.subject: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies
source.messages:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Hi Anna / Paul —

Following our call this morning, attaching a short evidence request to validate EuCorp's residency constraints and to finalize the private VPC topology plan for EU and APAC. High-level asks:

- Confirm hard constraints for data-at-rest (eu region only), including any allowed transient controls in APAC for failover.
- Which systems require encryption keys to be resident in-region vs keys allowed in a centralized KMS? (Please list key aliases)
- Acceptable log retention boundaries for audit logs and tracebacks (EU site vs central collection)
- Any required network peering or approved cloud providers for cross-border failover (we need the list to validate peering playbook)

I've also listed the artifacts we need to attach to the contract package: SOC 2 Type II report, KMS integration diagram, network ACL list, and a signed data- residency attestation. See attached: residency_evidence_request.docx (short checklist).

Next steps from our side: Rafael will produce the initial VPC topology PDF and Marcus will outline the control-plane handshake for failover. Can you confirm a deadline for the artifacts? We are aiming to include everything in the final SOW by 2027-06-01.

Thanks,
Naomi
Naomi Feldman
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
naomi@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0198

Attachment: residency_evidence_request.docx (docx, 28 KB)

----
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>, Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Thanks Naomi — quick update from infra/arch side.

I attached our proposed VPC topology for review (vpc-topology-eu-apac.pdf). Key points: 
- Primary data plane: EU region (single primary cluster in eu-west-1 equivalent). All customer datasets are pinned to that cluster.
- Warm-standby in APAC: compute-only nodes in ap-northeast-1 with encrypted KV cache that contains only stateless runtime caches. No persisted customer data will be written to APAC disks.
- Failover handshake: control-plane uses mutual TLS with cert pinning; failover is initiated by health checks + control-plane quorum. A preconfigured routing policy will enforce that EU-origin requests (identified by headers) always prefer EU endpoints; APAC-origin requests prefer APAC.
- KMS: we propose customer-managed keys in EU KMS for data-at-rest. For control-plane certificates and ephemeral keys, we recommend a centralized HSM in EU with cross-region read-only replication for emergency unseal (audit-controlled).

Open questions for Anna/Paul:
1) Would EuCorp accept read-only DR unlock keys replicated to APAC under strict audit and with immediate revocation capability?
2) Are there vendors (cloud or colo) you cannot accept for peering?

I'll follow up with a CSV that maps endpoints to routing policies and expected RTO/RPO.

-Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Principal Product Manager, Private Deployments | Redwood Inference
Attachment: vpc-topology-eu-apac.pdf (pdf, 450 KB)

----
From: Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 09:05:00 +02:00
Subject: Re: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Thanks Naomi and Rafael — appreciate the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from the compliance side: 

- EuCorp accepts the warm-standby compute-only approach, provided there is no persistent storage of customer PII outside EU. Please ensure logs with PII are redacted before any cross-region replication.
- On the KMS replication: we can accept read-only replicated keys in APAC only if the replica is sealed and requires a EuCorp-approved process to unseal (we will want the unseal workflow documented and auditable).
- Vendors: we cannot peer to any provider that does not support customer-managed routing controls (we have a blacklist of 3 colo vendors; I'll send internally).
- Evidence deadline: we need SOC 2 attestation + KMS diagram and the routing-failover-matrix.csv by 2027-05-28 to satisfy our internal review board.

Forwarding the SOC request to our security manager.

On Fri, May 19, 2027 at 09:12 Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Anna / Paul —
> 
> Following our call this morning, attaching a short evidence request to validate EuCorp's residency constraints and to finalize the private VPC topology plan for EU and APAC. High-level asks:
> 
> - Confirm hard constraints for data-at-rest (eu region only), including any allowed transient controls in APAC for failover.
> - Which systems require encryption keys to be resident in-region vs keys allowed in a centralized KMS? (Please list key aliases)
> - Acceptable log retention boundaries for audit logs and tracebacks (EU site vs central collection)
> - Any required network peering or approved cloud providers for cross-border failover (we need the list to validate peering playbook)
> 
> I've also listed the artifacts we need to attach to the contract package: SOC 2 Type II report, KMS integration diagram, network ACL list, and a signed data- residency attestation. See attached: residency_evidence_request.docx (short checklist).
> 
> Next steps from our side: Rafael will produce the initial VPC topology PDF and Marcus will outline the control-plane handshake for failover. Can you confirm a deadline for the artifacts? We are aiming to include everything in the final SOW by 2027-06-01.
> 
> Thanks,
> Naomi

----
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>
Cc: Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 11:05:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Anna — quick operational note on the handshake and failover matrix (summary). Full CSV attached (routing-failover-matrix.csv).

- Control-plane certs: mutual TLS with cert pinning; certs stored in EU HSM. During failover, the APAC control-plane nodes use an ephemeral TLS cert signed by an emergency CA key that is read-only replicated. Emergency CA signing requires a two-person EuCorp approval (documented).
- Health checks: we use a two-tiered system — local node liveness + cross-region quorum. Failover only occurs when both local and quorum checks fail OR when EuCorp submits an approved manual failover request via our control portal.
- Data movement: no disk writes allowed in APAC; only encrypted KV cache. Logs with potential PII will be scrubbed before replication; we will publish the scrub rules.

I'll send the CSV and a short runbook describing the manual failover steps and audit hooks to the Drive link Naomi shared.

-Marcus
Marcus Lin
Sr. Infrastructure Engineer | Redwood Inference
Attachment: routing-failover-matrix.csv (csv, 12 KB)

From: Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 09:05:00 +02:00
Subject: Re: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Thanks Naomi and Rafael — appreciate the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from the compliance side: 

- EuCorp accepts the warm-standby compute-only approach, provided there is no persistent storage of customer PII outside EU. Please ensure logs with PII are redacted before any cross-region replication.
- On the KMS replication: we can accept read-only replicated keys in APAC only if the replica is sealed and requires a EuCorp-approved process to unseal (we will want the unseal workflow documented and auditable).
- Vendors: we cannot peer to any provider that does not support customer-managed routing controls (we have a blacklist of 3 colo vendors; I'll send internally).

Forwarding the SOC request to our security manager.

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>
Cc: Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 11:05:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Anna — quick operational note on the handshake and failover matrix (summary). Full CSV attached (routing-failover-matrix.csv).

- Control-plane certs: mutual TLS with cert pinning; certs stored in EU HSM. During failover, the APAC control-plane nodes use an ephemeral TLS cert signed by an emergency CA key that is read-only replicated. Emergency CA signing requires a two-person EuCorp approval (documented).
- Health checks: we use a two-tiered system — local node liveness + cross-region quorum. Failover only occurs when both local and quorum checks fail OR when EuCorp submits an approved manual failover request via our control portal.

I'll send the CSV and a short runbook describing the manual failover steps and audit hooks to the Drive link Naomi shared.

-Marcus

text:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Hi Anna / Paul —

Following our call this morning, attaching a short evidence request to validate EuCorp's residency constraints and to finalize the private VPC topology plan for EU and APAC. High-level asks:

- Confirm hard constraints for data-at-rest (eu region only), including any allowed transient controls in APAC for failover.
- Which systems require encryption keys to be resident in-region vs keys allowed in a centralized KMS? (Please list key aliases)
- Acceptable log retention boundaries for audit logs and tracebacks (EU site vs central collection)
- Any required network peering or approved cloud providers for cross-border failover (we need the list to validate peering playbook)

I've also listed the artifacts we need to attach to the contract package: SOC 2 Type II report, KMS integration diagram, network ACL list, and a signed data- residency attestation. See attached: residency_evidence_request.docx (short checklist).

Next steps from our side: Rafael will produce the initial VPC topology PDF and Marcus will outline the control-plane handshake for failover. Can you confirm a deadline for the artifacts? We are aiming to include everything in the final SOW by 2027-06-01.

Thanks,
Naomi
Naomi Feldman
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
naomi@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0198

Attachment: residency_evidence_request.docx (docx, 28 KB)

----
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>, Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Thanks Naomi — quick update from infra/arch side.

I attached our proposed VPC topology for review (vpc-topology-eu-apac.pdf). Key points: 
- Primary data plane: EU region (single primary cluster in eu-west-1 equivalent). All customer datasets are pinned to that cluster.
- Warm-standby in APAC: compute-only nodes in ap-northeast-1 with encrypted KV cache that contains only stateless runtime caches. No persisted customer data will be written to APAC disks.
- Failover handshake: control-plane uses mutual TLS with cert pinning; failover is initiated by health checks + control-plane quorum. A preconfigured routing policy will enforce that EU-origin requests (identified by headers) always prefer EU endpoints; APAC-origin requests prefer APAC.
- KMS: we propose customer-managed keys in EU KMS for data-at-rest. For control-plane certificates and ephemeral keys, we recommend a centralized HSM in EU with cross-region read-only replication for emergency unseal (audit-controlled).

Open questions for Anna/Paul:
1) Would EuCorp accept read-only DR unlock keys replicated to APAC under strict audit and with immediate revocation capability?
2) Are there vendors (cloud or colo) you cannot accept for peering?

I'll follow up with a CSV that maps endpoints to routing policies and expected RTO/RPO.

-Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Principal Product Manager, Private Deployments | Redwood Inference
Attachment: vpc-topology-eu-apac.pdf (pdf, 450 KB)

----

source.subject: Unplanned prefix-eviction emergency patch — short-notice maintenance
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 04:12:00 +0000
Subject: Prefix-eviction anomaly causing tokenization mismatches — proposed emergency patch

Team,

We started seeing a small but growing set of requests that hit an early prefix-eviction path in the runtime which incorrectly trims the KV cache for long-conversation sessions. Symptoms: occasional tokenization mismatches and failed structured outputs for a subset of large-context chat sessions (p95 latency spike also observed). Root cause points to a regression in the eviction heuristic introduced in the recent scheduler update (sha: a1b2c3).

Impact: ~0.4% of requests across a small customer cohort (not all models). For Acme specifically, their test traffic shows repeated tracer IDs that triggered the path.

I have a hotfix patch ready that tightens the eviction guard and adds a defensive check to avoid premature KV truncation. Patch + runbook attached.

Proposal: unscheduled 30–40 minute maintenance window to apply the hotfix to the affected Dedicated cluster (eu-west-2 region for Acme). Steps are in ops_runbook_update_v2.md. Rollback designed to be immediate if we see error rates increase.

Requesting:
- Approval to proceed as soon as Kimberly or Ben confirm (I can schedule a 06:00 UTC window tonight).
- Marissa, please prep the short customer notice; keep it concise and include expected impact and rollback plan.

Attachments: kv_guard_hotfix.patch (patch), ops_runbook_update_v2.md (runbook), support-logs-20270520.tar.gz (sample logs)

Rafael
Senior SRE — Runtime Reliability
Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 04:30:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Prefix-eviction anomaly causing tokenization mismatches — proposed emergency patch

Thanks Rafael — good catch.

Ben/Marissa: please proceed. Ben, coordinate the maintenance window for 06:00–06:45 UTC tonight for the eu-west-2 Dedicated pool serving Acme. We should aim for the lowest-risk rollout: canary to 2 nodes, monitor 5m, then ramp. If telemetry shows no regressed errors, proceed to full pool.

Marissa: can you draft a short customer advisory and include the following: what we are changing (brief), why (prevent token mismatches), expected user impact (brief, likely transient errors or slight latency increase during the 5m canary), rollback criteria, and postmortem commitment. Tag me and Rafael on the draft — I want final signoff on wording.

Also, Rafael — keep the rollback path hot. If we see increased 5xx or new anomalies, abort and revert to previous scheduler image.

Kimberly Park
Head of Sales Operations (acting for this thread)
Redwood Inference
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Hensley <t.hensley@acme-payments.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, acme-ops <ops+redwood@acme-payments.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 05:02:00 +0000
Subject: Short-notice maintenance: unscheduled prefix-eviction hotfix affecting some chat sessions

Tom / Acme Ops,

I’m Ben from Redwood Inference — quick heads-up. We’ve identified an issue in our runtime that can cause occasional tokenization mismatches for longer conversations. This impacts a small subset of traffic and we’ve prepared an emergency hotfix to prevent premature KV eviction.

What we will do:
- Apply a hotfix to the Dedicated node pool that serves your EU region.
- Planned start: 2027-05-20 06:00 UTC. Duration: ~30–45 minutes. Canary (2 nodes) for 5 minutes then ramp if stable.

Potential impact to you:
- Brief elevated error rate or transient structured-output mismatches for requests that hit the affected code path during the roll. We expect impact to be small and short-lived.
- No user-facing data loss. All customer data and logs are retained.

Rollback:
- We will abort and revert immediately if we observe increased error rates or new regressions.

Follow-ups:
- We will send a confirmation when the change is complete and share a postmortem with root cause and mitigations within 48 hours.

If you cannot accommodate the window above or prefer a different start time, please reply here immediately — we can reschedule to suit your maintenance hours.

Attachments referenced: kv_guard_hotfix.patch (internal), ops_runbook_update_v2.md (runbook).

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Platform
Redwood Inference

On Thu, May 20, 2027 at 04:30 Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Rafael — good catch.
>
> Ben/Marissa: please proceed. Ben, coordinate the maintenance window for 06:00–06:45 UTC tonight for the eu-west-2 Dedicated pool serving Acme. We should aim for the lowest-risk rollout: canary to 2 nodes, monitor 5m, then ramp. If telemetry shows no regressed errors, proceed to full pool.
>
> Marissa: can you draft a short customer advisory and include the following: what we are changing (brief), why (prevent token mismatches), expected user impact (brief, likely transient errors or slight latency increase during the 5m canary), rollback criteria, and postmortem commitment. Tag me and Rafael on the draft — I want final signoff on wording.

From: Tom Hensley <t.hensley@acme-payments.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: acme-ops <ops+redwood@acme-payments.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 09:03:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Short-notice maintenance: unscheduled prefix-eviction hotfix affecting some chat sessions

Ben,

Thanks for the quick notification and for the conservative rollout plan. The 06:00 UTC window works for us. Two quick asks:
1) Please confirm once the canary (2-node) completes and again when the rollout is finished.
2) We will need a short postmortem including the root cause, steps you took, and any customer-facing mitigations — targeted within 48 hours is fine. Also include any recommended changes to our integration/usage patterns if applicable.

Appreciate the transparency.

—Tom
Senior Engineering Manager, Platform Integrations
Acme Payment Systems

> On Thu, May 20, 2027 at 05:02 Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Tom / Acme Ops,
> >
> > I’m Ben from Redwood Inference — quick heads-up. We’ve identified an issue in our runtime that can cause occasional tokenization mismatches for longer conversations. This impacts a small subset of traffic and we’ve prepared an emergency hotfix to prevent premature KV eviction.
> >
> > What we will do:
> > - Apply a hotfix to the Dedicated node pool that serves your EU region.
> > - Planned start: 2027-05-20 06:00 UTC. Duration: ~30–45 minutes. Canary (2 nodes) for 5 minutes then ramp if stable.
> >
> > Potential impact to you: ...

I’m Ben from Redwood Inference — quick heads-up. We’ve identified an issue in our runtime that can cause occasional tokenization mismatches for longer conversations. This impacts a small subset of traffic and we’ve prepared an emergency hotfix to prevent premature KV eviction.

What we will do:
- Apply a hotfix to the Dedicated node pool that serves your EU region.
- Planned start: 2027-05-20 06:00 UTC. Duration: ~30–45 minutes. Canary (2 nodes) for 5 minutes then ramp if stable.

Potential impact to you:
- Brief elevated error rate or transient structured-output mismatches for requests that hit the affected code path during the roll. We expect impact to be small and short-lived.
- No user-facing data loss. All customer data and logs are retained.

Rollback:
- We will abort and revert immediately if we observe increased error rates or new regressions.

Follow-ups:
- We will send a confirmation when the change is complete and share a postmortem with root cause and mitigations within 48 hours.

If you cannot accommodate the window above or prefer a different start time, please reply here immediately — we can reschedule to suit your maintenance hours.

Attachments referenced: kv_guard_hotfix.patch (internal), ops_runbook_update_v2.md (runbook).

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Platform
Redwood Inference

On Thu, May 20, 2027 at 04:30 Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Rafael — good catch.
>
> Ben/Marissa: please proceed. Ben, coordinate the maintenance window for 06:00–06:45 UTC tonight for the eu-west-2 Dedicated pool serving Acme. We should aim for the lowest-risk rollout: canary to 2 nodes, monitor 5m, then ramp. If telemetry shows no regressed errors, proceed to full pool.
>
> Marissa: can you draft a short customer advisory and include the following: what we are changing (brief), why (prevent token mismatches), expected user impact (brief, likely transient errors or slight latency increase during the 5m canary), rollback criteria, and postmortem commitment. Tag me and Rafael on the draft — I want final signoff on wording.



From: Tom Hensley <t.hensley@acme-payments.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: acme-ops <ops+redwood@acme-payments.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 09:03:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Short-notice maintenance: unscheduled prefix-eviction hotfix affecting some chat sessions

Ben,

Thanks for the quick notification and for the conservative rollout plan. The 06:00 UTC window works for us. Two quick asks:
1) Please confirm once the canary (2-node) completes and again when the rollout is finished.
2) We will need a short postmortem including the root cause, steps you took, and any customer-facing mitigations — targeted within 48 hours is fine. Also include any recommended changes to our integration/usage patterns if applicable.

Appreciate the transparency.

—Tom
Senior Engineering Manager, Platform Integrations
Acme Payment Systems

source.subject: Post-call: stream handshake, concurrency & quota recap
messages:
From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Cloud <avery@averycloud.com>, Jordan Price <jordan.price@averycloud.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: stream handshake, concurrency & quota recap

Thanks again for the time today — great discussion. Below is a concise recap of the performance requirements you shared and the immediate asks we agreed to surface to our capacity and pricing teams. Please flag anything I missed.

Summary of requirements (from call)
- Latency SLO: P99 <= 150ms for single-stream low-latency responses; P95 <= 60ms for short streaming tokens (customer priority is interactive demos).
- Streaming pattern: 60% of requests use streaming responses; average response length ~120 tokens; bursts up to 600 concurrent live streams for peak demo events.
- Throughput: sustained ingest ~6k tokens/sec, spike capacity up to 40k tokens/sec for short windows (1–5 min).
- Concurrency: typical steady-state concurrency ~120 parallel sessions; expected demo spikes up to 600 parallel sessions for ~3–7 minutes.
- Connection model: customer will multiplex many logical streams over a small fleet of long-lived connections (keep-alive), prefer 128 connection pool size per client IP.
- Failure tolerance: graceful fallback to a lower-capacity model variant; no full-request drops during demo events (must keep 95% of streams active).

Immediate next steps we agreed
1) Avery: share a 48-hour sample traffic export (CSV) with per-minute stream count and token counts — you said you can send this by EOD Wednesday.
2) Redwood (Rafael/Olga): propose a temporary burst quota and connection-pool configuration that covers demo spikes, plus delta pricing for burst windows.
3) Redwood (Ben): prepare a single-sheet worksheet showing the proposed quota, expected cost delta, and estimated latency SLO impact for each option.
4) All: 30-min follow-up call Friday 10:00am PT to review the worksheet and DocuSign-ready terms for the burst addendum.

Attachments: I attached our connection worksheet template and marked sections for your traffic numbers. Also attached a short example CSV schema so your engineering team can format the sample.

Questions for your engineering team (if you can include answers with the CSV):
- Are streams pinned to unique user sessions or can they be multiplexed across shared sockets?
- Average token encode vs. generated tokens per request (if you have a breakdown).
- Are there authentication sharding patterns (many client keys vs. single service token)?

Thanks — I'll coordinate internally and aim to get a proposed quota + pricing update within 24 business hours after we receive the CSV.

Cheers,
Olga Petrov
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.ai
attachments: redwood-conn-worksheet.xlsx (spreadsheet), example-traffic-schema.csv (CSV)
From: Jordan Price <jordan.price@averycloud.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
Cc: Avery Cloud <avery@averycloud.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 08:41:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: stream handshake, concurrency & quota recap

Thanks Olga — appreciate the clear recap. A few clarifications from our side before we send the CSV: 

- Streams are tied to user sessions, but we multiplex 4 logical sessions per TCP connection in our current websocket gateway. For demos we expect the multiplex factor to be closer to 1:1 due to client id isolation.
- Token breakdown: encode (input) ~20 tokens median per request, generated median ~110 tokens.
- Auth: single service token per demo host (we won't shard client keys for the pilot).

We'll attach the 48-hour export by EOD Thursday (engineering needs one day to strip PII). We'll also include a note with timestamps for the demo peak yesterday (that had ~420 concurrent streams for ~4 minutes).

On quota: can you confirm if Redwood can temporarily raise the connection pool to ~700 for demo windows and if that adjustment will be charged as burst usage or an increased committed capacity? We prefer a burst model for now.

Cheers,
Jordan
Senior Platform Engineer | Avery Cloud
jordan.price@averycloud.com

On Tue, May 18, 2027 at 3:02 PM Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [original message above]
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: stream handshake, concurrency & quota recap

Forwarding with internal recommendation for negotiation. Key notes:

- Short-term approach (recommended): grant a time-bound burst quota covering 700 concurrent streams and token spikes to 40k tokens/sec for windows up to 10 minutes. Bill as "burst units" at a premium multiplier (x1.8) to avoid changing committed capacity. Reserve a safety margin: cap at 750 concurrent streams to prevent oversubscription.
- Connection pooling: allow client-level connection_pool_limit of 700 for demo windows; recommend enforcing per-IP throttles (5000 RPS per IP) and a soft queue for extra connections.
- Latency SLOs: with that capacity we project P99 ~180–220ms for heavy spikes on shared GPU pools; if Avery needs strict P99 <=150ms during spikes, propose a short Dedicated slot reservation for demo windows (2x A100-equivalent) — price estimate in the worksheet.
- Requirements from Avery: traffic sample (CSV), preferred demo window timestamps, and expected number of simultaneous developers accessing demos.

Ben — can you draft the worksheet with three options: (A) burst-only, (B) burst + slightly increased commit, (C) short Dedicated reservation for critical demos. Put estimated cost delta and expected P99 for each.

Rafael
Head of Solutions | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai

On Wed, May 19, 2027 at 08:41 AM Jordan Price <jordan.price@averycloud.com> wrote:
> [Jordan's clarifying message above]
From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Price <jordan.price@averycloud.com>, Avery Cloud <avery@averycloud.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: stream handshake, concurrency & quota recap

Appreciate the quick clarifications, Jordan — looking forward to the CSV. Updated mutual action list and proposed times: 

- Redwood will draft a burst addendum (covers 700 concurrent streams, up to 40k tokens/sec bursts, soft cap 750) and a worksheet with three options by EOD Thursday (my ask: Rafael/Ben to finalize by EOD Wednesday internal).
- Avery to send 48-hour CSV by end of day Thursday (thanks in advance).
- Follow-up call: Friday 10:00am PT — confirm via calendar invite.

If the CSV shows sustained 300+ concurrency outside demo windows we should consider option C (short Dedicated). For the burst model we will:
- Provision a temporary connection_pool_limit override (700) for the scheduled demo windows.
- Apply burst billing for the demo window usage.

DocuSign: I'll push the addendum via DocuSign once we agree on the worksheet — placeholder link below.
https://docusign.com/embedded/abcdef123456

Ben / Rafael — please review the attached draft worksheet (redwood-conn-worksheet.xlsx) and update pricing cells. I'll send calendar invite for Friday after I get the CSV.

Thanks,
Olga
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.ai
attachments: redwood-conn-worksheet.xlsx

On Wed, May 19, 2027 at 10:05 AM Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [Rafael's internal recommendation above]

source.subject: Procurement kickoff: ops QA, portal onboarding, and demo scheduling
source.thread:
From: Sophie Vale <sophie.vale@acmetech.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: procure@acmetech.com
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Procurement kickoff: renewal & capacity uplift

Hi Avery / Rafael,

Thanks again for the walkthrough last week. Acme is planning a 2x capacity uplift at renewal and we need to refresh our vendor onboarding artifacts so procurement can approve the PO. Attached is the procurement_onboarding_form.pdf with our fields pre-filled — we still need Redwood's SOC2 attestation and a brief mutual action plan showing deliverables and dates.

Key asks from procurement side:
- Is there a one-page SOC2 summary or an attestation letter we can attach?
- Can Redwood confirm the contract amendment timeline (we hope to sign by 2027-06-02)?
- We need an ops QA session for our SREs to validate prefix caching behavior and regional routing (30–45 min).

Procurement contact: procure@acmetech.com. Please loop them into cadence and share a DocuSign link when the amendment is ready.

Thanks,
Sophie Vale
Head of Vendor Ops
Acme Technology

---

On Mon, 17 May 2027 at 12:34, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com> wrote:
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>

Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 12:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Procurement kickoff: renewal & capacity uplift

Hi Sophie — thanks, received.

We can absolutely provide an SOC2 summary (I mean, we have a one-page attestation summary) and put together a short mutual action plan for signature. Id propose the following immediate steps so procurement has everything they need:  
1) Redwood to upload SOC2_summary.pdf to your procurement portal (we can also email directly).
2) Schedule an ops QA session with our runtime SREs (30 min) and your SRE/eng. Suggested slots: Tue 5/18 09:00 PT or Wed 5/19 11:00 PT.
3) Draft amendment + MAP and share a DocuSign link the same day we have sign-off on scope.

I've attached MAP_draft_v1.docx (draft MAP + milestones). Rafael — can you confirm the amendment language we discussed last quarter still applies for the capacity uplift and whether finance needs a formal quote line item?

Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

---

On Tue, 18 May 2027 at 09:11, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com> wrote:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Sophie Vale <sophie.vale@acmetech.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 09:11:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Procurement kickoff: renewal & capacity uplift

Thanks Sophie — and thanks Avery for pulling the MAP together. Quick notes from finance/ops perspective: 

- We need a firm PO amount to push through Acme's PO lane. The PO should include the base renewal term plus the capacity uplift as a line item (we can provide a recommended quoting template if helpful).
- Finance will request evidence of controls: SOC2_summary.pdf is good; procurement may also ask for data retention and deletion policy snippets (we can attach a short extract).
- If the customer will sign a short amendment (term extension + capacity change), we prefer the amendment to reference existing contract IDs to avoid a full reprocurement.

I've attached SOC2_summary.pdf and a one-page controls extract. Rafael (internal) note: Grace, can you confirm the legal redline timing? We may need a two-business-day turnaround once the amendment draft is in DocuSign.

Rafael Mendes
Senior Commercial Ops, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Mutual action timeline & procurement gating — Meridian pilot acceptance runway
messages:
From: Alyssa Cole <alyssa.cole@meridianhealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 03 May 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot MAP & procurement gating questions

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks again for the demo last week — Meridian’s digital care team is excited to move the pilot forward. Procurement/legal asked me to put together a short mutual action plan (MAP) so they can slot resources (legal and procurement) and confirm timeline expectations. A couple of specific asks from their side: 

- Can you call out the dependent deliverables we need to close (MSA + DPA + order form) and how they affect the pilot start date?
- Any constraints around data residency or additional compliance paperwork we should expect?
- Estimated time to receive final counters (assuming standard redline flow)? We prefer a firm date range for internal gating.

We’d like to aim for a pilot kickoff in early June if possible — but procurement wants to understand the critical path and milestones.

Attaching Meridian’s intake form for reference. 

Best,
Alyssa
Alyssa Cole
Program Manager, Digital Care
Meridian Health
[Attachment: meridian_intake_form.pdf (pdf)]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alyssa Cole <alyssa.cole@meridianhealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 04 May 2027 11:38:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot MAP & procurement gating questions

Hi Alyssa — thanks, and great to hear the team is ready to move. Below is a concise MAP and the sequencing I recommend. I’ve attached a one‑page MAP and a proposed acceptance criteria template so Procurement can validate milestones quickly.

High-level sequencing (critical path):
1) MSA review (legal) — Redwood to receive initial redlines; target turnaround 5–7 business days after we share draft.
2) DPA confirmation (privacy) — runs in parallel with MSA but requires final MSA terms for signoff; target 3 business days after MSA agreement.
3) Order form & SOW (commercial) — order form references agreed SLOs and pilot acceptance criteria; finalizes pricing and billing cadence.
4) DocuSign execution & onboarding kickoff — once MSA/DPA/order form signed, we schedule a 2 week onboarding cadence for pilot instrumentation and acceptance tests.

Suggested dates (assuming we start the MSA share on 5/5):
- Redwood shares MSA v1: 2027-05-05
- Meridian returns redlines: by 2027-05-12 (5 business days)
- DPA signoff: by 2027-05-15
- Order form finalized and countersigned: target 2027-05-20
- Pilot onboarding kickoff: week of 2027-05-24

Notes / gating items:
- If Meridian needs vendor security evidence (SOC 2 report, data flow diagrams), we can package an evidence bundle; anticipated prep time 2 business days.
- Changes to acceptance criteria (SLA/tolerance) will push the order form finalization date. Please confirm the key acceptance tests so we can bake them into the SOW.

Attachments: MAP_proposal_meridian_v1.pdf, acceptance_criteria.csv

Next steps: If this sequencing looks okay, I’ll loop in Marcus (pricing) and Laura (CS/onboarding) to lock internal owners and intake the MSA for routing to our legal counsel. Would Meridian legal prefer tracked redlines or a clean countersign?

Thanks — KL
Kimberly Park
Senior Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: MAP_proposal_meridian_v1.pdf (pdf)]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 05 May 2027 08:50:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Pilot MAP & procurement gating questions

Team — forwarding Meridian’s intake and my proposed MAP. Quick asks so we can align on the milestone owners and realistic SLAs: 

- Marcus: can you confirm whether the pilot order form pricing needs internal approvals or discount committee review (this could add 3–5 business days)?
- Laura: onboarding window — we’d need a 10 business day schedule to complete instrumentation and acceptance tests; can CS support a condensed 7 business day pilot if procurement insists on early kickoff?
- Rafael (legal): I plan to send MSA v1 to Meridian on 5/05; can you commit to initial redline rundown within 5 business days? If we need external counsel review we should call that out now.

Quoted for context:
> On Mon, May 3, 2027 at 09:14 Alyssa Cole <alyssa.cole@meridianhealth.com> wrote:
> > Thanks again for the demo last week — Meridian’s digital care team is excited to move the pilot forward. Procurement/legal asked me to put together a short mutual action plan (MAP) so they can slot resources (legal and procurement) and confirm timeline expectations.
> >  — Alyssa

Please reply with availability and any blockers; I’d like to confirm the milestone owners by EOD tomorrow so I can share an updated MAP with Meridian.

Thanks — Kimberly
[Attachment: acceptance_criteria.csv (csv)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Tom Becker <tbecker@meridianhealth.com>
Cc: Alyssa Cole <alyssa.cole@meridianhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 16:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Mutual action timeline & procurement gating — Meridian pilot acceptance runway

Hi all — looping Meridian legal in and attaching our redlines for the MSA. We reviewed and the internal approvals for the pilot order form are covered under standard delegated authority; no discount committee required for the pilot pricing as structured.

Key points from our side: 
- MSA redlines attached (MSA_redlines_v1.docx). Please review and return comments within 3 business days if possible so we can keep the timeline.
- Data residency: Redwood will store pilot telemetry in US regions only; if Meridian requires EU residency we’ll need a DPA addendum and additional lead time.
- Acceptance criteria: see attached CSV; Meridian should confirm the three tests (ingest latency, inference latency P95, and output fidelity sampling) for SOW inclusion.

Next steps: 
1) Meridian legal (Tom) to confirm preference for tracked redlines vs. clean copy and share any high‑priority clauses (indemnity, liability cap, data residency).
2) If Tom returns redlines by 5/13, we can finalize DPA and order form by 5/18 and execute by 5/20.

DocuSign link (prepped for Meridian): https://www.docusign.com/1234-5678-meridian-msaredline

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Head of Commercial Operations, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: MSA_redlines_v1.docx (docx)]

source.subject: Throughput assurance & term-flex proposal for Dedicated GPU pool
messages:
From: Samira Khan <samira.k@orchidhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@orchidhealth.com
Date: 2027-05-03T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-pilot: committed capacity recommendation?

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for running the pilot last month. The team is happy with latency but we need to lock down committed capacity and commercial terms before we expand to production. A few specifics we want covered in the proposal:

- Sustained throughput target: ~350–450 inference qps (70–90 concurrent users, 8–12 tokens per request on average) with 95th pct latency under 250ms.
- Burst capacity for peak sessions (up to 800 qps for 10–15 minutes).
- Pricing bands for 12-month vs 24-month commits; flexibility to scale up/down after quarter 1.
- Any credits or transition support for model quantization/ops tuning.

I've attached the pilot_load_test_summary.pdf with our telemetry (traces and sample logs). Procurement is cc'd and will need an estimated MRR for budgeting.

Would appreciate a written recommendation and a commercial worksheet we can review with finance.

Best,
Samira
Director of Product
Orchid Health

Attachment: pilot_load_test_summary.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@orchidhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-03T13:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-pilot: committed capacity recommendation?

Hi Samira —

Thanks for the clear asks and for the pilot data. We pulled the telemetry into our sizing tool and I copied Marissa (SE) and Laura (sales lead) to help with the commercial side. Quick notes up front:

- The pilot traces look congruent with a 400 qps sustained target if we allocate ~8 A100-equivalent GPUs (our A100-equivalent metric factors in quantization benefits you saw).
- For brief spikes to ~800 qps we recommend burst credits (short-lived surplus capacity) coupled with autoscaling rules that cap cost exposure.
- We can offer a term-flex pricing structure (12 mo vs 24 mo) with tiered discounts and an explicit scale-up window in Q1.

Marissa will follow up with the sizing calc and a recommended MRR band; Laura will attach a commercial worksheet. If possible, can procurement share the internal budget window and whether a single PO or monthly invoicing is preferred?

I'll be on the call Friday to walk through the details — suggest 30 minutes.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0182

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-04T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: sizing notes & recommended term-flex structure (notes for Samira)

Team —

I dug into the attached pilot summary and ran it against our production-equivalent load simulator. Short version and recommended commercial guardrails:

Sizing recommendation (conservative):
- Baseline: 8 A100-equivalent GPUs to sustain 400 qps at p95 latency ~180–220ms (model: open-gpt-large, sequence avg 12 tokens).
- Burst headroom: additional 4 GPU-equivalents as "burst credits" that can be used for 15-minute windows; handled via transient capacity pool.
- KV-cache hit patterns in the pilot show a 12% effective token saving; we baked that into the calc.

Commercial proposal (term-flex):
- 12-month commit: 20% list discount off hosted-dedicated baseline; includes 500k token credits for ops tuning + 8 hours of SE support for quantization and runtime tuning.
- 24-month commit: 28% list discount, 3 months of burst credits included, and prioritized slot reservations during region maintenance windows.
- Add-on: option to convert burst credits to permanent capacity at pro‑rated rates within first 120 days.

SLA/guarantees:
- Sustained throughput guarantee: We commit to 400 qps (±5%) at p95 latency under normal operating load with an availability SLA of 99.9% for Dedicated pool.
- Exclusions: sustained DDoS, or customer-driven load tests > 2x committed average unless pre-notified.

Attached is sizing_calcs.xlsx with the per-GPU qps envelope and a commercial_termflex_v2.xlsx draft. Rafael — please sanity-check the discount math and margin impact; I'm showing ~32% gross margin at 20% list discount for a 12-mo.

I'll prepare a short one-pager for Samira summarizing the trade-offs (commit vs burst).

Thanks,
Marissa
Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachment: sizing_calcs.xlsx (Excel, 45KB); commercial_termflex_v2.xlsx (Excel, 22KB)

From: procurement@orchidhealth.com <procurement@orchidhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Samira Khan <samira.k@orchidhealth.com>
Date: 2027-05-05T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Throughput assurance & term-flex proposal for Dedicated GPU pool

Ben / Samira —

Thanks for the draft. A couple procurement items we need clarified before we can approve:

1) Price protection: if we commit 12 months at the 20% discount, will there be protection against upward list price changes for that term? We need a firm not-to-exceed price.
2) Early termination: standard termination with pro-rated refunds is acceptable but we need language for a 90-day notice and a cap on termination fees.
3) SLA credits: define methodology for SLA measurement and credit calculation.

Attached is our standard_vendor_terms_and_security.zip (excerpted questionnaire attached as orchid_security_questionnaire.docx).

Regarding discount: our finance team is asking for 25% as a target for 12 months if we can get price protection and a modest termination cap. If you can get within 22–24% with the SLA language, we can likely sign.

Please advise — we need to finalize the MRR by next Tuesday for the May board packet.

Regards,
Procurement
Orchid Health

Attachment: orchid_security_questionnaire.docx (DOCX, 150KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: procurement@orchidhealth.com <procurement@orchidhealth.com>, Samira Khan <samira.k@orchidhealth.com>

Subject: Re: Throughput assurance & term-flex proposal for Dedicated GPU pool

Procurement team / Samira —

Appreciate the clarity. We've updated the commercial worksheet and the legal/ops guardrails to reflect your asks. Key changes in v2:

- Price protection: committed MRR will be locked for the duration of the agreed term (12 or 24 months) — list increases do not affect the committed price.
- Early termination: 90-day notice is acceptable; termination fee capped at the greater of (3 months committed MRR) or actual usage delta for the quarter.
- SLA credits: measured at the Dedicated pool level using token/latency sampling; credits calculated as % of monthly MRR (standard table included in the worksheet).

Commercial concessions offered:
- 12-month commit: 22% discount off list, includes 8 hours SE tuning + 500k token credits.
- 24-month commit: 30% discount, 3 months burst credits included, and reserved slot priority.

Next steps: I attached commercial_termflex_v2.xlsx (final draft) and a DocuSign envelope link for the Statement of Work + Order Form. If Orchid signs the 12-month at 22% today, we can schedule the private onboarding runbook session next week and lock the burst credit pool.

title: Throughput assurance & term-flex proposal for Dedicated GPU pool
preview:
From: Samira Khan <samira.k@orchidhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@orchidhealth.com
Date: 2027-05-03T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-pilot: committed capacity recommendation?

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for running the pilot last month. The team is happy with latency but we need to lock down committed capacity and commercial terms before we expand to production. A few specifics we want covered in the proposal:

- Sustained throughput target: ~350–450 inference qps (70–90 concurrent users, 8–12 tokens per request on average) with 95th pct latency under 250ms.
- Burst capacity for peak sessions (up to 800 qps for 10–15 minutes).
- Pricing bands for 12-month vs 24-month commits; flexibility to scale up/down after quarter 1.
- Any credits or transition support for model quantization/ops tuning.

I've attached the pilot_load_test_summary.pdf with our telemetry (traces and sample logs). Procurement is cc'd and will need an estimated MRR for budgeting.

Would appreciate a written recommendation and a commercial worksheet we can review with finance.

Best,
Samira
Director of Product
Orchid Health

Attachment: pilot_load_test_summary.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira.k@orchidhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-03T13:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-pilot: committed capacity recommendation?

Hi Samira —

Thanks for the clear asks and for the pilot data. We pulled the telemetry into our sizing tool and I copied Marissa (SE) and Laura (sales lead) to help with the commercial side. Quick notes up front:

- The pilot traces look congruent with a 400 qps sustained target if we allocate ~8 A100-equivalent GPUs (our A100-equivalent metric factors in quantization benefits you saw).
- For brief spikes to ~800 qps we recommend burst credits (short-lived surplus capacity) coupled with autoscaling rules that cap cost exposure.
- We can offer a term-flex pricing structure (12 mo vs 24 mo) with tiered discounts and an explicit scale-up window in Q1.

Marissa will follow up with the sizing calc and a recommended MRR band; Laura will attach a commercial worksheet. If possible, can procurement share the internal budget window and whether a single PO or monthly invoicing is preferred?

I'll be on the call Friday to walk through the details — suggest 30 minutes.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
