From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-04T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: sizing notes & recommended term-flex structure (notes for Samira)

Team —

I dug into the attached pilot summary and ran it against our production-equivalent load simulator. Short version and recommended commercial guardrails:

Sizing recommendation (conservative):
- Baseline: 8 A100-equivalent GPUs to sustain 400 qps at p95 latency ~180–220ms (model: open-gpt-large, sequence avg 12 tokens).
- Burst headroom: additional 4 GPU-equivalents as "burst credits" that can be used for 15-minute windows; handled via transient capacity pool.
- KV-cache hit patterns in the pilot show a 12% effective token saving; we baked that into the calc.

Commercial proposal (term-flex):
- 12-month commit: 20% list discount off hosted-dedicated baseline; includes 500k token credits for ops tuning + 8 hours of SE support for quantization and runtime tuning.
- 24-month commit: 28% list discount, 3 months of burst credits included, and prioritized slot reservations during region maintenance windows.
- Add-on: option to convert burst credits to permanent capacity at pro‑rated rates within first 120 days.

SLA/guarantees:
- Sustained throughput guarantee: We commit to 400 qps (±5%) at p95 latency under normal operating load with an availability SLA of 99.9% for Dedicated pool.
- Exclusions: sustained DDoS, or customer-driven load tests > 2x committed average unless pre-notified.

Attached is sizing_calcs.xlsx with the per-GPU qps envelope and a commercial_termflex_v2.xlsx draft. Rafael — please sanity-check the discount math and margin impact; I'm showing ~32% gross margin at 20% list discount for a 12-mo.

I'll prepare a short one-pager for Samira summarizing the trade-offs (commit vs burst).

Thanks,
Marissa
Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachment: sizing_calcs.xlsx (Excel, 45KB); commercial_termflex_v2.xlsx (Excel, 22KB)

Thanks for the draft. A couple procurement items we need clarified before we can approve:

1) Price protection: if we commit 12 months at the 20% discount, will there be protection against upward list price changes for that term? We need a firm not-to-exceed price.
2) Early termination: standard termination with pro-rated refunds is acceptable but we need language for a 90-day notice and a cap on termination fees.
3) SLA credits: define methodology for SLA measurement and credit calculation.

Attached is our standard_vendor_terms_and_security.zip (excerpted questionnaire attached as orchid_security_questionnaire.docx).

Regarding discount: our finance team is asking for 25% as a target for 12 months if we can get price protection and a modest termination cap. If you can get within 22–24% with the SLA language, we can likely sign.

Please advise — we need to finalize the MRR by next Tuesday for the May board packet.

Regards,

source.subject: Confirming: no model personalization + log sanitization plan
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Johnson <ajohnson@cortextech.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Confirming: no model personalization + log sanitization plan

Hi Alex / Elena,

Thanks again for the call this morning — good to meet the CortexTech team. Quick recap and the commitments we discussed so we can move to contracting: 

- Training-on-customer-data: CortexTech will opt out of any "model improvement" or training pipelines. Redwood will not persist any customer data for model retraining or external tuning.
- Real-time inference telemetry: We will collect minimal operational telemetry (inference latency, error rates, token counts).
- PII handling: Any values matching PII patterns (SSNs, credit card numbers, unstructured personal emails) will be removed or replaced via automatic redaction before storage or logs.
- Log retention & anonymization: Logs (request/response) will be stored for 60 days by default, with automatic field-level hashing of user identifiers. After 60 days the records are permanently deleted.
- Data residency: All production inference and logs for your account will live in the US region (us-west-2) unless you request EU residency.

Next steps I promised:
1) I attached our 1-pager on privacy posture and a draft DPA addendum that encodes the opt-out on training and the 60-day retention.
2) Rafael will follow up with a short note on how PII redaction works (regex + entity detector + configurable policy).
3) Lauren, can you confirm legal review timeline for the DPA addendum?

If that matches what you require, I can attach the signed addendum to the SOW and hand to procurement. If anything above is incorrect or you'd like the retention shortened to 14 days we can update the DPA.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: redwood_privacy_onepager.pdf, DPA_Addendum_v1.docx

From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cortextech.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Johnson <ajohnson@cortextech.com>, Procurement <procurement@cortextech.com>
Date: Wed, 5 May 2027 09:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Confirming: no model personalization + log sanitization plan

Aditya —

Thanks for the summary, that looks broadly correct and appreciated for the clarity. A few quick clarifications from our side: 

1) Auditability: Our security team needs immutable audit logs showing access to raw request data (even if redacted) for 180 days as part of our audit program. Is there a mechanism to export redaction proofs or access logs that meet SOC2/AICPA standards?
2) Tokenization: Can you confirm whether the KV cache used for latency improvements ever stores unredacted user content? We need guarantees that the cache also enforces the same PII redaction and TTLs.
3) Procurement asked about liability language — they'll want the opt-out on model training codified in the DPA and a simple addendum to the main SaaS agreement.

Alex and I prefer the 60-day retention if redaction proofs are available; otherwise we may ask for 14 days. Please have Rafael and Lauren weigh in so we can confirm for procurement.

Thanks,
Elena Morales
Head of Platform Security | CortexTech
elena.morales@cortextech.com

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cortextech.com>, Alex Johnson <ajohnson@cortextech.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 5 May 2027 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Confirming: no model personalization + log sanitization plan

Hi Elena / Alex — quick technical replies inline: 

1) Auditability: Redwood can emit an export package with redaction checksums and an access audit trail that shows which internal Redwood users accessed the tenant logs, timestamps, and operation IDs. That package is stored for 180 days upon request (export only) and is signed with our internal artifact signature. We do not keep the raw unredacted payload after the configured retention window.

2) KV cache: The inference KV cache is configured per-tenant. We do not write raw unredacted customer text to disk in the cache; all KV entries are derived from tokenized, normalized representations. For customers that opt out of any model learning, we disable cross-tenant cache sharing and enforce a per-tenant TTL that defaults to 1 hour (configurable down to 0). We will include this configuration in the DPA addendum.

3) DPA language: Aditya — suggest adding a paragraph explicitly stating: "Redwood will not use customer-provided data to improve models or models' parameters. All telemetry and logs will be anonymized prior to any persistent storage. Customer may request export of signed redaction proofs for up to 180 days following data creation."

If the CortexTech security team wants a short architecture diagram I can send a one-pager showing where redaction runs in the pipeline.

-- Rafael
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cortextech.com>, Procurement <procurement@cortextech.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 6 May 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Confirming: no model personalization + log sanitization plan

Elena / CortexTech procurement team —

Thanks for the questions. Legal has reviewed the DPA addendum and updated a few clauses to explicitly reflect the opt-out on model training, the 60-day retention with an option to reduce to 14 days, and the export process for redaction proof packages. Key notes: 

- We can codify the opt-out as a non-optional contract term scoped to your account ID (CT-2048) and will include a signed addendum to the Master SaaS Agreement.
- Liability: standard mutual indemnities remain; we added a sentence limiting liability for redaction failures only to direct damages capped at the contract value for the first 12 months.
- Audit artifacts: we will provide the export package (signed and time-stamped) that Rafael described; it is an export, not continuous retention of raw payloads.

Aditya — I've attached the redlined DPA (see attachment stub) and will send the DocuSign link once the CortexTech team confirms retention window. Elena — please confirm whether you want 60 or 14 days and we'll circulate DocuSign for signatures.

Best,
Lauren Bishop
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.ai

Attachment: DPA_Addendum_v1.docx (redline)

3) DPA language: Aditya — suggest adding a paragraph explicitly stating: \"Redwood will not use customer-provided data to improve models or models' parameters. All telemetry and logs will be anonymized prior to any persistent storage. Customer may request export of signed redaction proofs for up to 180 days following data creation.\"

If the CortexTech security team wants a short architecture diagram I can send a one-pager showing where redaction runs in the pipeline.

-- Rafael
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cortextech.com>, Procurement <procurement@cortextech.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 6 May 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Confirming: no model personalization + log sanitization plan

Elena / CortexTech procurement team —

Thanks for the questions. Legal has reviewed the DPA addendum and updated a few clauses to explicitly reflect the opt-out on model training, the 60-day retention with an option to reduce to 14 days, and the export process for redaction proof packages. Key notes: 

- We can codify the opt-out as a non-optional contract term scoped to your account ID (CT-2048) and will include a signed addendum to the Master SaaS Agreement.
- Liability: standard mutual indemnities remain; we added a sentence limiting liability for redaction failures only to direct damages capped at the contract value for the first 12 months.
- Audit artifacts: we will provide the export package (signed and time-stamped) that Rafael described; it is an export, not continuous retention of raw payloads.

Aditya — I've attached the redlined DPA (see attachment stub) and will send the DocuSign link once the CortexTech team confirms retention window. Elena — please confirm whether you want 60 or 14 days and we'll circulate DocuSign for signatures.

Best,
Lauren Bishop
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.ai

Attachment: DPA_Addendum_v1.docx (redline)
text:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Johnson <ajohnson@cortextech.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Confirming: no model personalization + log sanitization plan

Hi Alex / Elena,

Thanks again for the call this morning — good to meet the CortexTech team. Quick recap and the commitments we discussed so we can move to contracting: 

- Training-on-customer-data: CortexTech will opt out of any \"model improvement\" or training pipelines. Redwood will not persist any customer data for model retraining or external tuning.
- Real-time inference telemetry: We will collect minimal operational telemetry (inference latency, error rates, token counts).
- PII handling: Any values matching PII patterns (SSNs, credit card numbers, unstructured personal emails) will be removed or replaced via automatic redaction before storage or logs.
- Log retention & anonymization: Logs (request/response) will be stored for 60 days by default, with automatic field-level hashing of user identifiers. After 60 days the records are permanently deleted.
- Data residency: All production inference and logs for your account will live in the US region (us-west-2) unless you request EU residency.

Next steps I promised:
1) I attached our 1-pager on privacy posture and a draft DPA addendum that encodes the opt-out on training and the 60-day retention.
2) Rafael will follow up with a short note on how PII redaction works (regex + entity detector + configurable policy).
3) Lauren, can you confirm legal review timeline for the DPA addendum?

If that matches what you require, I can attach the signed addendum to the SOW and hand to procurement. If anything above is incorrect or you'd like the retention shortened to 14 days we can update the DPA.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: redwood_privacy_onepager.pdf, DPA_Addendum_v1.docx


From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@cortextech.com>

Thanks for the summary, that looks broadly correct and appreciated for the clarity. A few quick clarifications from our side: 

1) Auditability: Our security team needs immutable audit logs showing access to raw request data (even if redacted) for 180 days as part of our audit program. Is there a mechanism to export redaction proofs or access logs that meet SOC2/AICPA standards?
2) Tokenization: Can you confirm whether the KV cache used for latency improvements ever stores unredacted user content? We need guarantees that the cache also enforces the same PII redaction and TTLs.
3) Procurement asked about liability language — they'll want the opt-out on model training codified in the DPA and a simple addendum to the main SaaS agreement.

Alex and I prefer the 60-day retention if redaction proofs are available; otherwise we may ask for 14 days. Please have Rafael and Lauren weigh in so we can confirm for procurement.

Thanks,
Elena Morales
Head of Platform Security | CortexTech
elena.morales@cortextech.com

Hi Elena / Alex — quick technical replies inline: 

1) Auditability: Redwood can emit an export package with redaction checksums and an access audit trail that shows which internal Redwood users accessed the tenant logs, timestamps, and operation IDs. That package is stored for 180 days upon request (export only) and is signed with our internal artifact signature. We do not keep the raw unredacted payload after the configured retention window.

2) KV cache: The inference KV cache is configured per-tenant. We do not write raw unredacted customer text to disk in the cache; all KV entries are derived from tokenized, normalized representations. For customers that opt out of any model learning, we disable cross-tenant cache sharing and enforce a per-tenant TTL that defaults to 1 hour (configurable down to 0). source.subject: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — batching & quantization guardrails
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kristin Hayes <kristin.hayes@lumenai.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — batching & quantization guardrails

Hi Kristin —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Attached is a concise commercial proposal and a short technical appendix that outlines recommended batching/cache configuration and a path for safe quantization for the models in your pipeline. High-level summary: 

- Term: 12-month commercial commitment (pilot-friendly staging available)
- Pricing: tiered discounts based on committed monthly token floor and deployment type (hosted vs Dedicated)
- Performance levers: batching multiplier and KV-cache retention tiers that materially lower per-token cost when used as recommended
- Quantization: recommended int8 conversion for our medium-latency models with estimated 20–30% per-token savings; we recommend a focused A/B on a small production shadow first to validate quality.

Attachments: LumenAI_Optimize_Pricing_Proposal_20270501.pdf, technical-appendix-quant-batching-v1.pdf, SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf

Asks / next steps: 
1) Can procurement confirm whether a 6-month pilot with a token floor is acceptable to qualify for the pilot discount?
2) Would your infra/ML lead be available for a 30–45 minute tech sync next week to review the Appendix?
3) Any procurement constraints (PO cadence, required addenda) we should factor into the DocuSign package?

I copied Kimberly for contract/redline support. Happy to revise commercial bullets based on your constraints.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Sr. Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0142

--
Attachments referenced: LumenAI_Optimize_Pricing_Proposal_20270501.pdf, technical-appendix-quant-batching-v1.pdf, SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf

From: Kristin Hayes <kristin.hayes@lumenai.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: LumenAI Procurement <procurement@lumenai.com>
Date: 2027-05-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — batching & quantization guardrails

Rafael,

Thanks for the quick follow-up and the materials — useful. A few clarifying questions from our side before we loop procurement formally: 

- "Batching multiplier": how do you measure average batch size for the discount trigger? Is this computed across the tenant or at route-level? We need deterministic reporting for our finance team.
- KV-cache retention tiers: the Appendix references 6/12/24 hour bands. Do longer retention bands require dedicated capacity? What are the failure modes if cache hits drop?
- Quantization and quality: the estimated 20–30% token savings looks attractive, but what guardrails exist to detect regressions in generation quality? Will we get a pre- and post-conversion eval on our owned prompt set?
- Pilot credits: what's the minimum token floor to qualify for the pilot discount and is there a clear rollback path if early production results are poor?
- SOC2: please send the attestation (we see SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf but need the full audit point of contact). Also share SSO and data-retention docs for our security team.

Procurement (cc'd) — we prefer a short addendum to cover the pilot acceptance criteria + quality guardrails rather than a broad price protection clause.

Can we do a tech sync on Tue 5/4 at 10am PT? If so, please confirm who from your side will attend.

Best,
Kristin Hayes
Head of Platform Partnerships, LumenAI
kristin.hayes@lumenai.com

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-02T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Re: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — strategic response & negotiation plan

Kim, Neha — forwarding LumenAI's clarifying questions (below). Procurement has already been cc'd. Quick thoughts on negotiation posture: 

1) Measurement: we should compute "avg batch size" at tenant-level but publish both tenant and route-level metrics in the Console. That satisfies finance determinism.

2) Cache tiers: default offering (hosted) supports up to 6hr retention without Dedicated. 12/24hr bands are recommended for Dedicated deployments or when the KV cache is pinned to a dedicated pool; we should call this out explicitly. If cache hits drop we fall back to lower batch efficiency; we should provide automatic telemetry-driven alerts and a fallback to smaller batch SLO to prevent regressions.

3) Quantization: propose we treat quantization as an optional professional services engagement: one-time conversion + evaluation to your prompt-set. That lets us manage quality and rollbacks; suggested price $8,500 for the conversion and an explicit A/B evaluation plan with checkpoints.

4) Pilot credit / rollback: we can offer a 6-month pilot with a modest token floor (200M tokens total over pilot) to qualify for the pilot discount. Include a quality acceptance clause: if F1 on agreed eval drops > 2% we revert to baseline pricing for the subsequent month until remediation is complete. 

Suggested commercial counter to present to procurement: 
- Hosted: 12% off list for the pilot period (after acceptance becomes standard list with committed min).
- Dedicated: 18% off list.
- Onboarding: 2 months free (credits) for implementation/ops work.
- Batch multiplier: if tenant-level avg batch size >= 24 during pilot month, apply a 3% rebate to that month’s invoice (validated via Console).
- Quantization: optional PS at $8,500 (includes conversion, eval, rollback plan).

I recommend we keep the quality guardrail language tight and measurable (agreed eval set + thresholds). Kimberly, if you're good with the numbers above I can incorporate into a redline and send DocuSign.

Thanks,
Rafael

> On May 1, 2027, Kristin Hayes <kristin.hayes@lumenai.com> wrote:
> > Rafael,
> > 
> > Thanks for the quick follow-up and the materials — useful. A few clarifying questions from our side...

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-02T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Re: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — counteroffer & redlines

Rafael / team —

I like the structure. A few suggested concessions to present that are still bounded and align with our playbook: 

- Hosted: 12% off list for the pilot (confirm).
- Dedicated: 18% off list.
- Onboarding credits: 2 months free (consume within first 90 days).
- Batch multiplier: publish tenant and route metrics in Console; a 3% rebate is okay if tenant avg batch >= 24.
- Quality guardrail: explicit eval suite, rollback if >2% F1 regression on agreed prompt set; remediation plan with a 2-week SLA to restore baseline performance.
- Quantization: keep as optional PS at $8,500; we avoid embedding permanent discounts tied to technical choices.

Operational asks for the DocuSign package:
1) Add a short "Quality Evaluation Addendum" (1 page) that cites the eval dataset id (we'll provide an internal id) and the measurement window.
2) Include SOC2 & SSO attachments (SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf is fine as a stub here).
3) Add a one-sentence data-retention note (default 30 days logs; extended available by request).

If Rafael is comfortable, update the proposal PDF to reflect the above and send to Kristin + procurement for signature. I'll prep the addendum template and a DocuSign wrapper.

- Kimberly
VP, Commercial Operations
kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kristin Hayes <kristin.hayes@lumenai.com>
Cc: LumenAI Procurement <procurement@lumenai.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-03T15:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — batching & quantization guardrails

Kristin —

Really appreciate the quick review. Per internal discussion (cc Kimberly for redlines), we can confirm the updated terms below: 

Commercial summary (pilot period):
- Hosted API: 12% off list during pilot
- Dedicated: 18% off list during pilot
- Pilot minimum: 200M tokens over 6 months to qualify for the pilot discounts
- Onboarding credits: 2 months of credits for integration and ops (to be consumed within first 90 days)
- Batch multiplier rebate: tenant-level avg batch size >= 24 in a billing month triggers a 3% rebate applied to that month
- KV-cache: default retention 6 hours for hosted; 12/24hr bands recommended for Dedicated (documented in Appendix)
- Quantization: optional professional services conversion at $8,500 (includes pre/post eval and rollback plan). Not embedded as a permanent discount.
- Quality guardrail: if F1 on agreed eval set drops >2% we revert to baseline pricing for the next billing period until a remediation plan is executed (remediation target: 2 weeks).

Attachments: LumenAI_Optimize_Pricing_Proposal_20270501.pdf (updated), technical-appendix-quant-batching-v1.pdf, SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf
DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/Signing/?s=abc123 (we'll send the addendum + order form; procurement can sign online)

Next steps: 
1) Please confirm procurement is OK with the pilot token floor (200M) and the quality guardrail phrasing.
2) If you confirm, I'll send the DocuSign package today and we can schedule the tech kickoff for Tue 5/11 10am PT (Kimberly and Priyom will join from our side).

Thanks — looking forward to moving this to signature.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Sr. Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0142

--
This thread contains internal coordination and external negotiation; attachments referenced are the updated pricing PDF and technical appendix.

- Performance levers: batching multiplier and KV-cache retention tiers that materially lower per-token cost when used as recommended
- Quantization: recommended int8 conversion for our medium-latency models with estimated 20–30% per-token savings; we recommend a focused A/B on a small production shadow first to validate quality.

Attachments: LumenAI_Optimize_Pricing_Proposal_20270501.pdf, technical-appendix-quant-batching-v1.pdf, SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf

Asks / next steps: 
1) Can procurement confirm whether a 6-month pilot with a token floor is acceptable to qualify for the pilot discount?
2) Would your infra/ML lead be available for a 30–45 minute tech sync next week to review the Appendix?
3) Any procurement constraints (PO cadence, required addenda) we should factor into the DocuSign package?

I copied Kimberly for contract/redline support. Happy to revise commercial bullets based on your constraints.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Sr. Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0142

--
Attachments referenced: LumenAI_Optimize_Pricing_Proposal_20270501.pdf, technical-appendix-quant-batching-v1.pdf, SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf

From: Kristin Hayes <kristin.hayes@lumenai.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: LumenAI Procurement <procurement@lumenai.com>
Date: 2027-05-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — batching & quantization guardrails

Rafael,

Thanks for the quick follow-up and the materials — useful. A few clarifying questions from our side before we loop procurement formally: 

- \"Batching multiplier\": how do you measure average batch size for the discount trigger? Is this computed across the tenant or at route-level? We need deterministic reporting for our finance team.
- KV-cache retention tiers: the Appendix references 6/12/24 hour bands. Do longer retention bands require dedicated capacity? What are the failure modes if cache hits drop?
- Quantization and quality: the estimated 20–30% token savings looks attractive, but what guardrails exist to detect regressions in generation quality? Will we get a pre- and post-conversion eval on our owned prompt set?
- Pilot credits: what's the minimum token floor to qualify for the pilot discount and is there a clear rollback path if early production results are poor?
- SOC2: please send the attestation (we see SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf but need the full audit point of contact). Also share SSO and data-retention docs for our security team.

Procurement (cc'd) — we prefer a short addendum to cover the pilot acceptance criteria + quality guardrails rather than a broad price protection clause.

Can we do a tech sync on Tue 5/4 at 10am PT? If so, please confirm who from your side will attend.

Best,
Kristin Hayes
Head of Platform Partnerships, LumenAI
kristin.hayes@lumenai.com


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-02T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Re: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — strategic response & negotiation plan

Kim, Neha — forwarding LumenAI's clarifying questions (below). Procurement has already been cc'd. Quick thoughts on negotiation posture: 

1) Measurement: we should compute \"avg batch size\" at tenant-level but publish both tenant and route-level metrics in the Console. That satisfies finance determinism.

2) Cache tiers: default offering (hosted) supports up to 6hr retention without Dedicated. 12/24hr bands are recommended for Dedicated deployments or when the KV cache is pinned to a dedicated pool; we should call this out explicitly. If cache hits drop we fall back to lower batch efficiency; we should provide automatic telemetry-driven alerts and a fallback to smaller batch SLO to prevent regressions.

3) Quantization: propose we treat quantization as an optional professional services engagement: one-time conversion + evaluation to your prompt-set. That lets us manage quality and rollbacks; suggested price $8,500 for the conversion and an explicit A/B evaluation plan with checkpoints.

4) Pilot credit / rollback: we can offer a 6-month pilot with a modest token floor (200M tokens total over pilot) to qualify for the pilot discount. Include a quality acceptance clause: if F1 on agreed eval drops > 2% we revert to baseline pricing for the subsequent month until remediation is complete. 

Suggested commercial counter to present to procurement: 
- Hosted: 12% off list for the pilot period (after acceptance becomes standard list with committed min).
- Dedicated: 18% off list.
- Onboarding: 2 months free (credits) for implementation/ops work.
- Batch multiplier: if tenant-level avg batch size >= 24 during pilot month, apply a 3% rebate to that month’s invoice (validated via Console).
- Quantization: optional PS at $8,500 (includes conversion, eval, rollback plan).

I recommend we keep the quality guardrail language tight and measurable (agreed eval set + thresholds). Kimberly, if you're good with the numbers above I can incorporate into a redline and send DocuSign.

Thanks,
Rafael

> On May 1, 2027, Kristin Hayes <kristin.hayes@lumenai.com> wrote:
> > Rafael,
> > 
> > Thanks for the quick follow-up and the materials — useful. A few clarifying questions from our side...


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-02T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Re: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — counteroffer & redlines

Rafael / team —

I like the structure. A few suggested concessions to present that are still bounded and align with our playbook: 

- Hosted: 12% off list for the pilot (confirm).
- Dedicated: 18% off list.
- Onboarding credits: 2 months free (consume within first 90 days).
- Batch multiplier: publish tenant and route metrics in Console; a 3% rebate is okay if tenant avg batch >= 24.
- Quality guardrail: explicit eval suite, rollback if >2% F1 regression on agreed prompt set; remediation plan with a 2-week SLA to restore baseline performance.
- Quantization: keep as optional PS at $8,500; we avoid embedding permanent discounts tied to technical choices.

Operational asks for the DocuSign package:
1) Add a short "Quality Evaluation Addendum" (1 page) that cites the eval dataset id (we'll provide an internal id) and the measurement window.
2) Include SOC2 & SSO attachments (SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf is fine as a stub here).
3) Add a one-sentence data-retention note (default 30 days logs; extended available by request).

If Rafael is comfortable, update the proposal PDF to reflect the above and send to Kristin + procurement for signature. I'll prep the addendum template and a DocuSign wrapper.

- Kimberly
VP, Commercial Operations
kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kristin Hayes <kristin.hayes@lumenai.com>
Cc: LumenAI Procurement <procurement@lumenai.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-05-03T15:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit commercial update — batching & quantization guardrails

Kristin —

Really appreciate the quick review. Per internal discussion (cc Kimberly for redlines), we can confirm the updated terms below: 

Commercial summary (pilot period):
- Hosted API: 12% off list during pilot
- Dedicated: 18% off list during pilot
- Pilot minimum: 200M tokens over 6 months to qualify for the pilot discounts
- Onboarding credits: 2 months of credits for integration and ops (to be consumed within first 90 days)
- Batch multiplier rebate: tenant-level avg batch size >= 24 in a billing month triggers a 3% rebate applied to that month
- KV-cache: default retention 6 hours for hosted; 12/24hr bands recommended for Dedicated (documented in Appendix)
- Quantization: optional professional services conversion at $8,500 (includes pre/post eval and rollback plan). Not embedded as a permanent discount.
- Quality guardrail: if F1 on agreed eval set drops >2% we revert to baseline pricing for the next billing period until a remediation plan is executed (remediation target: 2 weeks).

Attachments: LumenAI_Optimize_Pricing_Proposal_20270501.pdf (updated), technical-appendix-quant-batching-v1.pdf, SOC2-snapshot-2026.pdf
DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/Signing/?s=abc123 (we'll send the addendum + order form; procurement can sign online)

Next steps: 
1) Please confirm procurement is OK with the pilot token floor (200M) and the quality guardrail phrasing.
2) If you confirm, I'll send the DocuSign package today and we can schedule the tech kickoff for Tue 5/11 10am PT (Kimberly and Priyom will join from our side).

Thanks — looking forward to moving this to signature.

Best,
Rafael Mendes
Sr. Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
rafael_mendes@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0142

--
This thread contains internal coordination and external negotiation; attachments referenced are the updated pricing PDF and technical appendix.

source.subject: Re: HelioHealth Security Questionnaire — data residency & retention
messages:
From: Laura Chen <security@heliohealth.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: HelioHealth Legal <legal@heliohealth.com>
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: HelioHealth Security Questionnaire — Data Residency & Retention

Hi Priya,

Thanks for taking this. Attached is our standard security questionnaire focused on data residency, retention, and deletion for hosted APIs. Our legal and security teams would like detailed answers so we can complete our vendor assessment. Key items for us: where data is stored, default retention windows for request/response logs and embeddings, deletion SLAs (including backups), and any options for customer-controlled keys or geo-locational constraints.

Please see the attached (HelioHealth_SecQ_20270425.xlsx). Can you aim to return answers by EOD 30-Apr? If you need to escalate, loop in your compliance lead.

Laura Chen
Security Engineer — HelioHealth
security@heliohealth.com

Attachment: HelioHealth_SecQ_20270425.xlsx (MS Excel — 18KB)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.inference.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: HelioHealth Security Questionnaire — Data Residency & Retention

Folks — forwarding Helio's questionnaire. I started a rough draft of answers in the PDF attached below (redwood_data_retention_response_draft.txt) but want your eyes on the sections about backups and key control before I send. High-level notes:

- Hosted API default logs: we retain request/response metadata and anonymized telemetry for 30 days; sensitive PII should be redacted by the customer or we can enable opt-out logging for enterprise customers.
- Deletion SLA: remove from active systems within 72 hours; purge backups within 30 days unless legal hold applies. Rafael, you suggested 30 days for backups; confirm wording.
- BYOK: default uses Redwood-managed KMS; enterprise customers may bring their own KMS (AWS KMS / GCP CMEK) for encryption-at-rest keys used for persisted artifacts.

Please review the draft wording inline and flag anything that will cause scope creep. I plan to finalize the PDF and return to Laura by EOD 30-Apr.

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Head of Security Programs, Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.inference.com

[Quoted message]
> From: Laura Chen <security@heliohealth.com>
> To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference.com>
> Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
> Subject: HelioHealth Security Questionnaire — Data Residency & Retention
>
> (See attached Excel)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference.com>
To: Laura Chen <security@heliohealth.com>
Cc: HelioHealth Legal <legal@heliohealth.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 15:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HelioHealth Security Questionnaire — data residency & retention

Laura — thank you for the questionnaire. Attached is our formal response (redwood_data_retention_response_20270430.pdf). Summary of key points below; the PDF contains the full Q&A mapped to your spreadsheet.

Summary — Hosted API (default behavior):

1) Data residency and storage
- Hosted API traffic (requests and model outputs) is processed in the region you call (we operate multi-region endpoints). For customers without a region preference, requests are routed to the nearest available region subject to capacity.
- We do not provide per-customer tenancy by default on the hosted tier; data co-mingling is limited to metadata and ephemeral request content (see retention below).

2) Default retention windows
- Request/response logs (for debugging, abuse monitoring, quality): retained by default for 30 days. These logs include request metadata, timestamps, and the text of prompts/responses unless redaction is enabled.
- Embeddings: we do not store embeddings generated by the hosted API unless the customer explicitly opts into a storage feature (for search/indexing). If stored, retention is customer-configurable within the console and is subject to the applicable contract.
- Model telemetry and aggregated metrics: retained for 24 months for product analytics (anonymized).

3) Deletion and purge procedures (customer-initiated)
- Live systems: when a deletion request is submitted via the Console or Support ticket, records associated with the request are removed from our active serving and logging systems within 72 hours.
- Backups and snapshots: backups are retained for disaster recovery and are purged on a rolling schedule; typical purge window is 30 days. Customers can request accelerated purge; we commit to evaluating and executing accelerated purge requests within a 5 business-day SLA where technically feasible.
- Legal hold: data subject to a legal hold or audit may be retained longer; we will notify the customer if a legal hold supersedes a purge request.

4) Data handling controls and encryption
- In transit: all traffic to Redwood endpoints is TLS 1.2+ with strong ciphers.
- At rest: persisted artifacts (backups, stored embeddings, logs retained beyond ephemeral caching) are encrypted at rest by default using Redwood-managed KMS keys.
- BYOK: enterprise customers may provide their own KMS keys (AWS KMS, GCP CMEK) for persisted storage encryption; this is available as an add-on and is documented in our Enterprise onboarding guide.

5) Customer options to minimize retention
- Logging opt-out: enterprise accounts may enable a "data_processing: minimal" header that prevents storage of request/response payloads for logging and analytics (we still retain minimal telemetry for rate-limiting/abuse prevention).
- Redaction tools: we provide client-side helpers and guidance for prompt redaction; we recommend removing PII before sending to hosted endpoints if retention is a concern.

6) Auditability and compliance
- SOC 2: Redwood maintains a SOC 2 Type II attestation; reports are available under NDA.
- Audit logs: we provide customer-facing audit trails for administrative actions in the Console.

Attachments: redwood_data_retention_response_20270430.pdf (contains full table-mapped answers), summary CSV with exact row mappings.

If you'd like a DocuSign DPA with the BYOK addendum included, we can prepare that and send a link. Happy to hop on a short call if any item needs clarification.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Head of Security Programs, Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.inference.com
Attachment: redwood_data_retention_response_20270430.pdf (PDF — 45KB)

From: Laura Chen <security@heliohealth.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: HelioHealth Legal <legal@heliohealth.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 17:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HelioHealth Security Questionnaire — data residency & retention

Priya — thank you for the detailed responses and the PDF. This covers most of what we need. Two quick follow-ups: (1) can you confirm the accelerated purge path and the operational steps to request it (ticket vs. Console checkbox)? (2) please send the DPA/DocuSign link that includes the BYOK addendum; legal will review.

If you can send the DocuSign link and a short note on the accelerated purge workflow, we'll mark this vendor as green pending DPA execution.

Thanks,
Laura Chen
Security Engineer — HelioHealth
security@heliohealth.com

Attachment: none

To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: HelioHealth Legal <legal@heliohealth.com>

Two quick follow-ups: (1) can you confirm the accelerated purge path and the operational steps to request it (ticket vs. Console checkbox)? (2) please send the DPA/DocuSign link that includes the BYOK addendum; legal will review.

If you can send the DocuSign link and a short note on the accelerated purge workflow, we'll mark this vendor as green pending DPA execution.

source.subject: Confirmation: physical separation, regional backups, and retention for Dedicated deployment
source.messages:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acme-health.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated deployment - physical separation & backup residency questions

Hi Mina,

Thanks for the demo last week. Our compliance team has a couple of follow-ups before we finish the security review for the Dedicated deployment:

1) Can you confirm whether customer data (including backups, snapshots, and any asynchronous replicas) for a pinned Dedicated pool remains physically located in the same region we select? In particular: are nightly snapshots stored in-region or replicated out-of-region by default?

2) For geo-failure DR, do you provide cross-region replication only as an opt-in feature? We need to understand whether enabling dedicated region pinning prevents any unintended replication.

3) Requested evidence: a short diagram of data flow for Dedicated (where runtime compute, backups, and logs live) and an SOP describing deletion of data and snapshots after a customer erasure request.

Appreciate a quick confirmation and any artifacts you can share. Our target is to close this by EOD Thursday.

Thanks,
Lena Ortiz
Head of Security Programs
Acme Health

------
From: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acme-health.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 12:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated deployment - physical separation & backup residency questions

Lena — thanks for the clear list. Short answers up front:
- Customer data and associated backups/snapshots for a region-pinned Dedicated pool are kept in-region by default.
- Cross-region replication is opt-in for Dedicated; it is not enabled unless the customer requests DR replication and signs the updated config/terms.
- I'll attach a simplified dataflow diagram and our SOP for deletion; see attachments.

Details below and I've looped in Dev for technical confirmation on snapshots and Rafe (ops) for wipe timelines.

Attachments: region-dataflow.svg (diagram), sop-dedicated-wipe.pdf (SOP excerpt)

Best,
Mina
Mina Haddad
Senior Customer Security Manager
Redwood Inference

------
From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acme-health.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 14:32:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated deployment - physical separation & backup residency questions

Thanks for the ping, Mina / Lena. Adding the engineering detail below.

1) Snapshots/backups: For Dedicated pools we take incremental snapshots of instance volumes nightly. By default those snapshot objects are stored in the same cloud region as the compute (same region/availability zone boundary) and are encrypted at rest with either Redwood-managed keys or customer-managed KMS keys when requested.

2) Replication: Cross-region replication is strictly opt-in. We offer a DR add-on where snapshots are replicated to a secondary region you define. That process requires explicit configuration and an updated agreement. No replication is done automatically.

3) Logs/metrics: Operational logs (traces, metrics) for a Dedicated instance can be routed to the customer-owned logging endpoint inside the pinned VPC; if routed to Redwood-managed stacks we keep them in-region and limit retention by config.

4) Deletion and retained copies: When a customer issues an erasure request we trigger immediate deletion of live data and schedule snapshot purging. Snapshots are marked for deletion and removed from active storage within 24 hours; underlying object store reclamation follows the cloud provider lifecycle and typically completes within 30 days. For customers requiring shorter windows we offer expedited deletion (engineering wipe) with a documented process; that is done under a change window and we provide a signed attestation when complete.

5) Evidence artifacts: The attached diagram and SOP are representative. For SOC2 evidence we can provide audit logs showing snapshot deletion events, and a signed wipe attestation.

If you need the exact cloud object IDs and a sample purge log for a test erasure, we can produce that within 48 hours.

—Dev
Dev Patel
Principal Infra Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

------
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acme-health.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 09:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated deployment - physical separation & backup residency questions

Lena — operational confirmation from my side:

- Physical isolation: Dedicated runs on tenant-isolated GPU pools. We do not colocate tenant compute on the same host as other customers in a pinned pool. Networking and storage access are scoped to the VPC/project we provision.

- Backups: Nightly snapshots are retained in-region. We keep a 30-day rolling retention by default; longer retention is configurable. If you request a complete erasure, we run the wipe playbook (SOP attached) which includes: snapshot marking, metadata deletion, object-store lifecycle accelerated deletion where available, and an integrity check. After the wipe we provide a signed attestation (PDF) and a snippet from the audit log (object IDs + timestamps).

- Cold copies: Any exported cold copies (manual exports for analytics or support) are subject to a documented export approval flow and are stored according to the region you specify. We log all exports and can include those entries in the attestation.

Let me know if Acme requires a shorter snapshot reclamation SLA (e.g., 7 days) and we'll raise an SRE runbook for an expedited wipe option.

Rafael Mendes
Head of Site Reliability
Redwood Inference

------
From: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acme-health.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 28 Apr 2027 15:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated deployment - physical separation & backup residency questions

Lena — following up to confirm the deliverables and next steps:

- Confirmed: backups/snapshots remain in-region for a region-pinned Dedicated pool; cross-region replication is opt-in.
- Confirmed: default retention is 30 days for snapshots; expedited reclamation (7-day or accelerated) is available under an SLA addendum.
- Evidence bundle: attached diagram (region-dataflow.svg), SOP excerpt (sop-dedicated-wipe.pdf).
- Action: We will produce a sample purge log and a signed wipe attestation within 48 hours after you sign the access request form. Link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1AcmeComplianceDocs

If that aligns with Acme's requirements I'll schedule a quick QA call for Friday to walk through the attestation.

Regards,
Mina Haddad
Senior Customer Security Manager
Redwood Inference

> Thanks for the demo last week. Our compliance team has a couple of follow-ups before we finish the security review for the Dedicated deployment:
>

>
> 2) For geo-failure DR, do you provide cross-region replication only as an opt-in feature? We need to understand whether enabling dedicated region pinning prevents any unintended replication.
>
> 3) Requested evidence: a short diagram of data flow for Dedicated (where runtime compute, backups, and logs live) and an SOP describing deletion of data and snapshots after a customer erasure request.

> Appreciate a quick confirmation and any artifacts you can share. Our target is to close this by EOD Thursday.

source.subject: MAP: streaming TTFT validation and close timeline (Vertex Health)
messages:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Claire Zhou <claire.zhou@vertexhealth.ai>, procurement@vertexhealth.ai
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: streaming TTFT validation and close timeline (Vertex Health)

Claire —

Thanks again for the call earlier. Per our discussion I put together a short mutual action plan (attached) that covers the performance validation step we agreed on before contract signature. High level: we run a focused TTFT + streaming-latency benchmark against two model variants, capture p50/p95/p99 for TTFT and streaming latency across 3 concurrency bands, and confirm that real-app latency meets your UX SLOs.

Key ask: can you confirm the proposed test window (Apr 21–22) and provide the list of sample prompts or data slices you want included? If procurement needs a PO timeline, cc'd procurement can flag any timing constraints.

Quick summary of the benchmarking plan (see attachment "vertex_map_v3.xlsx"):
- Scope: Chat/text generation for triage workflows (short prompts ~8–40 tokens and medium prompts ~40–200 tokens)
- Model variants: redwood/gpt-large-fp16 (baseline) and redwood/gpt-medium-4q (quantized candidate)
- Metrics: TTFT (time to first token streaming), streaming token latency (per-token), throughput (tokens/sec), p50/p95/p99, and error rate
- Concurrency bands: 4, 16, 64 concurrent sessions (we can add 128 if you want a peak stress run)
- Target acceptance criteria: p95 TTFT <= 450ms for short prompts; p99 TTFT <= 900ms; sustained token throughput > 3k tokens/s at 16 concurrency for the large model
- Test harness: Locust-based driver configured to 90% streaming, 10% non-stream; KV-cache disabled to capture cold-path TTFT for first-turn scenarios

Attachments: vertex_map_v3.xlsx (MAP + timeline), loadtest-config.yaml (initial configs).

Proposed next steps:
1) Confirm test window and sample prompts by EOD today
2) We run benchmarks Apr 21 morning (pre-seeding) and Apr 22 produce results and a short summary
3) If results meet criteria, we add a short production rollout step in the MAP (Dedicated capacity reservation)

If this plan looks good, I’ll lock the test harness and share the load test S3 bucket before the run.

—Naomi
Naomi Feldman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: vertex_map_v3.xlsx (spreadsheet), loadtest-config.yaml (text)

From: Claire Zhou <claire.zhou@vertexhealth.ai>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@vertexhealth.ai
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: streaming TTFT validation and close timeline (Vertex Health)

Thanks Naomi — appreciate the clear plan. A few quick items from our side:

- Procurement: we need results and an estimate for monthly cost delta if we move the quantized candidate to Dedicated vs using Hosted. I’ve looped in procurement for timeline constraints.
- Data slices: attached is a short list (I’ll paste a pair below) — please include both short triage prompts and longer summary prompts.
  * "Patient presents with chest pain, list initial triage questions" (~12 tokens)
  * "Summarize the following chart and suggest next steps for cardiology referral" (~120 tokens)
- Acceptance criteria: those TTFT numbers look reasonable. We also need to ensure p95 streaming token latency for medium prompts is < 1.2s.

Can we schedule a 30-min kickoff tomorrow at 10am PT for test-run coordination? Confirm and we’ll send a calendar invite.

—Claire
Claire Zhou
Head of Clinical Products, Vertex Health
claire.zhou@vertexhealth.ai

On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 09:12 Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Claire —
>
> Thanks again for the call earlier. Per our discussion I put together a short mutual action plan (attached) that covers the performance validation step we agreed on before contract signature...

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Claire Zhou <claire.zhou@vertexhealth.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@vertexhealth.ai
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 08:37:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: streaming TTFT validation and close timeline (Vertex Health)

Claire — thanks, 10am PT tomorrow works; I’ll send calendar. A couple of clarifications to make the test and acceptance criteria unambiguous: 

Test harness & config highlights (attached loadtest-config.yaml):
- Driver: Locust + custom streaming client that measures TTFT (first byte of the stream) and per-token latency
- Traffic mix: 70% short prompts (8–40 tokens), 30% medium prompts (40–200 tokens)
- Cache state: two runs per prompt — cold-first-turn to measure TTFT, and warm run to measure sustained token latency
- Concurrency bands: 4 / 16 / 64 for steady-state; 64->128 ramp to simulate burst

Evaluation definitions (for clarity):
- TTFT: wall-clock from request start to first streamed token delivered to client (includes server-side tokenization + kernel dispatch)
- Streaming per-token latency: time between consecutive tokens arriving at client (useful for jitter metrics)
- Throughput: aggregate tokens/sec across all sessions during sustained window (60s windows)

Acceptance thresholds we’ll report against (we recommend these initial gates):
- Short prompts: p50 TTFT <= 120ms, p95 <= 450ms, p99 <= 900ms
- Medium prompts: p50 TTFT <= 200ms, p95 <= 700ms, p99 <= 1.2s
- Streaming token p95 (medium prompts) <= 200ms per token
If any gate fails we’ll do targeted root-cause runs (kernel-level profiling + batch-size sweep).
I’ve attached loadtest-config.yaml for your ops team; Rafael will run the job from our Dedicated cluster unless you prefer Hosted for a like-for-like comparison.
—Naomi

> Thanks Naomi — appreciate the clear plan....

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 14:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: internal: quantization baseline question

Naomi / Stephanie — quick internal note before tomorrow’s kickoff:

- For the initial run I recommend we include both the quantized candidate (gpt-medium-4q) and the fp16 baseline (gpt-large-fp16). The quantized run should use the same kernel path we validated in our lab (4-bit per-channel). We should record kernel-level counters (dispatch times, GEMM durations) for the p99 investigation.
- Also confirm if Vertex wants a Dedicated run expense estimate. If they do, I’ll prepare a short appendix in the MAP with projected monthly cost delta for Dedicated vs Hosted at the observed throughput.

I’ll bring the load generator up in the QA region and seed the prompt set tonight.

—Rafael
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Claire Zhou <claire.zhou@vertexhealth.ai>, procurement@vertexhealth.ai
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 17:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Final MAP update + benchmark results (TTFT + streaming) — next steps

Claire / procurement — please find the run summary below and the full CSV attached (ttft_stream_results_20270422.csv). Rafael executed the runs from our QA cluster; we included both model variants you requested.

Summary (short prompts / medium prompts) — consolidated p50 / p95 / p99 TTFT and streaming p95 token latency at the 16-concurrency band (most representative):
- gpt-large-fp16 (baseline):
  * Short prompts TTFT: p50=95ms, p95=380ms, p99=760ms
  * Medium prompts TTFT: p50=170ms, p95=620ms, p99=1120ms
  * Streaming token p95 (medium): 185ms
- gpt-medium-4q (quantized candidate):
  * Short prompts TTFT: p50=120ms, p95=460ms, p99=980ms
  * Medium prompts TTFT: p50=210ms, p95=840ms, p99=1500ms
  * Streaming token p95 (medium): 240ms

Throughput at 16 concurrency (tokens/sec):
- gpt-large-fp16: sustained 3.4k tokens/sec
- gpt-medium-4q: sustained 3.8k tokens/sec (higher tokens/sec but increased tail TTFT on cold first-turns)

Observations & next steps:
- Baseline meets the short-prompt TTFT gates comfortably; medium prompt tail (p99) for baseline slightly above the 1.1s soft threshold but within an acceptable margin for a pilot.
- Quantized candidate shows improved sustained throughput but has degraded p99 TTFT on cold first-turns — likely due to an extra kernel dispatch path on the quantized runtime. We recommend a targeted kernel dispatch profile and a canary that routes only warm-session traffic to quantized for the initial rollout.
- Rafael will generate a short appendix with a cost delta for Dedicated vs Hosted at observed throughput; we’ll include that in the MAP workbook.

Action items:
1) Vertex: confirm whether you want us to run a Dedicated vs Hosted comparison (we’ll need permission to run a Hosted job in your test org or confirm a Dedicated trial)
2) Vertex procurement: confirm PO timing for a pilot (30–60 day trial) so we can schedule Dedicated capacity if needed
3) Redwood (Rafael): follow-up kernel profiling run to isolate p99 TTFT spikes on gpt-medium-4q

If you’re happy, we can move to a short pilot contract and then schedule a production rollout canary for warm-session quantized routing.

Thanks all — I’ll update the MAP spreadsheet with the numbers and send the dedicated cost appendix when Rafael has it ready.

—Naomi
Attachments: ttft_stream_results_20270422.csv

Subject: MAP: streaming TTFT validation and close timeline (Vertex Health)

Claire —

Thanks again for the call earlier. Per our discussion I put together a short mutual action plan (attached) that covers the performance validation step we agreed on before contract signature. High level: we run a focused TTFT + streaming-latency benchmark against two model variants, capture p50/p95/p99 for TTFT and streaming latency across 3 concurrency bands, and confirm that real-app latency meets your UX SLOs.

Key ask: can you confirm the proposed test window (Apr 21–22) and provide the list of sample prompts or data slices you want included? If procurement needs a PO timeline, cc'd procurement can flag any timing constraints.

Quick summary of the benchmarking plan (see attachment \"vertex_map_v3.xlsx\"):
- Scope: Chat/text generation for triage workflows (short prompts ~8–40 tokens and medium prompts ~40–200 tokens)
- Model variants: redwood/gpt-large-fp16 (baseline) and redwood/gpt-medium-4q (quantized candidate)
- Metrics: TTFT (time to first token streaming), streaming token latency (per-token), throughput (tokens/sec), p50/p95/p99, and error rate
- Concurrency bands: 4, 16, 64 concurrent sessions (we can add 128 if you want a peak stress run)
- Target acceptance criteria: p95 TTFT <= 450ms for short prompts; p99 TTFT <= 900ms; sustained token throughput > 3k tokens/s at 16 concurrency for the large model
- Test harness: Locust-based driver configured to 90% streaming, 10% non-stream; KV-cache disabled to capture cold-path TTFT for first-turn scenarios

Attachments: vertex_map_v3.xlsx (MAP + timeline), loadtest-config.yaml (initial configs).

Proposed next steps:
1) Confirm test window and sample prompts by EOD today
2) We run benchmarks Apr 21 morning (pre-seeding) and Apr 22 produce results and a short summary
3) If results meet criteria, we add a short production rollout step in the MAP (Dedicated capacity reservation)

If this plan looks good, I’ll lock the test harness and share the load test S3 bucket before the run.

—Naomi

source.subject: Final approval cadence + signature routing for ACME MAP and SOW
messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Mike Jansen <mike.jansen@acme.com>, Leah Summers <leah.summers@acme.com>
Cc: Acme Procurement <procurement@acme.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Apr 2027 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Proposed final approval cadence & MAP (v3)

Hi Mike / Leah —

Thanks again for the time earlier. Attached is the latest MAP (ACME_MAP_v3.pdf) and the updated SOW (SOW_v2.docx) that reflect the scoped pilot and the pricing table we discussed. To keep the launch timeline tight, here’s the handoff/signature cadence we propose: 

- Apr 20: DocuSign routing to authorized signers (procurement + legal + authorized exec)
- Apr 22: Final signature complete (buffer for countersignature)
- May 03: Onboarding kickoff (first integration sprint)

Can you confirm ACME’s signer list and any blackout dates between Apr 20-22? If procurement needs a routing diagram or a specific signer order, I’ll share a Signature_Routing.pdf.

Also flagged a small security appendix that Security asked for; Laura (cc’d on internal) will confirm we can surface the checklist to you without further gating.

Thanks —
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Account Exec | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: ACME_MAP_v3.pdf, SOW_v2.docx

From: Mike Jansen <mike.jansen@acme.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Procurement <procurement@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:06:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Proposed final approval cadence & MAP (v3)

Rafael —

Thanks for sending. MAP looks good at a high level. Quick clarifications from Procurement: 

- We have two authorized signers (CFO and Head of Cloud Ops). Preference is single DocuSign envelope but with sequential routing (CFO -> Head of Cloud Ops).
- Could you confirm who at Redwood will countersign? Our legal needs the name and title of the Redwood signer for the SOW.
- Is Apr 22 a hard deadline for start date? We’d prefer May 03, but need signed SOW no later than Apr 25 to meet internal billing cycles.

If you can share the routing PDF and the Redwood counterparty details, we’ll align on our side and confirm the envelope order today.

Best,
Mike
Director, Cloud Integrations | ACME Corp
+mike.jansen@acme.com

> [original message omitted]

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 08:34:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: ACME signature routing & timing — need approvals and counterparty info

Team — forwarding ACME’s notes. Need quick confirmations so we can lock routing today:

source.subject: MedAlign procurement sequencing & MAP alignment
messages:
From: Susan Li <susan.li@medalign.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@medalign.com>
Date: Sat, 3 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MedAlign procurement sequencing & MAP alignment

Hi Kimberly / Rafael,

Thanks again for walking us through Redwood's hosted + dedicated options this week. The executive sponsors here at MedAlign want a concise mutual action plan that ties our evaluation milestones to procurement/legal gating so we can share with finance and compliance. Two quick asks to start: 

1) Can you send a draft MAP that lays out milestone owners, key deliverables, and target sign-off dates for MSA / DPA / Order Form?
2) We have an internal procurement questionnaire and a list of initial redlines — attaching the procurement Qs now. Can you confirm whether Redwood can run MSA and DPA in parallel or if you require MSA signed first for order form work?

Attachments: medalign_procurement_qs.pdf (pdf)

Ideal target: pilot contract signed by 2027-05-21. We realize some items (privacy review) may push that — looking for clarity on dependencies and a recommended gating sequence.

Appreciate it,

Susan Li
Procurement Manager | MedAlign Health
procurement@medalign.com
(415) 555-0197

---

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference>
To: Susan Li <susan.li@medalign.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2027 10:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MedAlign procurement sequencing & MAP alignment

Hi Susan — thanks for the clear ask and for sharing the questionnaire. I put together a short MAP draft and a proposed sequencing approach (attachments). High level thoughts up front:

- We recommend running the MSA and DPA in parallel where possible; the DPA often requires the privacy team sign-off which can be the longer pole, but it doesn't block order-form commercial negotiations in our experience.
- Order Form dependencies: pricing, usage SLA, eff. date — we can finish those in parallel with legal redlines but will hold contract execution until both MSA + DPA are accepted or fallbacks agreed.
- Target timeline:
  * 2027-04-06 to 2027-04-12: Redwood to deliver MAP + redline guidance (done)
  * 2027-04-13 to 2027-04-26: Legal review (both sides) — iterative redline exchange
  * 2027-04-27 to 2027-05-10: Procurement finalization & PO prep
  * 2027-05-17: Target signature week for pilot order form

I attached: MAP-draft.xlsx (spreadsheet with owners, milestones, and fallback paths) and MSA_redline_guidance.pdf (brief guidance on clauses we flag as common negotiation points).

Grace — can you confirm onboarding / pilot acceptance checkpoints so I can add them to the MAP?

Next steps: Rafael will take point on the legal redlines and loop in outsourced counsel if needed. Susan — if the MedAlign privacy team needs additional artifact, please flag in the questionnaire and we’ll prioritize.

Thanks,

Kimberly Park
Head of Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.inference
(650) 555-0123

Attachments: MAP-draft.xlsx (xlsx), MSA_redline_guidance.pdf (pdf)

On Sat, Apr 3, 2027 at 09:12 AM Susan Li <susan.li@medalign.com> wrote:
> Hi Kimberly / Rafael,
> 
> Thanks again for walking us through Redwood's hosted + dedicated options this week. The executive sponsors here at MedAlign want a concise mutual action plan that ties our evaluation milestones to procurement/legal gating so we can share with finance and compliance. Two quick asks to start: 
> 
> 1) Can you send a draft MAP that lays out milestone owners, key deliverables, and target sign-off dates for MSA / DPA / Order Form?
> 2) We have an internal procurement questionnaire and a list of initial redlines — attaching the procurement Qs now. Can you confirm whether Redwood can run MSA and DPA in parallel or if you require MSA signed first for order form work?
> 
> Attachments: medalign_procurement_qs.pdf (pdf)
> 
> Ideal target: pilot contract signed by 2027-05-21. We realize some items (privacy review) may push that — looking for clarity on dependencies and a recommended gating sequence.
> 
> Appreciate it,
> 
> Susan Li
> Procurement Manager | MedAlign Health
> procurement@medalign.com
> (415) 555-0197
>
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference>
To: Susan Li <susan.li@medalign.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.inference>, Jim Patel <jim.patel@outsourced-legal.com>
Date: Wed, 7 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MedAlign procurement sequencing & MAP alignment

Thanks Kimberly — useful draft. A few legal items and a suggested sequencing tweak from our side after reviewing MedAlign's procurement Qs (high level):

1) Data processing addendum (DPA) — privacy team flagged that the data classification examples in your questionnaire imply PHI adjacency. If that's accurate, privacy will require an additional attestation and the DPA review will likely add ~10 business days. Because of that risk, I recommend we treat DPA as a gating item for production activation but not for pilot order formation.

2) MSA redlines — I'm attaching redline_MSA_v3.docx with comments on indemnity, liability cap carve-outs, and a proposed alternate clause for audit requests. These are standard; MedAlign can accept a mutual indemnity + $5M cap or propose a defined carve for regulatory exposures.

3) Order Form sequencing — to keep the pilot timeline we can accept an executed order form that references an MSA and DPA in negotiation, provided the order form includes an explicit fallback (timeboxed escrow of data, limited pilot scope, and escrowed funds holdback). I marked the MAP draft with suggested fallbacks in the sheet.

Action items I propose we update the MAP to:
- mark DPA as privacy-gated for production activation (not pilot)
- add 10 business days contingency to the DPA review window
- include fallback language template in the Order Form section

Attaching: redline_MSA_v3.docx (docx)

Kimberly — happy to hop on a quick call with Susan + Jim (outsourced counsel) to walk through the redlines if helpful.

-Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.inference

On Mon, Apr 5, 2027 at 10:37 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference> wrote:
> Hi Susan — thanks for the clear ask and for sharing the questionnaire. I put together a short MAP draft and a proposed sequencing approach (attachments). High level thoughts up front:
> 
> - We recommend running the MSA and DPA in parallel where possible; the DPA often requires the privacy team sign-off which can be the longer pole, but it doesn't block order-form commercial negotiations in our experience.
> - Order Form dependencies: pricing, usage SLA, eff. date — we can finish those in parallel with legal redlines but will hold contract execution until both MSA + DPA are accepted or fallbacks agreed.
> - Target timeline: ...
>
From: Susan Li <susan.li@medalign.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@medalign.com>, Jim Patel <jim.patel@outsourced-legal.com>
Date: Fri, 9 Apr 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MedAlign procurement sequencing & MAP alignment

Kimberly / Rafael — thanks for the fast turnaround and the clear MAP. We discussed Rafael's suggested DPA gating with our privacy lead; they confirm the PHI adjacency concern and are okay proceeding with an order form for a limited pilot provided the fallback language is included.

Agreed next steps: 
- MedAlign to review redline_MSA_v3 and return any last commercial comments by 2027-04-14.
- Privacy to review DPA artifacts; expected sign-off by 2027-04-28 (we'll escalate if this slips).
- Redwood to provide the fallback order form template and updated MAP with contingency windows by 2027-04-11.

I'll circulate internally and copy finance. If you can get the fallback template to me by EOD 2027-04-11 that keeps us on track to sign a pilot order form mid-May.

Thanks — Susan
Susan Li
Procurement Manager | MedAlign Health
procurement@medalign.com
(415) 555-0197

On Wed, Apr 7, 2027 at 02:22 PM Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.inference> wrote:
> Thanks Kimberly — useful draft. A few legal items and a suggested sequencing tweak from our side after reviewing MedAlign's procurement Qs (high level): ...
>
> -Rafael
> Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
>
