messages:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Dana Cole <dana.cole@greenwave.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: GW: key ops & service-account check-in

Hi Dana — great conversation today, thanks for the time. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can line up the technical deep dive next week.

Recap (what we heard)
- Primary requirement: GreenWave wants server-to-server auth that avoids static API keys in prod. Prefer a service-account + short-lived token flow.
- Rotation: you want automated rotation with auditable key usage and rotation events surfaced to your SIEM.
- Routing: calls to sensitive reranker endpoints should be routed to dedicated capacity in us-west for latency guarantees.
- Structured output/tool-calling: you plan to use tool calls for enrichment pipelines and want to ensure token scoping limits downside.

Proposed checklist / immediate asks (we'll deliver items 1–3 before the tech dive):
1) Redwood to share a one-page integration checklist (attached) and a short example of service-account JWT exchange.
2) GreenWave to share preferred rotation cadence (30/60/90 days), IP allowlist ranges, and whether you need managed STS or self-hosted exchange.
3) Schedule a 60-minute technical deep dive with your infra/security + our SRE/solutions engineer (Laura + Marissa).

Questions for you before the dive:
- Is there a legal requirement to avoid any third-party KMS?
- Will you require our audit logs (per-request token identity) to feed into your SIEM via Syslog or S3?

Next steps I recommend:
- I’ll attach the integration checklist (redwood-integration-checklist.xlsx) and an example doc for the JWT exchange.
- Laura can follow up with a suggested token exchange pattern and sample curl/SDK snippets.
- If that looks good, we’ll lock the tech dive for next week — propose Apr 5 10:00 AM PT.

Thanks again — Rafael

--
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0198

Attachment: redwood-integration-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet, 12KB)
From: Dana Cole <dana.cole@greenwave.ai>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Mike Huang <mike.huang@greenwave.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 09:18:15 -0700
Subject: Re: GW: key ops & service-account check-in

Thanks Rafael — this is helpful. A few clarifications from our side:

- We do prefer service accounts over long-lived API keys. Our security team wants short-lived tokens (<= 1 hour) issued by a trusted STS endpoint.
- Rotation cadence: we're targeting automated rotation every 30 days for long-lived credentials, but for access tokens we want ephemeral tokens with rotation handled by the issuing STS.
- KMS: we use a managed KMS (AWS KMS) and can't export keys, so any flow that requires key export is a non-starter. We'll need Redwood to integrate with our KMS via WK/STS or support token signing without key import.
- Audit logs: we need per-request identity (service-account id) in the access logs and a weekly export to an S3 bucket.

Also — we'd like to surface a couple of integration concerns for the reranker: will the routing to dedicated capacity require separate credentials or can we use scoped tokens that only permit rerank requests?

We can do Apr 5 10:00 AM PT for the deep dive. Can you add Mike (head of infra) on the invite; he'll handle the IP ranges and KMS constraints.

On Tue, Mar 29, 2027 at 2:03 PM Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Dana — great conversation today, thanks for the time. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can line up the technical deep dive next week.
> 
> [snip - checklist attached]

Best,
Dana

Dana Cole
VP of Platform
GreenWave AI
dana.cole@greenwave.ai | (617) 555-0124
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Dana Cole <dana.cole@greenwave.ai>, Mike Huang <mike.huang@greenwave.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 11:02:07 -0700
Subject: Re: GW: key ops & service-account check-in (technical follow-up)

Hi Dana / Mike — Laura here from Solutions Engineering. Thanks for the clear constraints. Below is a pragmatic pattern that matches your requirements and examples you can run locally.

Recommended pattern (summary)
- Use a Redwood service account model where GreenWave creates a service identity in your IdP and exchanges a signed JWT for a short-lived Redwood access token (1 hour).
- We support external token exchange via a standard OAuth2 JWT-assertion (client_credentials + JWT) flow or via STS (token exchange). No key material needs to be imported into Redwood; Redwood can validate JWTs using your IdP's public keys (JWKS).
- Token scoping: issue scoped tokens with claims that limit endpoints (e.g., scope: rerank:write, rerank:read, generate:prompt). Scopes are enforced by our routing layer so you can have a single credential which is only valid for reranker endpoints.
- Rotation & audit: recommended cadence — rotate long-lived signing credentials every 90 days; access tokens are 1h and do not require rotation. All token issuance and request-level audit logs can be exported to S3 (we'll provide a pre-signed delivery flow) and can also stream to your SIEM via our Syslog connector.

Example JWT assertion exchange (curl)

curl -X POST https://sts.redwood.ai/v1/token -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -d '\ngrant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&assertion=<BASE64_JWT>&scope=rerank:read%20rerank:write'

Response: { access_token: "ey...", token_type: "Bearer", expires_in: 3600 }

SDK snippet (node)

// initialize client with token exchange helper
const {RedwoodClient} = require('@redwood/inference-sdk');
const client = new RedwoodClient({
  tokenExchange: async (signedJwt) => {
    // call your IdP or Redwood STS endpoint to swap signedJwt for access_token
    const res = await fetch('https://sts.redwood.ai/v1/token', { method: 'POST', body: /* form */ });
    return (await res.json()).access_token;
  },
  region: 'us-west-2',
});

Operational notes
- Dedicated capacity routing: we can route reranker traffic to your dedicated pool by mapping a token claim (e.g., "pool:gw-dedicated") to the routing policy. That mapping is part of account configuration and does not require separate credentials.
- Structured output/tool calling: tokens can carry operation-level scopes (tool:invoke) so that tool calls are allowed only for approved service accounts.

I attached a short markdown with the exchange flow (sa-jwt-exchange.md) and will drop a reference implementation into the shared Drive link Rafael will send after this thread.

If helpful I can include a short diagram in the drive that shows token flow + audit export.

Regards,
Laura Bennett
Solutions Engineer\Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

Attachment: sa-jwt-exchange.md (text, 3KB)
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Dana Cole <dana.cole@greenwave.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Mike Huang <mike.huang@greenwave.ai>
Date: Wed, 31 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: GW: key ops & service-account check-in — next steps and scheduling

Dana / Mike — thanks for the quick clarifications and Laura for the clear pattern. Confirming the agreed next steps so we can be operationally ready before POC: 

Action items (owners & ETA)
- Redwood (Laura): provide STS endpoint details + full example repo (token exchange, scoped tokens, routing claims) — ETA Apr 2 COB.
- Redwood (Rafael): send calendar invite for Apr 5 10:00 AM PT and HubSpot deal update (I’ll include meeting agenda + Drive link).
- GreenWave (Mike): share IP allowlist, preferred signing key rotation cadence for the IdP, and S3 bucket details for audit exports — ETA Apr 1.
- Both: confirm whether the reranker endpoint should be restricted to a separate claim (recommended) or a separate pool (optional).

If everyone is aligned I’ll drop the Drive folder + invite shortly. Looking forward to the deep-dive.

Cheers,
Rafael

--
Rafael Mendes
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
rafael.mendes@redwood.ai

On Tue, Mar 30, 2027 at 11:02 AM Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Dana / Mike — Laura here from Solutions Engineering. Thanks for the clear constraints. Below is a pragmatic pattern that matches your requirements and examples you can run locally.
> 
> [snip - exchange pattern & examples]

Calendar: Apr 5, 2027 10:00 AM PT — https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=redwood-gw-tech-dive-20270405
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/67890

curl -X POST https://sts.redwood.ai/v1/token -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -d '
grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&assertion=<BASE64_JWT>&scope=rerank:read%20rerank:write'

Response: { access_token: "ey...", token_type: "Bearer", expires_in: 3600 }

SDK snippet (node)

// initialize client with token exchange helper
const {RedwoodClient} = require('@redwood/inference-sdk');
const client = new RedwoodClient({
  tokenExchange: async (signedJwt) => {
    // call your IdP or Redwood STS endpoint to swap signedJwt for access_token
    const res = await fetch('https://sts.redwood.ai/v1/token', { method: 'POST', body: /* form */ });
    return (await res.json()).access_token;
  },
  region: 'us-west-2',
});

Operational notes
- Dedicated capacity routing: we can route reranker traffic to your dedicated pool by mapping a token claim (e.g., "pool:gw-dedicated") to the routing policy. That mapping is part of account configuration and does not require separate credentials.
- Structured output/tool calling: tokens can carry operation-level scopes (tool:invoke) so that tool calls are allowed only for approved service accounts.

I attached a short markdown with the exchange flow (sa-jwt-exchange.md) and will drop a reference implementation into the shared Drive link Rafael will send after this thread.

If helpful I can include a short diagram in the drive that shows token flow + audit export.

Regards,
Laura Bennett
Solutions Engineer\Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

Attachment: sa-jwt-exchange.md (text, 3KB)

Recap (what we heard)
- Primary requirement: GreenWave wants server-to-server auth that avoids static API keys in prod. Prefer a service-account + short-lived token flow.
- Rotation: you want automated rotation with auditable key usage and rotation events surfaced to your SIEM.
- Routing: calls to sensitive reranker endpoints should be routed to dedicated capacity in us-west for latency guarantees.
- Structured output/tool-calling: you plan to use tool calls for enrichment pipelines and want to ensure token scoping limits downside.

Proposed checklist / immediate asks (we'll deliver items 1–3 before the tech dive):
1) Redwood to share a one-page integration checklist (attached) and a short example of service-account JWT exchange.
2) GreenWave to share preferred rotation cadence (30/60/90 days), IP allowlist ranges, and whether you need managed STS or self-hosted exchange.
3) Schedule a 60-minute technical deep dive with your infra/security + our SRE/solutions engineer (Laura + Marissa).

Questions for you before the dive:
- Is there a legal requirement to avoid any third-party KMS?
- Will you require our audit logs (per-request token identity) to feed into your SIEM via Syslog or S3?

Next steps I recommend:
- I’ll attach the integration checklist (redwood-integration-checklist.xlsx) and an example doc for the JWT exchange.
- Laura can follow up with a suggested token exchange pattern and sample curl/SDK snippets.
- If that looks good, we’ll lock the tech dive for next week — propose Apr 5 10:00 AM PT.

Thanks Rafael — this is helpful. A few clarifications from our side:

- We do prefer service accounts over long-lived API keys. Our security team wants short-lived tokens (<= 1 hour) issued by a trusted STS endpoint.
- Rotation cadence: we're targeting automated rotation every 30 days for long-lived credentials, but for access tokens we want ephemeral tokens with rotation handled by the issuing STS.
- KMS: we use a managed KMS (AWS KMS) and can't export keys, so any flow that requires key export is a non-starter. We'll need Redwood to integrate with our KMS via WK/STS or support token signing without key import.
- Audit logs: we need per-request identity (service-account id) in the access logs and a weekly export to an S3 bucket.

Also — we'd like to surface a couple of integration concerns for the reranker: will the routing to dedicated capacity require separate credentials or can we use scoped tokens that only permit rerank requests?

We can do Apr 5 10:00 AM PT for the deep dive. Can you add Mike (head of infra) on the invite; he'll handle the IP ranges and KMS constraints.

Hi Dana / Mike — Laura here from Solutions Engineering. Thanks for the clear constraints. Below is a pragmatic pattern that matches your requirements and examples you can run locally.

Recommended pattern (summary)
- Use a Redwood service account model where GreenWave creates a service identity in your IdP and exchanges a signed JWT for a short-lived Redwood access token (1 hour).
- We support external token exchange via a standard OAuth2 JWT-assertion (client_credentials + JWT) flow or via STS (token exchange). No key material needs to be imported into Redwood; Redwood can validate JWTs using your IdP's public keys (JWKS).
- Token scoping: issue scoped tokens with claims that limit endpoints (e.g., scope: rerank:write, rerank:read, generate:prompt). Scopes are enforced by our routing layer so you can have a single credential which is only valid for reranker endpoints.
- Rotation & audit: recommended cadence — rotate long-lived signing credentials every 90 days; access tokens are 1h and do not require rotation. All token issuance and request-level audit logs can be exported to S3 (we'll provide a pre-signed delivery flow) and can also stream to your SIEM via our Syslog connector.

Example JWT assertion exchange (curl)

curl -X POST https://sts.redwood.ai/v1/token -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -d '
grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&assertion=<BASE64_JWT>&scope=rerank:read%20rerank:write'

Response: { access_token: "ey...", token_type: "Bearer", expires_in: 3600 }

SDK snippet (node)

// initialize client with token exchange helper
const {RedwoodClient} = require('@redwood/inference-sdk');
const client = new RedwoodClient({
  tokenExchange: async (signedJwt) => {
    // call your IdP or Redwood STS endpoint to swap signedJwt for access_token
    const res = await fetch('https://sts.redwood.ai/v1/token', { method: 'POST', body: /* form */ });
    return (await res.json()).access_token;
  },
  region: 'us-west-2',
});

Operational notes
- Dedicated capacity routing: we can route reranker traffic to your dedicated pool by mapping a token claim (e.g., "pool:gw-dedicated") to the routing policy. That mapping is part of account configuration and does not require separate credentials.
- Structured output/tool calling: tokens can carry operation-level scopes (tool:invoke) so that tool calls are allowed only for approved service accounts.

I attached a short markdown with the exchange flow (sa-jwt-exchange.md) and will drop a reference implementation into the shared Drive link Rafael will send after this thread.

If helpful I can include a short diagram in the drive that shows token flow + audit export.

Regards,

source.subject: Fintronics — recap & next steps (logging scope, PII, retention)
messages:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Ava Parikh <ava.parikh@fintronics.com>
Cc: sales.ops@fintronics.com
Date: 2027-03-22T18:00:00-07:00
Subject: Recap: Fintronics call — logging scope, PII handling, and success criteria

Hi Ava — thanks again for your time earlier. Quick written recap so we have a single source of truth and clear next steps.

Short summary
- Pain: unpredictable inference costs and inability to reliably redact or segregate PII from logs.
- Current stack: on-prem inference layer + ELK for logs; data lake on S3; custom masking scripts run post-ingest.
- Goal: move to a predictable inference runtime, keep <200ms p95 for key endpoints, and implement auditable PII redaction with configurable retention (primary ask: 2 years for audit artifacts, 90 days for raw tokens).

Key discussion points (from call)
1) Redaction model: Fintronics needs redaction-at-ingest or redaction-on-write so PII never appears in persistent logs in plain text. You mentioned existing regex redaction is brittle and you need token-level policies that can be enforced by the serving layer.
2) Auditability: compliance needs an immutable audit trail (who/when) but stored in a way that PII is masked (e.g., cryptographic hashes + mapping kept in a separate KMS-protected store).
3) Retention: compliance wants 2 years for audit metadata, but only 90 days for any raw token material; full-text long-term storage is disallowed.
4) Access controls: strict RBAC and customer-managed KMS for any mapping tables.

Success criteria you called out
- No PII in customer-accessible logs; demonstrable redaction at the serving layer.
- Ability to produce an export for an audit within 72 hours.
- Predictable serving SLOs for peak traffic (p95 < 200ms).

Immediate next steps
- I will send the SOC2 and data-flow diagram we discussed (attached). [Attachment: recap-20270322.pdf]
- You said you'll share the specific fields you consider PII (hoping for the spreadsheet you mentioned) — I copied sales.ops so they can drop the template.
- Proposed: technical deep-dive next week with our infra engineer + security (30–45m) to go over redaction patterns and retention controls.

If I've missed anything or mis-specified a constraint, please flag. Otherwise I'll loop in Marissa and Rafael to align on procurement/contracting and follow up with a proposed NDA and SOW cadence.

Thanks,
Connor O'Brien
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0182
[Attachment: recap-20270322.pdf (PDF)]
From: Ava Parikh <ava.parikh@fintronics.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: sales.ops@fintronics.com
Date: 2027-03-22T19:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Recap: Fintronics call — logging scope, PII handling, and success criteria

Thanks Connor — this is great and captures the call. A couple clarifications from our compliance team: 

- Redaction timing: we require redaction-on-write for any user-submitted text that contains PII. Post-ingest redaction is acceptable only if the raw text is never persisted to durable logs; in practice we prefer the serving layer to emit a sanitized token stream to downstream systems.
- Audit trail: acceptable approach is hashed identifiers with a separate mapping table that is encrypted with our KMS; mapping access must be auditable and require dual-approval.
- Retention nuance: 90 days for raw tokens; 24 months for audit metadata (who accessed/which mapping IDs were used).

I'll attach the initial PII field list — it's a work in progress but should help scope. Also, can Redwood support per-customer retention policies (we have different requirements for EU vs US customers)?

Appreciate it — let's lock the tech deep-dive for next Tue 3/30 if that works.

Ava
Ava Parikh
Head of Platform Security, Fintronics
ava.parikh@fintronics.com | +1 (617) 222-4410
[Attachment: fintronics_pii_fields_v1.xlsx (XLSX)]
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-22T20:10:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Recap: Fintronics call — they need redaction-on-write + retention controls

Marissa / Rafael — forwarding the thread from Fintronics. Key asks: redaction-on-write (token-level), per-customer retention policies (90d raw / 24mo audit), customer-managed KMS for mapping tables, and a requirement to produce audit exports within 72h.

Can you advise on:
- Whether Redwood can offer an architecture that redacts before logs reach durable storage (i.e., sanitized tokens only).
- Pricing implications for 24-month audit retention and KMS-backed mapping store.
- Any contract language we should surface for audit access and data segregation.

Attaching the brief they sent and the PII spreadsheet.

Connor O'Brien
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: fintronics_log_requirements.xlsx (XLSX)]

-----Forwarded message-----
From: Ava Parikh <ava.parikh@fintronics.com>
Date: 2027-03-22 19:05
Subject: Re: Recap: Fintronics call — logging scope, PII handling, and success criteria
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, sales.ops@fintronics.com

(embedded above)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Parikh <ava.parikh@fintronics.com>, sales.ops@fintronics.com
Date: 2027-03-23T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Recap: Fintronics call — they need redaction-on-write + retention controls

Thanks for the forward, Connor — and thanks Ava for the clear constraints. Quick response and proposed plan: 

1) Capabilities
- Redwood supports configurable redaction policies at the serving layer via rule + ML-assisted detectors; we can deploy 'redact-on-write' so logs persist only the sanitized token stream.
- Audit metadata (access logs, mapping IDs) can be retained for configurable periods; we support customer-managed KMS for encryption of mapping tables.
- Per-customer retention policies are supported (we already have EU/US region-aware retention configs).

2) Commercial/contract notes
- Retaining audit metadata for 24 months is a billable add-on (storage + extended retention SLA).
- We'll need an addendum for KMS integration and for any request-to-produce-audit within 72 hours; I'll draft language that limits scope to metadata exports (no full-text dumps).

3) Next steps (proposed timeline)
- Tech deep-dive next Tue 2027-03-30 10:00–10:45 PT — include one infra engineer and one security architect from Redwood. (Connor, please confirm with Ava.)
- I'll circulate a draft SOW + NDA (DocuSign) and an initial commercial estimate by EOD Thursday.
- Sales Ops: once Connor confirms timeline, please open HubSpot deal and include the fintronics_log_requirements.xlsx in the record.

Links / refs
- HubSpot: https://hubspot.redwood.com/deal/FT-2026-0432
- Draft NDA: https://docusign.com/envelopes/0A1B2C3

Connor — happy to take ownership of the SOW/contract language. Rafael, can you review commercial sizing for the 24mo audit retention and KMS integration?

Best,
Marissa Cole
Director, Commercial Ops | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0119
[Attachment: nda-fintronics.pdf (PDF)]

Key discussion points (from call)
1) Redaction model: Fintronics needs redaction-at-ingest or redaction-on-write so PII never appears in persistent logs in plain text. You mentioned existing regex redaction is brittle and you need token-level policies that can be enforced by the serving layer.
2) Auditability: compliance needs an immutable audit trail (who/when) but stored in a way that PII is masked (e.g., cryptographic hashes + mapping kept in a separate KMS-protected store).
3) Retention: compliance wants 2 years for audit metadata, but only 90 days for any raw token material; full-text long-term storage is disallowed.
4) Access controls: strict RBAC and customer-managed KMS for any mapping tables.

Success criteria you called out
- No PII in customer-accessible logs; demonstrable redaction at the serving layer.
- Ability to produce an export for an audit within 72 hours.
- Predictable serving SLOs for peak traffic (p95 < 200ms).

Immediate next steps
- I will send the SOC2 and data-flow diagram we discussed (attached). [Attachment: recap-20270322.pdf]
- You said you'll share the specific fields you consider PII (hoping for the spreadsheet you mentioned) — I copied sales.ops so they can drop the template.
- Proposed: technical deep-dive next week with our infra engineer + security (30–45m) to go over redaction patterns and retention controls.

If I've missed anything or mis-specified a constraint, please flag. Otherwise I'll loop in Marissa and Rafael to align on procurement/contracting and follow up with a proposed NDA and SOW cadence.

Thanks,
Connor O'Brien
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0182
[Attachment: recap-20270322.pdf (PDF)]


From: Ava Parikh <ava.parikh@fintronics.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: sales.ops@fintronics.com
Date: 2027-03-22T19:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Recap: Fintronics call — logging scope, PII handling, and success criteria

Thanks Connor — this is great and captures the call. A couple clarifications from our compliance team: 

- Redaction timing: we require redaction-on-write for any user-submitted text that contains PII. Post-ingest redaction is acceptable only if the raw text is never persisted to durable logs; in practice we prefer the serving layer to emit a sanitized token stream to downstream systems.
- Audit trail: acceptable approach is hashed identifiers with a separate mapping table that is encrypted with our KMS; mapping access must be auditable and require dual-approval.
- Retention nuance: 90 days for raw tokens; 24 months for audit metadata (who accessed/which mapping IDs were used).

I'll attach the initial PII field list — it's a work in progress but should help scope. Also, can Redwood support per-customer retention policies (we have different requirements for EU vs US customers)?

Appreciate it — let's lock the tech deep-dive for next Tue 3/30 if that works.

Ava
Ava Parikh
Head of Platform Security, Fintronics
ava.parikh@fintronics.com | +1 (617) 222-4410
[Attachment: fintronics_pii_fields_v1.xlsx (XLSX)]


From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-22T20:10:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Recap: Fintronics call — they need redaction-on-write + retention controls

Marissa / Rafael — forwarding the thread from Fintronics. Key asks: redaction-on-write (token-level), per-customer retention policies (90d raw / 24mo audit), customer-managed KMS for mapping tables, and a requirement to produce audit exports within 72h.

Can you advise on:
- Whether Redwood can offer an architecture that redacts before logs reach durable storage (i.e., sanitized tokens only).
- Pricing implications for 24-month audit retention and KMS-backed mapping store.
- Any contract language we should surface for audit access and data segregation.

Attaching the brief they sent and the PII spreadsheet.

Connor O'Brien
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: fintronics_log_requirements.xlsx (XLSX)]

-----Forwarded message-----
From: Ava Parikh <ava.parikh@fintronics.com>
Date: 2027-03-22 19:05
Subject: Re: Recap: Fintronics call — logging scope, PII handling, and success criteria
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, sales.ops@fintronics.com

(embedded above)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Parikh <ava.parikh@fintronics.com>, sales.ops@fintronics.com
Date: 2027-03-23T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Recap: Fintronics call — they need redaction-on-write + retention controls

Thanks for the forward, Connor — and thanks Ava for the clear constraints. Quick response and proposed plan: 

1) Capabilities
- Redwood supports configurable redaction policies at the serving layer via rule + ML-assisted detectors; we can deploy 'redact-on-write' so logs persist only the sanitized token stream.
- Audit metadata (access logs, mapping IDs) can be retained for configurable periods; we support customer-managed KMS for encryption of mapping tables.
- Per-customer retention policies are supported (we already have EU/US region-aware retention configs).

2) Commercial/contract notes
- Retaining audit metadata for 24 months is a billable add-on (storage + extended retention SLA).
- We'll need an addendum for KMS integration and for any request-to-produce-audit within 72 hours; I'll draft language that limits scope to metadata exports (no full-text dumps).

3) Next steps (proposed timeline)
- Tech deep-dive next Tue 2027-03-30 10:00–10:45 PT — include one infra engineer and one security architect from Redwood. (Connor, please confirm with Ava.)
- I'll circulate a draft SOW + NDA (DocuSign) and an initial commercial estimate by EOD Thursday.
- Sales Ops: once Connor confirms timeline, please open HubSpot deal and include the fintronics_log_requirements.xlsx in the record.

Links / refs
- HubSpot: https://hubspot.redwood.com/deal/FT-2026-0432
- Draft NDA: https://docusign.com/envelopes/0A1B2C3

Connor — happy to take ownership of the SOW/contract language. Rafael, can you review commercial sizing for the 24mo audit retention and KMS integration?

Best,
Marissa Cole
Director, Commercial Ops | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0119
[Attachment: nda-fintronics.pdf (PDF)]

source.subject: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline
messages:
From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: James Holloway <james.h@healthgrid.com>, Maya Singh <maya@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline

Hi James / Maya —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap + proposed next steps to get the Technical Evaluation milestone on a tight cadence so we can hit the MAP target close date (details below).

Summary of what we agreed on
- Objective: Validate Redwood inference performance and cost vs your production SLAs with a 2-week technical eval.
- Success criteria (pass = all three):
  • Latency: p95 < 220ms for 512-token interactive queries at 8 concurrent users (internal load spec attached)
  • Cost: < $0.0009/token (measured after batching and cache) for steady-state load
  • Quality: model responds correctly to 95% of the 120 test prompts in the eval suite (acceptance thresholds in the spec)
- Model shortlist for the slate: Llama 2 70B (quantized), Mistral-Instruct-7B, and Redwood-v1-medium (baseline).

Owners & responsibilities
- HealthGrid (you): provide a sample of 1,000 representative prompts + expected outputs, and a service account for VPN access to the staging data store (Maya).
- Redwood (Rafael): run benchmark sweep, produce latency/cost report, and share experimental configs (batch sizes, kv-cache policy).
- Redwood (Karthik): test integration with your API and confirm end-to-end trace for p95 measurement.
- Redwood (Priya): collect security/compliance checklist items (data flow, retention) to ensure we're ok to run on dedicated staging env.

Proposed timeline (target close date in MAP: 2027-05-20)
- Mar 17: HealthGrid provides dataset + access (dependency)
- Mar 18–23: Redwood runs initial benchmark sweep and posts results to Drive
- Mar 24: Joint sync (30 min) to review results and agree on acceptance
- Mar 25–30: If needed, follow-up tuning run and final acceptance

Dependencies / blockers
- VPN/service account access (required by 3/17)
- Clarified prompt rubric for quality scoring (see column "expected_behavior" in attached CSV)
- SOC2 evidence for staging if you want the run on dedicated infra (Priya will follow-up)

Attachments: latency_test_spec.pdf (test harness), healthgrid_eval_dataset.csv (sample prompts), redwood-map-draft-v2.docx (MAP copy with milestones)

Next steps for this thread
1) James / Maya: confirm dataset delivery date and whether you'd like the run on our hosted staging or on a dedicated environment (affects cost).
2) Rafael / Karthik / Priya: confirm owners (done) and call out any additional needs.

Thanks — I'll schedule the Mar 24 sync once we have the data delivery date.

Best,
Grace
Grace O'Connor
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0142

From: James Holloway <james.h@healthgrid.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Maya Singh <maya@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline

Grace — thanks for the clear summary. A few quick points from our side: 

1) Dataset delivery: we can deliver the 1,000 prompts and expected outputs by end of day Mar 16 (we'll drop a CSV in the Drive link you shared).
2) Environment: we'd prefer to run on Redwood's hosted staging first to move faster; if results look promising we'll discuss a dedicated staging run for SOC or PII-sensitive checks.
3) Success criteria: the latency p95 target looks reasonable for interactive UX; the cost threshold is our current internal goal but flexible depending on batching gains you can show.

Action: I'll upload the CSV tonight and grant the service account access to the staging bucket. Maya and I will join the Mar 24 sync.

—James
James Holloway
Director of Platform Engineering | HealthGrid
james.h@healthgrid.com | (617) 555-0177

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
To: James Holloway <james.h@healthgrid.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Maya Singh <maya@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2027 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline

Thanks — Rafael here (Inference engineering). A few technical clarifications and what I'll deliver: 

Clarifications needed from HealthGrid:
- Confirm typical token distribution (median/95th) and percentage of generation vs. classification-style prompts in the 1,000 prompt bundle. That affects kernel selection and batch sizing.
- Will the prompts include multi-turn state (we need to know whether to persist KV caches per session).

What Redwood will deliver (by Mar 23 unless otherwise noted):
- An end-to-end runbook (markdown) showing exact model configs we tested, batching parameters, and quantization levels.
- Latency report with p50/p95/p99 for each model variant and concurrent-user scenario.
- Cost breakdown: token-level cost post-batching, and incremental cost delta vs your current endpoint baseline.
- Artifacts: raw load traces (gzip), aggregated metrics CSV, and a short runner script to reproduce the exact sweep.

Model slate rationale:
- Llama 2 70B (quantized): best candidate for quality at higher latency/cost; we expect good Q95 but will test quantized kernels.
- Mistral-Instruct-7B: lower cost profile, often acceptable quality for shorter prompts.
- Redwood-v1-medium: our internal baseline with optimizations for streaming and KV cache reuse.

I'll flag any failure modes early in the Drive folder. If HealthGrid needs the run on a dedicated pool for data governance, Priya — can you outline the SOC2/staging checklist and ETA to approve?

-Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Sr. Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: James Holloway <james.h@healthgrid.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Mar 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: Technical evaluation playbook — owners, success criteria, timeline

Perfect — thanks everyone. Short confirmation: 

- James: look for the Drive folder access request from me in ~30 minutes; please upload the CSV there tonight.
- Rafael / Karthik: we’ll aim to have the initial benchmark posted by EOD Mar 23. If the first sweep meets the pass criteria we’ll mark the technical milestone as complete in the MAP and move to procurement for Dedicated if HealthGrid requests.
- Priya: please start the SOC2 evidence checklist and flag any items that would prevent a dedicated staging run (I can loop in HealthGrid security separately).

I created the Mar 24 9:30am PT sync — calendar invite sent. Agenda will be: results review (10m), gaps/tuning (10m), acceptance decision (10m).

Attachments: redwood-map-draft-v2.docx, latency_test_spec.pdf

Thanks — looking forward to the run.

Grace
Grace O'Connor
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Cruz <elena.cruz@brightspan.com>, Simon James <simon.james@brightspan.com>

From: Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Feb 2027 08:12:00 +0000
Subject: Final pass: EMEA & APAC pipeline hygiene, reserve holds and stage remediation

Team —

We’re moving into the last-pass for this cycle and I need a focused, short sweep across EMEA + APAC to clean up stage noise and confirm any soft‑commits we want to carry into finance for reserve planning. Please treat this as the single source for any last updates — I’ll consolidate for the finance cut later Friday.

Quick asks (please reply inline or update the attached template):
- Review opportunities you own and either: 1) confirm commit and final close date, 2) move to Nurture (update reason), or 3) close-won / close-lost with a short note.
- Mark any deals that require a procurement hold / legal redline or are pending a PO (flag with "HOLD: procurement").
- Confirm any seat/usage estimates that differ from what’s in HubSpot.

Deadline: EOD Thursday (26 Feb) 17:00 UTC.

Attachment: EMEA-APAC-final-pass-template.xlsx (tabbed by region / AE).

I’ll run an evening rollup and share a one‑page for finance tomorrow morning. Callouts: Acme (RWD-ACME-2027-Q1) needs a final decision—Julia in procurement is asking for a PO ETA.

Thanks —
Olga
Olga Petrov
Senior Director, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
olga_petrov@redwood.com | +44 20 7000 1200

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga_petrov@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Feb 2027 09:05:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Final pass: EMEA & APAC pipeline hygiene, reserve holds and stage remediation

Thanks Olga — on it. Quick summary for my book: 3 deals in commit (one expects PO by 3/2), 2 soft-commits pending contract redlines, 4 that we’ll move to Nurture (low buyer intent).

I’ve updated the shared template and attached the mutual action plan for the Acme engagement (ACME_Mutual_Action_Plan.pdf) — Julia asked to confirm the implementation window (we’ve penciled May).

Action items from me:
- Confirm PO ETA with Julia (cc’d in later thread).
- Push contract redline to Legal for two deals (Karthik, can you standby for priority review?).

—Vanessa
Vanessa Ortiz
Account Executive, EMEA | Redwood Inference
vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com

source.subject: Praxis Health POC kickstart — calendar coordination & next steps
messages:
From: Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@praxishealth.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: infra@praxishealth.com
Date: 2027-02-20T09:12:00Z
Subject: POC kickoff — scheduling / agenda request

Hi Hannah + Connor,

Thanks again for the demo last week. The Praxis product team would like to run a hands-on POC kickstart next week to validate prompt routing and run a brief load profile (small-scale). Can we lock a 90-minute working session that includes: prompt review, routing configuration options, and a smoke load test (50–100 concurrent connections)?

Our preferred windows (all PST):
- Tue 2/22: 1:00–4:30pm
- Wed 2/23: 9:00–12:00pm

Attendees: Emma Sullivan (PM), Liam Patel (SRE), Praxis infra alias (infra@praxishealth.com). Please let us know if we should invite any additional Redwood folks. Also appreciate any pre-reads we should review ahead of time.

Thanks,
Emma
Emma Sullivan
Product Manager, Praxis Health

---
Attachment: none

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@praxishealth.com>, Liam Patel <liam.patel@praxishealth.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-02-20T10:05:00Z
Subject: Re: POC kickoff — scheduling / agenda request

Emma — thanks for the windows and context. Both Tue and Wed could work for us; propose two options and we can pick the one that fits your team best:

Option A: Tue 2027-02-22, 16:00–17:30 PST
Option B: Wed 2027-02-23, 10:00–11:30 PST

Proposed agenda (90 minutes):
- 5m intro / goals
- 20m prompt review and warmup (we'll bring sample prompt sets)
- 30m routing & policy configuration (canary routing, fallback variants)
- 20m small-scale load run & metrics review (50–100 connections)
- 15m action items / next steps

I will include Connor (solutions) for the runbook and Rafael (SE) if we need deeper runtime tuning — looping him in if the slot is confirmed.

Would you prefer we send a calendar invite for Option A or B? Also, we'll provide two short pre-reads: a prompt checklist and the POC runbook.

Best,
Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: none

From: Liam Patel <liam.patel@praxishealth.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@praxishealth.com>
Cc: infra@praxishealth.com, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-02-20T13:40:00Z
Subject: Re: POC kickoff — scheduling / agenda request

Thanks — quick heads up, I'm booked Tue afternoon and won't be available for Option A. Option B (Wed 10:00–11:30 PST) works for me, but can we move it an hour later if possible? Our SRE lead has a recurring call at 10:15 that day.

Proposed: Wed 2027-02-23, 11:00–12:30 PST (still within your original window). If that works for Redwood, we'll lock it.

Apologies for the switch — Dr. Patel (not me) is presenting a system update to their board earlier that day so I need to avoid overlap.

Liam
Liam Patel
SRE Manager, Praxis Health

---
Attachment: none

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam Patel <liam.patel@praxishealth.com>, Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@praxishealth.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-02-21T08:30:00Z
Subject: Re: POC kickoff — scheduling / agenda request 

Thanks for the update, Liam — Wed 2027-02-23 11:00–12:30 PST is good for the Redwood team. I've created a calendar invite and attached two short pre-reads: praxis_prework_prompts.md and redwood_poc_runbook_v1.pdf. Please review the prompt checklist before the session; it will save us time.

Session owners and roles:
- Redwood run lead: Connor O'Brien (solutions) — runbook & load test owner
- Redwood prompts & routing: Rafael Mendes (SE) — prompt tuning and routing config
- Redwood customer success: Grace O'Connor — onboarding & follow-ups

Logistics:
- Join: Zoom link in invite (sent)
- Prework: review the prompt checklist and share one representative prompt per workflow by EOD Tue 2/22
- Load test expectations: 50–100 parallel connections, short bursts (3–5 mins) against a sandbox model variant

Attachments: praxis_prework_prompts.md (text/markdown), redwood_poc_runbook_v1.pdf (application/pdf)

If anything changes, flag it here and we'll adjust. Looking forward to it.

Best,
Hannah
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference

---
Attachment stubs: praxis_prework_prompts.md, redwood_poc_runbook_v1.pdf

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emma Sullivan <emma.sullivan@praxishealth.com>, Liam Patel <liam.patel@praxishealth.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, infra@praxishealth.com
Date: 2027-02-23T16:45:00Z
Subject: Praxis POC — session recap & action items (2027-02-23)

Hi all,

Thanks for a productive session today. Quick recap and owners & timelines below. Please flag any corrections.

What we did:
- Prompt review: walked through 6 representative prompts from Praxis (Emma) and validated expected intents and slot extraction for 4 of them. Rafael suggested two minor tokenization adjustments to reduce hallucination risk.
- Routing: configured a basic canary that routes 10% of customer traffic to a quantized variant for cost testing; discussed automatic fallback to full-precision model on latency errors.
- Load smoke: ran three 3-minute bursts (50, 75, 100 parallel connections). Observed 95th pct latency ~210ms for the quantized variant; no OOMs. Connor captured metrics and shared the run logs in the Drive link.

Action items (owner — due):
- Emma: share the remaining 4 representative prompts and two example user sessions (due 2027-02-25)
- Connor O'Brien: upload full run logs + distilled metrics to the shared Drive folder and link in HubSpot (due 2027-02-24)
- Rafael Mendes: produce a short tuning checklist (3 items) to further lower 95th pct latency (due 2027-02-28)
- Grace O'Connor: draft a mutual action plan with success criteria for the next 30 days (first draft due 2027-03-02)
- Hannah Schmitt: coordinate next session time & follow up on procurement questions raised by Praxis procurement (due 2027-02-26)

Next session: Rafael recommended a targeted tuning session + expanded load test (up to 500 parallel connections) once we have the additional prompts. Suggested window: the week of 2027-03-07. I'll send a Doodle with 3 options this Friday.

Links & artifacts:
- Run logs: https://drive.redwoodinference.com/shared/praxis/run-logs-20270223
- Agenda & runbook: https://drive.redwoodinference.com/shared/praxis/poc-agenda
- HubSpot deal: https://hubspot.redwoodinference.com/deals/DEAL-2027-0137

Thanks again — great collaboration today.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments referenced: none (artifacts uploaded to Drive)

Subject: Praxis POC — session recap & action items (2027-02-23)

Thanks for a productive session today. Quick recap and owners & timelines below. Please flag any corrections.

What we did:
- Prompt review: walked through 6 representative prompts from Praxis (Emma) and validated expected intents and slot extraction for 4 of them. Rafael suggested two minor tokenization adjustments to reduce hallucination risk.
- Routing: configured a basic canary that routes 10% of customer traffic to a quantized variant for cost testing; discussed automatic fallback to full-precision model on latency errors.
- Load smoke: ran three 3-minute bursts (50, 75, 100 parallel connections). Observed 95th pct latency ~210ms for the quantized variant; no OOMs. Connor captured metrics and shared the run logs in the Drive link.

Action items (owner — due):
- Emma: share the remaining 4 representative prompts and two example user sessions (due 2027-02-25)
- Connor O'Brien: upload full run logs + distilled metrics to the shared Drive folder and link in HubSpot (due 2027-02-24)
- Rafael Mendes: produce a short tuning checklist (3 items) to further lower 95th pct latency (due 2027-02-28)
- Grace O'Connor: draft a mutual action plan with success criteria for the next 30 days (first draft due 2027-03-02)
- Hannah Schmitt: coordinate next session time & follow up on procurement questions raised by Praxis procurement (due 2027-02-26)

Next session: Rafael recommended a targeted tuning session + expanded load test (up to 500 parallel connections) once we have the additional prompts. Suggested window: the week of 2027-03-07. I'll send a Doodle with 3 options this Friday.

Links & artifacts:
- Run logs: https://drive.redwoodinference.com/shared/praxis/run-logs-20270223
- Agenda & runbook: https://drive.redwoodinference.com/shared/praxis/poc-agenda
- HubSpot deal: https://hubspot.redwoodinference.com/deals/DEAL-2027-0137

Thanks again — great collaboration today.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Request: Contract asks — termination rights & fee-withholding for ClearBox
messages:
From: Alexei Popov <alexei.popov@clearboxpay.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya@clearboxpay.com>, contracts@clearboxpay.com
Date: Sun, 14 Feb 2027 10:01:12 -0800
Subject: Request: Contract language for termination and fee-withholding (ClearBox account)

Hi Avery —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We're excited about the latency profile and reliability you showed, but our legal & compliance teams are pushing for stronger commercial remedies than standard service credits. Specifically they're asking for:

- A fee-withholding / escrow equivalent to 6 months of anticipated fees (to be released on successful operational milestones),
- Clear termination rights for repeat P1/P2 outages (we're talking sustained availability/lost throughput for payments), and
- A liquidated damages clause for direct processing losses tied to repeat SLA failures.

I attached a redline that our GC circulated; it's heavy-handed, but it outlines what they'd accept. Can your team review and tell us if any of this is workable and what alternatives Redwood would accept (capped escrow, staged buyout, cure periods, etc.)?

We need an initial position by EOD Thursday if possible — procurement is trying to line up roadmap approvals. I'd prefer a short call to walk through specifics.

Attachments: CB_SLA_Amendment_Redline.docx (redline from ClearBox GC)

Thanks,
Alexei Popov
Head of Payments Partnerships
ClearBox Payments

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Alexei Popov <alexei.popov@clearboxpay.com>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya@clearboxpay.com>
Date: Sun, 14 Feb 2027 11:06:03 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Contract language for termination and fee-withholding (ClearBox account)

Alexei — thanks for the clear summary and the redline. I appreciate the urgency.

Quick note: we won't be able to accept an open-ended liquidated damages exposure as written, but we should find a commercially workable compromise. I'm looping in our legal & AM partners to give you a concrete proposal (capped escrow / staged buyout / enhanced ops guarantees). Can we set a 30-minute call on Wednesday (Feb 17) or Thursday morning?

I'll follow up with an internal POV and a proposed doc we can use as a starting point.

Best,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Feb 2027 09:22:10 -0800
Subject: Fwd: ClearBox contract asks — need legal + AM POV

Folks — forwarding ClearBox's redline (attached). Short summary: customer wants 6mo escrow, termination rights for repeat P1/P2, and liquidated damages for losses. Procurement is pushing for a position this week.

Questions for legal / ops / AM:
- Legal: how far can we go without creating open-ended monetary liability? Any precedent for escrowed fee-withholdings?
- AM: what's the account impact if we propose staged buyout vs hard escrow? Revenue / churn risk?
- Ops: can we offer an elevated monitoring + weekly ops review and commit to defined remediation windows?

Proposed commercial anchor (my initial thought): 3 months MRR escrow with staged release on milestones, termination only after 3 distinct severity-1 incidents impacting >=48 hours total in a 90-day rolling window, and a capped buyout equal to remaining 6 months at NRR. But this is negotiable — want legal input.

Attachments: CB_SLA_Amendment_Redline.docx, redline_suggestions_avery_v1.pdf

Thanks — Avery

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Feb 2027 12:43:31 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: ClearBox contract asks — need legal + AM POV

Avery / team —

High-level legal position: we cannot accept uncapped liquidated damages or unconstrained escrow that is effectively a security for all customer risk. That creates systemic liability and insurance/regulatory complications. However, there are reasonable mitigations we can offer: a capped escrow/holdback equal to up to 3 months of committed fees (not 6), with explicit milestone release criteria; narrowly-tailored termination right that requires repeated, documented, severe SLA breaches with cure windows (e.g., three P1-equivalent incidents totaling >48 hours of degraded availability in 90 days) plus 30-day cure; and a clearly-capped buyout formula (e.g., remaining committed fees capped at 6x monthly average or a fixed multiplier).

We should avoid phrases like "liquidated damages for direct losses" unless we can tightly define loss categories and caps. Instead, propose structured cash holdback + enhanced credits + a one-time buyout option for repeated, unremediated failures. I'll attach a legal counter-redline with sample language and suggested caps.

Attachments: legal_counter_redline.docx

If we need to escalate commercially, Rafael can discuss exceptions with Sales Leadership, but legal prefers caps + structured cure as the starting point.

—Marissa
Deputy General Counsel
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alexei Popov <alexei.popov@clearboxpay.com>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Maya Singh <maya@clearboxpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Feb 2027 10:15:00 -0800
Subject: Proposal: Compromise on ClearBox SLA remedies

Alexei / Maya —

Thanks for the patience while we reviewed your request. We value ClearBox as a strategic partner and want to get to terms that protect you without exposing Redwood to unlimited liability. Here's a concrete compromise we can present: 

1) Holdback / Escrow: 3 months of estimated billed fees held in escrow. Release schedule: 50% after month 3 if no P1 incidents in the period, remaining 50% after month 6 subject to milestone checks.

2) Remediation & Termination: Termination right only after three documented P1-equivalent incidents totalling >48 hours of degraded availability in any 90-day rolling window, each incident subject to a 30-day cure window.

3) Buyout / Cash Cap: If termination is triggered, a buyout option is available capped at 6 months of average spend (not uncapped LD).

4) Credits & Ops: Enhanced SLA credits (higher credit %) for qualifying incidents, plus weekly ops review and a dedicated escalation path with a named SRE lead while the deal is under escrow.

We can share a redline that adopts Marissa's suggested language for the holdback and cure timetable. If that direction sounds okay, I'll circulate the redline and a DocuSign packet for an amendment and SOW update.

DocuSign: https://docusign.net/clearbox-sla-v1
Attachment: SLA_proposal_ClearBox_20270218.pdf

Are you available for a 30-minute call Thurs 2/18 1:00–1:30pm PT to align?

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Manager
Redwood Inference

From: Alexei Popov <alexei.popov@clearboxpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya@clearboxpay.com>, contracts@clearboxpay.com
Date: Thu, 18 Feb 2027 15:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: Compromise on ClearBox SLA remedies

Ben / Avery —

Thanks — that framing is helpful and much closer to what procurement will accept. The 3-month holdback + milestone release + capped buyout looks reasonable. Please send the redline and DocuSign, and let's take the 1:00pm PT slot today. I'll have our GC on the line for the call.

Attachments requested: the legal counter-redline you mentioned and the updated SOW.

Talk shortly —
Alexei
\n

Subject: Re: Proposal: Compromise on ClearBox SLA remedies

Ben / Avery —

Thanks — that framing is helpful and much closer to what procurement will accept. The 3-month holdback + milestone release + capped buyout looks reasonable. Please send the redline and DocuSign, and let's take the 1:00pm PT slot today. I'll have our GC on the line for the call.

Attachments requested: the legal counter-redline you mentioned and the updated SOW.

Talk shortly —

text:
From: Alexei Popov <alexei.popov@clearboxpay.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya@clearboxpay.com>, contracts@clearboxpay.com
Date: Sun, 14 Feb 2027 10:01:12 -0800
Subject: Request: Contract language for termination and fee-withholding (ClearBox account)

Hi Avery —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We're excited about the latency profile and reliability you showed, but our legal & compliance teams are pushing for stronger commercial remedies than standard service credits. Specifically they're asking for:

- A fee-withholding / escrow equivalent to 6 months of anticipated fees (to be released on successful operational milestones),
- Clear termination rights for repeat P1/P2 outages (we're talking sustained availability/lost throughput for payments), and
- A liquidated damages clause for direct processing losses tied to repeat SLA failures.

I attached a redline that our GC circulated; it's heavy-handed, but it outlines what they'd accept. Can your team review and tell us if any of this is workable and what alternatives Redwood would accept (capped escrow, staged buyout, cure periods, etc.)?

We need an initial position by EOD Thursday if possible — procurement is trying to line up roadmap approvals. I'd prefer a short call to walk through specifics.

Attachments: CB_SLA_Amendment_Redline.docx (redline from ClearBox GC)

Thanks,
Alexei Popov
Head of Payments Partnerships
ClearBox Payments


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Alexei Popov <alexei.popov@clearboxpay.com>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya@clearboxpay.com>
Date: Sun, 14 Feb 2027 11:06:03 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Contract language for termination and fee-withholding (ClearBox account)

Alexei — thanks for the clear summary and the redline. I appreciate the urgency.

Quick note: we won't be able to accept an open-ended liquidated damages exposure as written, but we should find a commercially workable compromise. I'm looping in our legal & AM partners to give you a concrete proposal (capped escrow / staged buyout / enhanced ops guarantees). Can we set a 30-minute call on Wednesday (Feb 17) or Thursday morning?

I'll follow up with an internal POV and a proposed doc we can use as a starting point.

Best,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference


From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Feb 2027 09:22:10 -0800
Subject: Fwd: ClearBox contract asks — need legal + AM POV

Folks — forwarding ClearBox's redline (attached). Short summary: customer wants 6mo escrow, termination rights for repeat P1/P2, and liquidated damages for losses. Procurement is pushing for a position this week.

Questions for legal / ops / AM:
- Legal: how far can we go without creating open-ended monetary liability? Any precedent for escrowed fee-withholdings?
- AM: what's the account impact if we propose staged buyout vs hard escrow? Revenue / churn risk?
- Ops: can we offer an elevated monitoring + weekly ops review and commit to defined remediation windows?

Proposed commercial anchor (my initial thought): 3 months MRR escrow with staged release on milestones, termination only after 3 distinct severity-1 incidents impacting >=48 hours total in a 90-day rolling window, and a capped buyout equal to remaining 6 months at NRR. But this is negotiable — want legal input.

Attachments: CB_SLA_Amendment_Redline.docx, redline_suggestions_avery_v1.pdf

Thanks — Avery


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Feb 2027 12:43:31 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: ClearBox contract asks — need legal + AM POV

Avery / team —

High-level legal position: we cannot accept uncapped liquidated damages or unconstrained escrow that is effectively a security for all customer risk. That creates systemic liability and insurance/regulatory complications. However, there are reasonable mitigations we can offer: a capped escrow/holdback equal to up to 3 months of committed fees (not 6), with explicit milestone release criteria; narrowly-tailored termination right that requires repeated, documented, severe SLA breaches with cure windows (e.g., three P1-equivalent incidents totaling >48 hours of degraded availability in 90 days) plus 30-day cure; and a clearly-capped buyout formula (e.g., remaining committed fees capped at 6x monthly average or a fixed multiplier).

We should avoid phrases like "liquidated damages for direct losses" unless we can tightly define loss categories and caps. Instead, propose structured cash holdback + enhanced credits + a one-time buyout option for repeated, unremediated failures. I'll attach a legal counter-redline with sample language and suggested caps.

Attachments: legal_counter_redline.docx

If we need to escalate commercially, Rafael can discuss exceptions with Sales Leadership, but legal prefers caps + structured cure as the starting point.

—Marissa
Deputy General Counsel
Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alexei Popov <alexei.popov@clearboxpay.com>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Maya Singh <maya@clearboxpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Feb 2027 10:15:00 -0800
Subject: Proposal: Compromise on ClearBox SLA remedies

Alexei / Maya —

Thanks for the patience while we reviewed your request. We value ClearBox as a strategic partner and want to get to terms that protect you without exposing Redwood to unlimited liability. Here's a concrete compromise we can present: 

1) Holdback / Escrow: 3 months of estimated billed fees held in escrow. Release schedule: 50% after month 3 if no P1 incidents in the period, remaining 50% after month 6 subject to milestone checks.

2) Remediation & Termination: Termination right only after three documented P1-equivalent incidents totalling >48 hours of degraded availability in any 90-day rolling window, each incident subject to a 30-day cure window.

3) Buyout / Cash Cap: If termination is triggered, a buyout option is available capped at 6 months of average spend (not uncapped LD).

4) Credits & Ops: Enhanced SLA credits (higher credit %) for qualifying incidents, plus weekly ops review and a dedicated escalation path with a named SRE lead while the deal is under escrow.

We can share a redline that adopts Marissa's suggested language for the holdback and cure timetable. If that direction sounds okay, I'll circulate the redline and a DocuSign packet for an amendment and SOW update.

DocuSign: https://docusign.net/clearbox-sla-v1
Attachment: SLA_proposal_ClearBox_20270218.pdf

Are you available for a 30-minute call Thurs 2/18 1:00–1:30pm PT to align?

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Manager
Redwood Inference


From: Alexei Popov <alexei.popov@clearboxpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya@clearboxpay.com>, contracts@clearboxpay.com
Date: Thu, 18 Feb 2027 15:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal: Compromise on ClearBox SLA remedies

Ben / Avery —

Thanks — that framing is helpful and much closer to what procurement will accept. The 3-month holdback + milestone release + capped buyout looks reasonable. Please send the redline and DocuSign, and let's take the 1:00pm PT slot today. I'll have our GC on the line for the call.

Attachments requested: the legal counter-redline you mentioned and the updated SOW.

Talk shortly —
Alexei

source.subject: BrightKey: Sizing and validation plan for dedicated inference capacity
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@brightkey.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-01-20T15:02:00-08:00
Subject: BrightKey: Sizing and validation plan for dedicated inference capacity

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and proposed validation approach so we have alignment before we lock a benchmarking window. Short version up top: you asked for a dedicated deployment that meets streaming SLOs for your assistant, with reserved throughput and predictable tail latency. Below I call out assumptions and two validation tracks we recommend.

Key assumptions from the call
- Model family: Llama 3-derived, ~33B (fine-tuned). You mentioned a quantized 8-bit variant may be the likely candidate for production.
- Typical sequence length: 1,024 tokens; occasional long tails up to 4,096 tokens for case escalations.
- Concurrency: target 350 active streaming sessions at peak, expected to scale to 500 over 3–6 months.
- Output profile: 150–250 tokens per reply on average; token generation is streaming (token-by-token) for UX.
- Desired SLO: p95 streaming end-to-end latency <= 300 ms when serving short responses (<= 150 tokens). p99 < 700 ms for longer responses is acceptable but lower is preferred.
- Deployment: private (customer VPC) in us-west1 with KMS/HSM for keys.

Proposed validation tracks
1) Microbench (baseline kernel & quantization check) — purpose: determine per-GPU steady-state token/s and latency for the target sequence length distribution. Run focused benchmarks against a trimmed prompt set (I attached the sample prompts).
   - Test vectors: short/hot prompts (<=150 tokens), medium (~250 tokens), long (~1k–4k tokens).
   - Hardware candidates: 1x H100 (largest single-device throughput), 2x A100-80GB (multi-device scaling with model sharding), and 4x L4-style instances (cost-optimized, smaller-memory).
   - Metrics captured: p50/p95/p99 latency (token generation and end-to-end), tokens/sec (sustained), GPU memory utilization, and KV-cache hit patterns.

2) Soak & concurrency test (production shaping) — purpose: validate reserved throughput under realistic concurrency, batching, and KV cache behavior.
   - Run a 4-hour soak at expected peak concurrency (350) and at 500 to validate headroom.
   - Include realistic inter-request arrival patterns, cold-starts for new sessions, and streaming turn-taking.
   - Check graceful degradation (fallback to CPU or lower-quality variant) and circuit-breaker behavior.

Quick hardware guidance (rough expectations)
- H100: best single-device throughput for large 33B models; lowest tail latency when model fits single device or with efficient tensor-parallelization. Higher cost and power draw.
- A100 (80GB): excellent balance; easier to horizontally scale for sharded setups; slightly higher per-token latency vs H100 at parity but better cost-per-token when using multiple devices.
- L4 / inference-focused cards: great price-performance for smaller quantized models or distilled variants; if you can stay on smaller model variants they can significantly reduce unit cost but may not meet latency for long sequences.

Next steps (what we need from you)
- Confirm the model binary you want us to benchmark (full name + quantization type).
- Provide a short sample of the training prompts or redaction-safe sample data (I attached a starter file).
- Confirm preferred benchmarking window: we can schedule a 4-hour soak any day next week (Jan 25–28) or the week after.
- Any compliance/credential details for your VPC access and KMS so we can prepare the private deployment template.

I attached the proposed runbook and the sample prompts. If this looks good I’ll provision the test harness and send a calendar invite for the first window.

Thanks —
Logan
Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0199

Attachments: call_recording_20270120.mp4 (recording), brightkey-sample-prompts.txt (prompt set), redwood-proposed-runbook.xlsx (runbook)

From: Elena Soto <elena.soto@brightkey.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightKey Infrastructure <infra@brightkey.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-01-20T18:46:00-08:00
Subject: Re: BrightKey: Sizing and validation plan for dedicated inference capacity

Thanks Logan — this is super helpful and aligns with what we discussed. A few clarifications from our side:

Model details
- Production target: "brightkey-llama3-33b-finetune" in an 8-bit quantized build (we're testing qat and static int8). I can export the exact artifact for benchmarking.

Traffic profile refinements
- Average concurrent streaming sessions during business hours: ~320; peak up to 500 during campaign spikes.
- Median reply size: 170 tokens; ~8% of requests exceed 1k tokens.
- We need streaming token latency such that UI latency (first-token+stream) feels continuous — p95 <= 300ms is the right bar.

Security & infra
- We'll need a private deployment in us-west1, peered into our infra. We use CloudKMS; we can stand up a transient vault for the test window. Infra will coordinate access.

Other asks
- Can you provide a ballpark cost delta for H100 vs A100 sizing so our finance folks can start a quick review?
- We would like the initial microbench first (to pick a hardware class) and then the soak.

I'll loop infra (infra@brightkey.com) to provide network/KMS details and export the model artifact this afternoon. If we can get a test window on Jan 26 that would be ideal.

— Elena
Elena Soto
Head of Product | BrightKey Diagnostics
elena.soto@brightkey.com

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-01-20T19:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: BrightKey: Sizing and validation plan for dedicated inference capacity

Looping in Raf to prep a concise hardware/cost delta we can send Elena. Logan — please flag any constraints (procurement lead time for H100 inventory, private deployment ops checklist). I want us to be ready to move to a paid pilot quickly if microbench is green.

Marissa
Director, Sales — Enterprise | Redwood Inference

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@brightkey.com>, BrightKey Infrastructure <infra@brightkey.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-01-21T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: BrightKey: Sizing and validation plan for dedicated inference capacity

Thanks Elena — Jan 26 works. Action items below, I’ll send the calendar invite with the runbook attached and the test harness links.

Action items (owner / due)
- BrightKey: provide model artifact (brightkey-llama3-33b-finetune-8bit.tar) and a 1–2k sample of production traffic (Elena / infra — EOD 2027-01-21).
- Redwood: spin up microbench candidates (H100 x1, A100 x2, L4 x4 profiles), baseline kernel/quantization check, and share a short cost delta table (Rafael — EOD 2027-01-24).
- Both: confirm network peering and KMS access scope for the private test (infra@brightkey.com / Redwood infra) — asap.

Bench window: Jan 26 09:00–13:00 PT (4 hours) — tentative. I’ll create the invite and we can refine the exact times after infra confirms.

If there are any compliance documents you want signed before we run the harness, send them over and we’ll prioritize.

Calendar & links: https://hubspot.company/HS-84219 (deal), https://drive.redwood/shared/brightkey/sizing-playbook

See you Friday —
Logan
