Looping in Raf to prep a concise hardware/cost delta we can send Elena. Logan — please flag any constraints (procurement lead time for H100 inventory, private deployment ops checklist). I want us to be ready to move to a paid pilot quickly if microbench is green.

Marissa
Director, Sales — Enterprise | Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@brightkey.com>, BrightKey Infrastructure <infra@brightkey.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-01-21T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: BrightKey: Sizing and validation plan for dedicated inference capacity

Thanks Elena — Jan 26 works. Action items below, I’ll send the calendar invite with the runbook attached and the test harness links.

Action items (owner / due)
- BrightKey: provide model artifact (brightkey-llama3-33b-finetune-8bit.tar) and a 1–2k sample of production traffic (Elena / infra — EOD 2027-01-21).
- Redwood: spin up microbench candidates (H100 x1, A100 x2, L4 x4 profiles), baseline kernel/quantization check, and share a short cost delta table (Rafael — EOD 2027-01-24).
- Both: confirm network peering and KMS access scope for the private test (infra@brightkey.com / Redwood infra) — asap.

Bench window: Jan 26 09:00–13:00 PT (4 hours) — tentative. I’ll create the invite and we can refine the exact times after infra confirms.

If there are any compliance documents you want signed before we run the harness, send them over and we’ll prioritize.

Calendar & links: https://hubspot.company/HS-84219 (deal), https://drive.redwood/shared/brightkey/sizing-playbook

See you Friday —
Logan

source.subject: AP onboarding & PO / VAT coordination — MediAssist vendor setup
messages:
From: Sophie Grant <sophie.grant@mediassist.co.uk>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@mediassist.co.uk
Date: 2027-01-10T09:12:00Z
Subject: AP onboarding & PO/Tax steps for Redwood Inference (MediAssist)

Hi Olga,

Thanks for engaging — we're ready to register Redwood as a vendor in our AP system. To move quickly our AP team needs:

- Official vendor/legal name to appear on the supplier record
- Company registration / VAT ID (for EU reverse-charge processing)
- Bank details (IBAN / BIC) and remit instructions
- Copies of any tax/VAT certificates you rely on (self-cert for reverse charge)
- Confirmation whether a customer PO will always be issued (we can enforce PO numbers)
- Preferred invoice file format (PDF, XML, CSV) and whether invoices can be submitted without a PO for subscription/usage billing

Also, do you accept our self-billing arrangement (we generate the invoice on your behalf) or do you require standard vendor-issued invoices? If self-billing is acceptable, please send your standard header fields so our template can be populated.

We'd like to complete registration by Jan 21 to not delay January invoicing. I've copied AP and procurement.

Best,
Sophie Grant
Procurement Lead — MediAssist Ltd
S: +44 203 555 0124

--
Attachment: (none in this mail)

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sophie Grant <sophie.grant@mediassist.co.uk>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-01-11T14:05:00Z
Subject: Re: AP onboarding & PO/Tax steps for Redwood Inference (MediAssist)

Hi Sophie — thanks for the clear list. Sending our AP onboarding packet and the items you asked for. Attachments included below. Quick answers first:

- Legal name: Redwood Inference, Inc. (we use DBAs on invoices if requested; we can include "Redwood" and full legal name in the vendor record)
- VAT / Tax: We are registered for VAT in the UK/EU as applicable. See attached EU-vat-self-cert.pdf for the certificate.
- Bank remit: IBAN and BIC included in banking-remit-reference.csv; if you require an intermediate payee or local collection account let me know.
- PO requirement: We can accept both PO-driven workflows and a small number of PO-less subscription invoices, but if PO is a hard validation in your AP system we request that subscription/usage invoices include a pseudo-PO or contract reference to avoid rejects.
- Self-billing: We are open to MediAssist self-billing for SaaS consumption invoices provided a signed self-bill agreement is in place. If your team sends the signed template we will countersign.
- Preferred invoice format: PDF is fine for official invoices; for bulk or automated ingestion we can provide an XML/CSV extract.

Next steps (proposed):
1) You confirm the vendor record name and remit contact in your portal (or send a vendor ID if you create records yourselves).
2) MediAssist uploads the self-bill template (if using self-bill) and we return a countersigned copy.
3) We'll provide a sample invoice header (see packet) and a test invoice to your AP inbox for ingestion test.

I've attached: redwood-ap-onboarding-packet_v2.pdf (AP packet, sample invoice header, required fields), eu-vat-self-cert.pdf, banking-remit-reference.csv.

If you want I can also create a short mutual action plan and schedule a 20-minute call to walk your AP team through field mapping.

Thanks — Olga
Olga Petrov
Senior Partnerships, Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com
+1 415 555 0189

Attachments: redwood-ap-onboarding-packet_v2.pdf, eu-vat-self-cert.pdf, banking-remit-reference.csv

From: Sophie Grant <sophie.grant@mediassist.co.uk>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: MediAssist AP <accounts-payable@mediassist.co.uk>, procurement@mediassist.co.uk
Date: 2027-01-12T08:47:00Z
Subject: Re: AP onboarding & PO/Tax steps for Redwood Inference (MediAssist)

Hi Olga — appreciate the packet. A couple of follow-ups before we kick off portal registration: 

1) For subscription billing: can Redwood accept PO-less invoices if they include our contract reference and start/end dates? Or do you require a PO number in all cases?
2) We've attached our self-bill template (MediAssist-self-bill-template.xlsx). Can you confirm the mapping for the following fields:
   - Supplier legal name
   - Supplier VAT ID
   - Invoice number (if you create) or our generated number (self-bill)
   - Tax treatment code (reverse-charge / exempt)
3) Your VAT certificate lists a UK registration and a separate EU VAT ID. We also need confirmation of the country of supply for each invoice (we're handling reverse-charge depending on the destination).

Also, AP asked for a scanned copy of an authorized signatory for the self-bill agreement (name + title). I've attached our template.

Thanks,
Sophie

--
Attachment: MediAssist-self-bill-template.xlsx

On 2027-01-11 14:05, Olga Petrov wrote:
> Hi Sophie — thanks for the clear list. Sending our AP onboarding packet and the items you asked for. Attachments included below.
> ...

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-01-12T09:10:00Z
Subject: Fwd: AP onboarding & PO/Tax steps for Redwood Inference (MediAssist)

Laura / Rafael — forwarding the request from MediAssist. They want to set Redwood up as a vendor and are asking whether we can do self-billing for subscription invoices and accept PO-less submissions when contract references are present. They also included their self-bill template and asked for a signed authorized signatory.

Key asks:
- Confirm if finance will accept self-bill arrangement (we usually do, with signed agreement)
- Confirm whether we can allow PO-less subscription invoices (we currently allow exceptions if contract ref provided)
- Quick check on bank remit CSV format (they will ingest IBAN/BIC) — Laura can you confirm the CSV fields match our bank?

I've attached their template in the forwarded message.

Thanks — Olga

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Sophie Grant <sophie.grant@mediassist.co.uk>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-01-12T08:47:00Z
Subject: AP onboarding & PO/Tax steps for Redwood Inference (MediAssist)
(see original)

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-01-12T10:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Fwd: AP onboarding & PO/Tax steps for Redwood Inference (MediAssist)

Olga / Rafael — quick reply:

- We can accept a self-bill arrangement provided their template is standard and an authorized signatory is added. Please ask Sophie to return the signed template; we'll countersign.
- PO-less subscription invoices: we allow exceptions where a contract reference or 'subscription id' is present — finance will not process invoices without either a PO or the agreed ref.
- Banking CSV: the fields shown (account_name, iban, bic, currency, remit_reference) look fine; Rafael please confirm the payee tag matches our ledger (we use RDW-LEGAL-ENTITY-001).

I'll prepare a one-page signatory page for the self-bill and loop in legal if needed.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Head of Finance, Redwood Inference

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sophie Grant <sophie.grant@mediassist.co.uk>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-01-15T11:22:00Z
Subject: Re: AP onboarding & PO/Tax steps for Redwood Inference (MediAssist) — next steps and signed agreement

Hi Sophie — thanks for your patience. Attached is our countersigned self-bill agreement and the AP packet again for your portal. Summary and next steps below: 

1) Vendor record: please create vendor as: Redwood Inference, Inc. — VAT ID: GB999 1234 56 (EU VAT cert attached). Our ledger payee tag: RDW-LEGAL-ENTITY-001.
2) Banking: IBAN and BIC are in banking-remit-reference.csv; please use remit_reference = "RDW-INV-{invoice_number}".
3) PO policy: If a PO is present, include PO in invoice header. For subscription/usage invoices you may send without a PO provided the invoice includes our contract reference or subscription id (see packet).
4) Self-bill: countersigned self-bill attached (signed by Laura Bennett — Head of Finance). We will accept MediAssist-generated invoice numbers under this agreement.
5) Test ingestion: please send one test invoice (PDF) and a CSV test file to accounts-payable@mediassist.co.uk and cc olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com.

If you'd like, let's do a 20-minute ingestion test on Jan 18. Otherwise we'll consider vendor setup complete once your portal shows the vendor ID and we've received a test invoice.

Thanks again — looking forward to a smooth onboarding.

Best,
Olga Petrov
Senior Partnerships, Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: signed-self-bill-agreement.pdf, redwood-ap-onboarding-packet_v2.pdf

Hi Sophie — thanks for your patience. Attached is our countersigned self-bill agreement and the AP packet again for your portal. Summary and next steps below: 

1) Vendor record: please create vendor as: Redwood Inference, Inc. — VAT ID: GB999 1234 56 (EU VAT cert attached). Our ledger payee tag: RDW-LEGAL-ENTITY-001.
2) Banking: IBAN and BIC are in banking-remit-reference.csv; please use remit_reference = "RDW-INV-{invoice_number}".
3) PO policy: If a PO is present, include PO in invoice header. For subscription/usage invoices you may send without a PO provided the invoice includes our contract reference or subscription id (see packet).
4) Self-bill: countersigned self-bill attached (signed by Laura Bennett — Head of Finance). We will accept MediAssist-generated invoice numbers under this agreement.
5) Test ingestion: please send one test invoice (PDF) and a CSV test file to accounts-payable@mediassist.co.uk and cc olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com.

If you'd like, let's do a 20-minute ingestion test on Jan 18. Otherwise we'll consider vendor setup complete once your portal shows the vendor ID and we've received a test invoice.

Thanks again — looking forward to a smooth onboarding.

Best,
Olga Petrov
Senior Partnerships, Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: signed-self-bill-agreement.pdf, redwood-ap-onboarding-packet_v2.pdf

source.subject: PO mapping, invoice slab/minimum and net term options
source.thread:
From: Sara Liu <sara.liu@acumenhealth.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@acumenhealth.com
Date: Sat, 10 Jan 2027 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: Redwood SOW / PO requirements and billing cadence

Hi Grace / Vanessa,

Thanks for the demo last week — procurement asked me to confirm a few billing items so we can raise a PO. Can you confirm the following for Acumen Health (RDW-4521)?

- Minimum invoice slab / billing granularity: can invoices be consolidated monthly across multiple subscriptions, or do you require per-SKU invoices?
- PO mapping: we need a line-item mapping so our AP system can match each PO line to an internal cost center (they prefer a single PO with multiple lines rather than multiple POs). Will Redwood accept a single PO with multiple line items referencing different Redwood SKUs?
- Net terms: Acumen's standard is Net30 but procurement sometimes needs Net45 for new vendors — what flexibility do you have?
- Attachment request: they asked for the latest SOW and the pricing slab table to attach to the PO.

Appreciate a quick response — procurement needs this to complete their vendor setup by next Tuesday.

Best,
Sara Liu
Director, Vendor Enablement
Acumen Health

---

From: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Sara Liu <sara.liu@acumenhealth.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Jan 2027 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Redwood SOW / PO requirements and billing cadence

Sara — thanks for the clear list. High level answers below; Vanessa and finance (cc'd) will confirm final wording and attach the files.

1) Invoice consolidation: We can produce a consolidated monthly invoice that includes multiple SKUs/line items. We can also emit separate invoices per PO line if your AP needs separate documents — both are supported.

2) PO mapping: Yes, we accept a single PO with multiple line items. To make matching easier we ask that each PO line include the Redwood SKU and the corresponding purchase order line reference (e.g., PO#12345 - Line 2 = SKU RDW-API-ENTERPRISE). We'll provide a CSV mapping as part of onboarding.

3) Net terms: Standard practice is Net30 for new enterprise customers on hosted contracts. We can consider Net45 for first-year pilots if a purchase commitment tier is met (e.g., annual prepay or minimum monthly commitment). Vanessa — can you propose a one-off concession for Acumen if they commit to the 12-month slab?

4) Attachments: attaching SOW and pricing sheet (vanessa will push final versions).

If procurement needs specific ERP codes or a PO template, please send an example and we'll map fields directly.

Cheers,
Grace O'Connor
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference

Attachments (stubs): SOW_Redwood_Acumen_v2.pdf (PDF, 320KB), Redwood-pricing-sheet-annual-slabs.xlsx (Excel, 45KB)

---

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Sara Liu <sara.liu@acumenhealth.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Jan 2027 10:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Redwood SOW / PO requirements and billing cadence

Sara — appreciate the checklist. Quick practical note on pricing and packaging in case procurement needs to justify to their finance team: if Acumen commits to the "Standard Annual Slab - 12m" (see attached pricing sheet) we can offer a 7.5% concession on list and approve Net45 for the first invoice cycle. Conditions:

- Deal must be signed by 31-Jan-2027
- One consolidated monthly invoice accepted by Acumen AP (we'll include PO line mapping in invoice line descriptions)
- Redwood requires PO# on invoice header and each invoice line should include the SKU reference for automated matching

I will attach the slightly revised SOW that includes the billing clause and an appendix showing how we format invoice lines for PO matching. Rafael — can you confirm internally on the Net45 pilot concession?

Next steps for procurement:
- Sara: confirm which cost centers (ERP codes) map to each SKU and whether you prefer one consolidated invoice per month or per-product.
- If consolidated monthly invoice: confirm the billing day (e.g., 1st of month or Net terms counted from invoice date).

Happy to hop on a short call if that will move things faster.

Best,
Vanessa Ortiz
Account Exec, Redwood Inference

Attachment stubs referenced: SOW_Redwood_Acumen_v2.pdf, Redwood-pricing-sheet-annual-slabs.xlsx

---

From: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>
To: Sara Liu <sara.liu@acumenhealth.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Jan 2027 16:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Redwood SOW / PO requirements and billing cadence

Sara / team — Finance has reviewed Vanessa's proposal. We can approve the Net45 for the first invoice cycle under the terms Vanessa outlined with two clarifications from our side:

1) Minimum invoice slab: Redwood's billing engine requires a minimum charge slab of $2,500 per invoice for enterprise support and reserved capacity line items. If the month's usage falls below that slab we will issue a consolidated statement showing zero-balance line items; however for AR purposes the invoice will still show the minimum recurring charge unless otherwise agreed and pre-paid. If this is an issue for Acumen's AP manager we can discuss prepay or credit arrangements.

2) PO metadata: please ensure the PO includes the following fields so our system can map correctly: PO Number, PO Line ID (1..n), SKU, Quantity, Unit Price, Customer Cost Center Code. We will return a sample CSV mapping after signature.

Rafael Mendes
Head of Finance, Redwood Inference

---

On Tue, 12 Jan 2027 16:42:00 -0800, Sara Liu <sara.liu@acumenhealth.com> wrote:
> Thanks Rafael — that slab is workable if we can add a short clause allowing mid-year reconciliation if actual consumption is materially lower than forecast (procurement asks for a 90-day reconciliation window).
> 
> I'll circulate that internally and confirm ERP codes by EOD Thursday.
> 
> Sara

[End of thread]

title: PO mapping, invoice slab/minimum and net term options

source.subject: Initiative charter — first draft + workshop to align eval & close timeline
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Albright <jalbright@averysolutions.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Jan 2027 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Initiative charter — first draft + workshop to align eval & close timeline

Hi Jordan —

Appended is the initial "initiative charter" draft (Google Doc) and a short slide deck that lays out a proposed evaluation cadence and a close-acceleration timeline. The goal here is to land a concise mutual action plan that:
- Defines success metrics for the POC,
- Identifies the minimal test surface (data slices / API routes),
- Sets a 30/60/90 evaluation cadence with owners,
- Lays out procurement/contract milestones so you can hit Q1 close.

Could you take a look (link below) and tell me:
1) Which stakeholders on your side should join a 60–90 minute alignment workshop?
2) Any must-have compliance evidence we should bring (SOC2 artifacts, data retention notes)?
3) If the proposed timeline feels feasible from your side?

Proposed workshop slots next week:
- Thu Jan 14 — 10:00–11:30 PT (preferred)
- Fri Jan 15 — 09:00–10:30 PT

Links / attachments:
- Initiative charter (draft): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1aBcDeF-example-charter
- Evaluation timeline slides: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1XyZ-example-slides/view
- Attachments: evaluation-timeline-v1.pptx

We’ll have Rafael do a short 10–15 minute technical demo and Marissa will walk through onboarding milestones and success metrics during the workshop. If the slots look good I’ll send a calendar invite and a short pre-read.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | (415) 555-0123

----
Attachment stubs: evaluation-timeline-v1.pptx (slides deck, 6 slides)

From: Jordan Albright <jalbright@averysolutions.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@averysolutions.com
Date: Mon, 11 Jan 2027 08:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initiative charter — first draft + workshop to align eval & close timeline

Ben —

Thanks for the draft and clear asks. This looks like the right structure. A few quick items from my side before we lock a workshop:
- Thu Jan 14 at 10:00 PT works for me. Can we keep it tight (60m) and prioritize milestone owners + a short demo?
- On compliance: we need SOC2 Type II evidence and a short note on data retention + logs for the POC (our security team will want this up front).
- For the POC surface, our priority is low-latency summarization for <redacted> pipeline and embedding generation for search. Rafael’s demo will be helpful.

Attendees I’ll bring: me (ML Ops), Priya S. (Prod Eng), and our Security lead (cc’d via ops@averysolutions.com — will loop in person). If you want any additional folks from our Product team I’ll add them after the invite.

Please send the calendar and let me know if you prefer a 60m focused agenda.

Best,
Jordan Albright
Head of ML Ops | Avery Solutions
jalbright@averysolutions.com

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Albright <jalbright@averysolutions.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Jan 2027 09:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initiative charter — first draft + workshop to align eval & close timeline

Jordan —

Perfect — locking Thu Jan 14, 10:00–11:00 PT (60m) and will keep the agenda tight:
- 00:00–00:05 — Introductions & desired outcomes (Ben)
- 00:05–00:20 — Success metrics & acceptance criteria (Marissa)
- 00:20–00:35 — Technical demo (Rafael) — focused on latency/embedding surface
- 00:35–00:50 — Compliance & evidence Q&A (Laura)
- 00:50–01:00 — Next steps & owners (Ben)

Jordan — per your ask, Laura will bring SOC2 artifact pointers and a brief note on data retention + audit log capabilities we can share during the meeting. I’ve also updated the charter doc to add a short compliance appendix and a procurement milestone row (Doc link below). Please add any other attendees to the calendar once you confirm.

Updated links:
- Initiative charter (updated): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1aBcDeF-example-charter
- Slides (demo + timeline): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1XyZ-example-slides/view

I’ll send the invite now. Rafael — can you be prepared to show the embedding latency numbers and a quick inference trace for the summarization flow?

Thanks —
Ben

On Fri, Jan 9, 2027 at 10:12 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Jordan —
>
> Appended is the initial "initiative charter" draft (Google Doc) and a short slide deck that lays out a proposed evaluation cadence and a close-acceleration timeline. The goal here is to land a concise mutual action plan that:
> - Defines success metrics for the POC,
> - Identifies the minimal test surface (data slices / API routes),
> - Sets a 30/60/90 evaluation cadence with owners,
> - Lays out procurement/contract milestones so you can hit Q1 close.
>
> Could you take a look (link below) and tell me:...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Jan 2027 16:23:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Initiative charter — first draft + workshop to align eval & close timeline

Ben / Rafael / Laura —

Quick internal note so we show up aligned for Thu: 
- Agenda: I’ll lead success metrics and acceptance criteria; Rafael will own the short demo; Laura will handle compliance artifacts.
- Pre-read: I trimmed the timeline slide to highlight the 2-week POC window + procurement gating items (see attachment).
- Logistics: I’ll own the follow-up MAP doc updates (I’ll take Jordan’s attendee list and fold any additional owners into the charter).
- Ask from Rafael: please include 1–2 real latency runs to reference during the demo (p95 and median) and a short notes slide on KV cache behavior for long contexts.

Attachment stub: evaluation-timeline-v1.pptx (updated: highlighted POC window)

See you all Thursday.

Cheers,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

source.subject: BridgePoint private connectivity orchestration pulse
messages:
From: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir.shah@bridgepoint.health>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: BridgePoint private connectivity orchestration pulse

Samir,

Thanks for the kickoff call earlier — putting a short pulse here to capture next steps for the private deployment (VPC peering, private endpoints, and on‑prem appliance planning). Goal is to agree on owners and a short two‑week implementation sprint plan.

Immediate asks:
- Can you confirm the VPC CIDR range you want us to peer with and whether you require Azure ExpressRoute or AWS Direct Connect?
- Please share any firewall egress allowlist constraints for our control plane IPs.
- Who on your side owns the network change window (name + email)?

Internal owners I've assigned:
- Rafael Mendes: networking runbook and peering ops
- Grace Oconnor: private endpoint routing and health checks
- Monica Patel: on‑prem appliance staging and install checklist
- Aditya Rao: rollout cadence and customer comms

Attachments: network‑diagram‑v1.pdf (draft), implementation‑checklist.xlsx (working). I'll follow up with a proposed 90‑minute deep‑dive workshop next week.

Thanks,
Michael

--
Michael Grant
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
michael.grant@redwoodinference.com
+1 415 555 0123

(attachment stubs: network-diagram-v1.pdf, implementation-checklist.xlsx)
From: Samir Shah <samir.shah@bridgepoint.health>
To: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tracy Lin <tracy@netswitch.net>, James Park <j.park@networkops.io>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 11:09:00 -0800
Subject: Re: BridgePoint private connectivity orchestration pulse

Michael — thanks, confirming a few things from our side:

- VPC: 10.200.0.0/16 (primary), we will create a peering VPC in the same region.

source.subject: Next steps: buying path, stakeholder map & security scope kickoff
messages:
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
To: Tracy Wu <tracy.wu@nimblehealth.com>, Martin Cole <martin.cole@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 15:10:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap + next steps (stakeholders, criteria, security kickoff)

Thanks again for the call earlier — good conversation and appreciate the clarity on priorities. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we all have the same picture: 

- Decision criteria (from Martin): latency < 200ms P95 for patient-facing flows; embeddings accuracy for semantic search; predictable monthly spend for budget planning.
- Economic buyer: Martin Cole (confirmed).
- Security/scope owner: Tracy Wu (security) — Tracy will own the questionnaire and evidence intake.
- Procurement/contract: Lena Ortiz (procurement) is the contact for PO/terms.

Immediate asks / proposed next steps:
1) Tracy: can you confirm the systems you want included in the security review (prod API, analytics pipeline, embeddings store, logging/retention)? A short list will let us scope the questionnaire correctly.
2) We'll attach our standard security intake questionnaire and a one-pager on our security posture (SOC2 scope, data handling). Please return the completed questionnaire or mark items out-of-scope.
3) Rafael (SE) — can join the security kickoff to review architecture, tokenization, and KV cache behavior. Proposed window: Thu 12/16 10:00–11:00 PT.
4) Marissa — I'll loop you for procurement cadence and any draft SOW templates Nimble prefers.

Attachments: Nimble_Security_Questionnaire_20261214.pdf (blank intake), Nimble_Buying_Criteria_and_Roles_20261214.xlsx (our working stakeholder map), Redwood_Security_Profile_1pager.pdf

If that looks right, Tracy — could you send a one-line confirmation of systems in-scope and whether your team prefers an initial kickoff or to return the questionnaire first? Happy to be flexible.

Thanks,
Jasmine Liu
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jasmine.liu@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0124
From: Tracy Wu <tracy.wu@nimblehealth.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
Cc: Martin Cole <martin.cole@nimblehealth.com>, Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@nimblehealth.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2026 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + next steps (stakeholders, criteria, security kickoff)

Thanks, Jasmine — good recap. Confirming scope and a couple clarifying Qs: 

Systems in scope (we want these assessed):
- Production patient-facing API (k8s cluster, GKE)
- Analytics ETL pipeline (Spark jobs, staging S3 buckets)
- Embeddings store (managed vector DB, logs retention)
- Audit logs & access controls (where logs are stored and who has access)

Questions: 
1) For the embeddings store: does Redwood persist raw input text anywhere after tokenization? We need the exact data flow to validate data residency controls.
2) Timeline: procurement is aiming for vendor approval by EOW 12/23; security needs a kickoff + initial intake by 12/17 to meet that. Is that feasible?

We'd prefer an initial kickoff (short call) so the security team can ask architecture questions in realtime, then we'll work through the questionnaire offline. Proposed kickoff slot: Thu 12/16 10:00 PT (works for our team).

-Tracy Wu
Senior Security Engineer | NimbleHealth
tracy.wu@nimblehealth.com
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
To: Tracy Wu <tracy.wu@nimblehealth.com>, Martin Cole <martin.cole@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2026 10:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + next steps (stakeholders, criteria, security kickoff)

Perfect — thanks for the scope confirmation, Tracy. 

Quick answers: 
- Embeddings persistence: Redwood does not retain raw customer input beyond the lifetime required for generation and caching unless configured by the customer. By default we cache kv-prefixes for active sessions (configurable TTL) and encrypt KV at rest. We'll have Rafael bring architecture diagrams and our data-flow diagram to the kickoff to walk through exact storage/TTL behaviors.

title: Next steps: buying path, stakeholder map & security scope kickoff
preview:
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
To: Tracy Wu <tracy.wu@nimblehealth.com>, Martin Cole <martin.cole@nimblehealth.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 15:10:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap + next steps (stakeholders, criteria, security kickoff)

Thanks again for the call earlier — good conversation and appreciate the clarity on priorities. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we all have the same picture: 

- Decision criteria (from Martin): latency < 200ms P95 for patient-facing flows; embeddings accuracy for semantic search; predictable monthly spend for budget planning.
- Economic buyer: Martin Cole (confirmed).
- Security/scope owner: Tracy Wu (security) — Tracy will own the questionnaire and evidence intake.
- Procurement/contract: Lena Ortiz (procurement) is the contact for PO/terms.

Immediate asks / proposed next steps:
1) Tracy: can you confirm the systems you want included in the security review (prod API, analytics pipeline, embeddings store, logging/retention)? A short list will let us scope the questionnaire correctly.
2) We'll attach our standard security intake questionnaire and a one-pager on our security posture (SOC2 scope, data handling). Please return the completed questionnaire or mark items out-of-scope.
3) Rafael (SE) — can join the security kickoff to review architecture, tokenization, and KV cache behavior. Proposed window: Thu 12/16 10:00–11:00 PT.
4) Marissa — I'll loop you for procurement cadence and any draft SOW templates Nimble prefers.

Attachments: Nimble_Security_Questionnaire_20261214.pdf (blank intake), Nimble_Buying_Criteria_and_Roles_20261214.xlsx (our working stakeholder map), Redwood_Security_Profile_1pager.pdf

If that looks right, Tracy — could you send a one-line confirmation of systems in-scope and whether your team prefers an initial kickoff or to return the questionnaire first? Happy to be flexible.

jasmine.liu@redwood.ai

Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2026 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + next steps (stakeholders, criteria, security kickoff)

Thanks, Jasmine — good recap. Confirming scope and a couple clarifying Qs: 

Systems in scope (we want these assessed):
- Production patient-facing API (k8s cluster, GKE)
- Analytics ETL pipeline (Spark jobs, staging S3 buckets)
- Embeddings store (managed vector DB, logs retention)
- Audit logs & access controls (where logs are stored and who has access)

Questions: 
1) For the embeddings store: does Redwood persist raw input text anywhere after tokenization? We need the exact data flow to validate data residency controls.
2) Timeline: procurement is aiming for vendor approval by EOW 12/23; security needs a kickoff + initial intake by 12/17 to meet that. Is that feasible?

We'd prefer an initial kickoff (short call) so the security team can ask architecture questions in realtime, then we'll work through the questionnaire offline. Proposed kickoff slot: Thu 12/16 10:00 PT (works for our team).

-Tracy Wu
Senior Security Engineer | NimbleHealth
tracy.wu@nimblehealth.com

source.subject: Architecture roadmap check-in — Dec 12 session unattended; reschedule & office hours
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam@orbitanalytics.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-10T16:00:00-08:00
Subject: Architecture roadmap check-in (Dec 12) — invite and agenda

Hi Liam,

Looking forward to our technical deep-dive on Dec 12 at 10:00 AM PT. We've put together a focused 45-minute agenda to review the integration points for model serving, expected throughput, and Redis-backed KV cache patterns. High level:

- 10:00 — quick intro & success criteria
- 10:10 — model serving topology options (single-tenant vs multi-tenant)
- 10:25 — caching/batching considerations and latency targets
- 10:35 — rollout/monitoring plan and next steps

Attached: redwood-arch-outline.pdf (diagram + talking points).
I've invited Rafael and Grace from our infra and solutions teams. If you'd like a pre-read or a short list of telemetry we'll need, let me know and I can add it.

Calendar invite: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abcdef12345

Best,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-arch-outline.pdf
From: Liam O'Connor <liam@orbitanalytics.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-12T10:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Architecture roadmap check-in (Dec 12) — invite and agenda

Hey Aisha — so sorry, we've had an unexpected incident this morning and the infra team had to scramble. I couldn't make the call. Can we reschedule? Also would be great if there's a short office-hours slot we can drop into later this week for specific configuration Qs.

Apologies again — Liam
Liam O'Connor
CTO, Orbit Analytics
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-12T11:00:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Re: Architecture roadmap check-in (Dec 12) — attendee couldn't join

Raf / Grace — Liam pinged that they were pulled into an incident and couldn't join. No notes were taken yet. I'm thinking we should:

1) Offer two reschedule windows (Dec 15 or Dec 16 mornings PT)
2) Offer a 30-minute "office hours" block where they can drop into a room for configuration Qs (we can do 1 hour on Thurs morning and split if needed)
3) Send a concise pre-read + the rollout spreadsheet so they have something actionable before the next sync (I'll attach the XLSX and short checklist)

If that sounds good I'll send the options and attach proposed-rollout-plan.xlsx. Rafael — can you prepare 2 slides about expected throughput by model size? Grace — please have a short checklist for telemetry/permissions we need from their side.

Thanks — Aisha
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam@orbitanalytics.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-12T14:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Architecture roadmap check-in (Dec 12) — reschedule options + office hours

Hi Liam — no worries, thanks for the note and hope the incident is under control.

Would either of these work for a 45-minute deep dive?
- Tue, Dec 15 — 10:00–10:45 AM PT
- Wed, Dec 16 — 9:00–9:45 AM PT

If you prefer an unscheduled touchpoint, we can host an open office-hours slot on Thu, Dec 17, 9:00–10:00 AM PT where Rafael and I will be available for configuration or telemetry questions.

I've attached a short rollout plan (proposed-rollout-plan.xlsx) so you can see the items we'll discuss and pick priorities ahead of the meeting. If either of the two reschedule slots works, reply with your preferred time and I'll send an updated invite and include a recording option.

Also, if you want Sergio from platform to attend for the infra integration piece, tell me and I'll loop him in.

Attachments: proposed-rollout-plan.xlsx
Calendar (pick): https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abcdef12345

Best,
Aisha
From: Liam O'Connor <liam@orbitanalytics.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-13T09:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Architecture roadmap check-in (Dec 12) — reschedule options + office hours

Thanks Aisha — Dec 15 at 10:00 AM PT works for us. Can you please include Sergio Alvarez on the invite? He owns on-prem connectivity and will have some specifics about our VPC setup. Also Rafael's throughput slides would be helpful.

Liam
On Sun, Dec 12, 2026 at 2:30 PM, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Liam — no worries, thanks for the note and hope the incident is under control.
> 
> Would either of these work for a 45-minute deep dive?
> - Tue, Dec 15 — 10:00–10:45 AM PT
> - Wed, Dec 16 — 9:00–9:45 AM PT
> 
> If you prefer an unscheduled touchpoint, we can host an open office-hours slot on Thu, Dec 17, 9:00–10:00 AM PT where Rafael and I will be available for configuration or telemetry questions.
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam@orbitanalytics.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio_alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael_mendes@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-13T11:22:00-08:00
Subject: Confirmed: Architecture roadmap check-in — Tue Dec 15, 10:00 AM PT

Thanks Liam — confirmed for Tue Dec 15, 10:00–10:45 AM PT. I just sent an updated calendar invite (link above) and attached Rafael's throughput slides to the invite. Sergio — welcome, and thanks for joining; we'll allocate 10–15 minutes to discuss VPC/firewall rules and peering during the session.

Quick pre-read for the group: the two attachments (redwood-arch-outline.pdf and proposed-rollout-plan.xlsx) summarize what we'll cover. Please flag any line items you want us to prioritize during the meeting.

See you all Tuesday — Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-arch-outline.pdf, proposed-rollout-plan.xlsx

Looking forward to our technical deep-dive on Dec 12 at 10:00 AM PT. We've put together a focused 45-minute agenda to review the integration points for model serving, expected throughput, and Redis-backed KV cache patterns. High level:

- 10:00 — quick intro & success criteria
- 10:10 — model serving topology options (single-tenant vs multi-tenant)
- 10:25 — caching/batching considerations and latency targets
- 10:35 — rollout/monitoring plan and next steps

Attached: redwood-arch-outline.pdf (diagram + talking points).
I've invited Rafael and Grace from our infra and solutions teams. If you'd like a pre-read or a short list of telemetry we'll need, let me know and I can add it.

Calendar invite: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abcdef12345

Best,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

Apologies again — Liam
Liam O'Connor

1) Offer two reschedule windows (Dec 15 or Dec 16 mornings PT)
2) Offer a 30-minute "office hours" block where they can drop into a room for configuration Qs (we can do 1 hour on Thurs morning and split if needed)

- Wed, Dec 16 — 9:00–9:45 AM PT

source.subject: Recap + Engagement Playbook (next steps & decision criteria)
messages:
From: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lin <sarah@clearedge.ai>, Tom Barrett <tbarrett@clearedge.ai>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-10T14:03:00-08:00
Subject: Recap + Engagement Playbook (next steps & decision criteria)

Hi Sarah / Tom —

Thanks again for taking the time today. Quick recap from my side and a proposed engagement playbook to keep momentum. Please review and flag anything I missed.

Key pain points you shared:
- High inference cost and variable p95 latency on current self-hosted endpoints (spikes during batch jobs).
- Difficulty shipping models across teams due to inconsistent serving configs and cache management.
- Compliance requirement for selective on-prem handling of PII-sensitive workloads.

Stated goals (30–90 day horizons):
1) Pilot: validate a production config that meets p95 latency target and reduces per-token cost.
2) Security: confirm network/KMS approach for PII workflows.
3) Decision: leadership sign-off for Dedicated or Private deployment by mid-Jan.

Proposed Engagement Playbook (high level) — milestones & owners:
A. Technical deep-dive (Redwood SRE + ClearEdge infra) — owner: Vivek Kulkarni — target: 2026-12-14 — deliverable: pilot runbook + SLOs.
B. Pilot (2 weeks) — owner: ClearEdge engineering + Redwood devops — target start: 2026-12-20 — deliverable: pilot results (latency/cost dashboard).
C. Procurement & Security review — owner: Tom Barrett (procurement) + ClearEdge legal — target: 2027-01-06 — deliverable: SOW/contract draft and security checklist.
D. Exec decision / Commitment — owner: Sarah Lin — target: 2027-01-20 — decision: Dedicated vs Private + budget range.

Decision criteria (what success looks like):
- Performance: p95 inference latency <= 200ms for standard 512-token requests during pilot (99th percentile handled separately).
- Cost: demonstrable >25% reduction in per-token CPU/GPU-backed cost vs current baseline or within agreed budget cap.
- Security: KMS integration validated and network control pattern approved.

Attachments: ClearEdge_MAP_v1.xlsx (playbook with milestone table), redwood-pricing-summary.pdf (ballpark pricing tiers).

Requests / next steps for ClearEdge:
1) Confirm pilot stakeholders and a primary technical contact (name + email).
2) Confirm availability for 60–90min technical deep-dive on 2026-12-14 (we can be flexible).
3) Share any specific compliance docs you need (SOC 2, data retention, KMS requirements).

I’ll update the MAP and HubSpot after I hear back.

Thanks —
Olga
Olga Petrov
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
olga@redwood.com | (415) 555-0182

From: Sarah Lin <sarah@clearedge.ai>
To: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Barrett <tbarrett@clearedge.ai>
Date: 2026-12-10T17:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Recap + Engagement Playbook (next steps & decision criteria)

Olga —

Thanks for the clear summary. This aligns closely with what we discussed. A few clarifications and items from our side: 

- Priority pain: the high p95 latency is causing timeouts for our real-time annotation pipeline during peak windows (9–11am PST). Reducing tail latency is highest priority.
- Decision criteria additions: we need evidence that the proposed setup handles sustained 500 RPS bursts for short windows without degraded p95.
- Security: on-prem routing for PII is non-negotiable for certain endpoints; please confirm Redwood Private options and KMS key rotation support.

Procurement/legal: I’ve looped in Tom Barrett (procurement) — Tom, our aim is to get a pilot SOW scoped and pricing ballpark to present to finance by 2027-01-06.

Availability: we can do the technical deep-dive on 2026-12-14 10:00–11:30am PT. Primary tech contacts: Alicia Gomez (a.gomez@clearedge.ai) and Marcus Liu (m.liu@clearedge.ai).

Can you call out any non-negotiable blockers you foresee before the pilot (e.g., required infra access, specific firewall rules)?

Best,
Sarah
Sarah Lin
Head of Product | ClearEdge Analytics

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lin <sarah@clearedge.ai>, Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-11T09:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Recap + Engagement Playbook (next steps & decision criteria)

Thanks — quick note from the engineering side. I’ll lead the technical deep-dive and the pilot engineering plan. Initial notes and runbook items:

- Pilot profile: 2-week window, baseline canonical model (open-architecture) with Redwood runtime optimizations. We’ll use a small dedicated cluster to prevent noisy neighbor issues.
- Observability: we’ll deliver a dashboard with p50/p95/p99 latency, token throughput, GPU utilization, and per-request cost.
- KV/prefix caching: we’ll run a controlled experiment to enable prefix caching for the annotation workload to measure tail latency impact.
- Requirements from ClearEdge: a test dataset (anonymized) or a stable synthetic workload, firewall rule to allow outbound to our pilot endpoints, and an SSH bastion for ops (if using Private).

I attached a short agenda for the deep-dive (technical-deep-dive-agenda.pdf). For the sustained burst requirement Sarah mentioned, we can simulate 500 RPS bursts during the pilot and report SLO breach probability.

I’ll prepare a short runbook and the pilot config and share it in the Drive folder before the deep-dive.

— Vivek
Vivek Kulkarni
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
vivek@redwood.com

From: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lin <sarah@clearedge.ai>, Tom Barrett <tbarrett@clearedge.ai>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-12T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Updated Engagement Playbook & Confirmed Next Steps

On Fri, Dec 10, 2026 at 5:45 PM Sarah Lin <sarah@clearedge.ai> wrote:
> Olga —
>
> Thanks for the clear summary. This aligns closely with what we discussed. A few clarifications and items from our side: 
>
> - Priority pain: the high p95 latency is causing timeouts for our real-time annotation pipeline during peak windows (9–11am PST). Reducing tail latency is highest priority.

Thanks — I pulled Sarah’s comments into the attached MAP and tightened dates based on your availability. Below is the updated, actionable mutual action plan. Please confirm by EOD Monday so we can lock resources.

Updated Mutual Action Plan (milestones, owners, due dates):
1) Technical deep-dive — owner: Vivek Kulkarni (Redwood) + Alicia Gomez (ClearEdge) — due: 2026-12-14 — deliverable: pilot runbook (Drive link).
2) Pilot setup & smoke tests — owner: ClearEdge engineering + Redwood devops — due: 2026-12-20 (start) / 2026-12-31 (end) — deliverable: pilot metrics report.
3) Security & procurement checklist completed — owner: Tom Barrett (ClearEdge) + Rafael Mendes (Redwood legal enablement) — due: 2027-01-06 — deliverable: SOW draft + security signoff.
4) Pilot review & exec decision — owner: Sarah Lin — due: 2027-01-20 — deliverable: go/no-go and budget signoff.

Acceptance criteria (detailed):
- p95 latency <= 200ms for standard 512-token requests during typical load; validated during pilot and reported to dashboard.
- Successful simulation of 500 RPS bursts without sustained SLO violations (>5% breach window) during pilot.
- KMS integration validated for labeled PII endpoints; KMS key rotation documented.

Open questions / asks for ClearEdge:
- Confirm Alicia Gomez and Marcus Liu as primary technical leads (emails listed by Sarah).
- Confirm Tom Barrett is procurement lead and provide billing contact for the SOW.
- Confirm budget band for the pilot and any pre-approved procurement thresholds (e.g., approvals required > $50k).

Next concrete actions (what I’ll do):
- Update the MAP spreadsheet (attached: ClearEdge_MAP_v1.xlsx) and push to the Drive folder.
- Create HubSpot tasks for the technical deep-dive and pilot kickoff (link included).
- Prepare an initial SOW template for procurement and share with Rafael to align on legal language.

Please reply with a one-line confirmation on stakeholders and the 12/14 deep-dive slot and I’ll lock resources.

Thanks —
Olga
Olga Petrov
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
olga@redwood.com | (415) 555-0182

Attachments: ClearEdge_MAP_v1.xlsx, redwood-pricing-summary.pdf, technical-deep-dive-agenda.pdf

Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
vivek@redwood.com



From: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lin <sarah@clearedge.ai>, Tom Barrett <tbarrett@clearedge.ai>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-12T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Updated Engagement Playbook & Confirmed Next Steps

On Fri, Dec 10, 2026 at 5:45 PM Sarah Lin <sarah@clearedge.ai> wrote:
> Olga —
>
> Thanks for the clear summary. This aligns closely with what we discussed. A few clarifications and items from our side: 
>
> - Priority pain: the high p95 latency is causing timeouts for our real-time annotation pipeline during peak windows (9–11am PST). Reducing tail latency is highest priority.

Thanks — I pulled Sarah’s comments into the attached MAP and tightened dates based on your availability. Below is the updated, actionable mutual action plan. Please confirm by EOD Monday so we can lock resources.

Updated Mutual Action Plan (milestones, owners, due dates):
1) Technical deep-dive — owner: Vivek Kulkarni (Redwood) + Alicia Gomez (ClearEdge) — due: 2026-12-14 — deliverable: pilot runbook (Drive link).
2) Pilot setup & smoke tests — owner: ClearEdge engineering + Redwood devops — due: 2026-12-20 (start) / 2026-12-31 (end) — deliverable: pilot metrics report.
3) Security & procurement checklist completed — owner: Tom Barrett (ClearEdge) + Rafael Mendes (Redwood legal enablement) — due: 2027-01-06 — deliverable: SOW draft + security signoff.
4) Pilot review & exec decision — owner: Sarah Lin — due: 2027-01-20 — deliverable: go/no-go and budget signoff.

Acceptance criteria (detailed):
- p95 latency <= 200ms for standard 512-token requests during typical load; validated during pilot and reported to dashboard.
- Successful simulation of 500 RPS bursts without sustained SLO violations (>5% breach window) during pilot.
- KMS integration validated for labeled PII endpoints; KMS key rotation documented.

Open questions / asks for ClearEdge:
- Confirm Alicia Gomez and Marcus Liu as primary technical leads (emails listed by Sarah).
- Confirm Tom Barrett is procurement lead and provide billing contact for the SOW.
- Confirm budget band for the pilot and any pre-approved procurement thresholds (e.g., approvals required > $50k).

Next concrete actions (what I’ll do):
- Update the MAP spreadsheet (attached: ClearEdge_MAP_v1.xlsx) and push to the Drive folder.
- Create HubSpot tasks for the technical deep-dive and pilot kickoff (link included).
- Prepare an initial SOW template for procurement and share with Rafael to align on legal language.

Please reply with a one-line confirmation on stakeholders and the 12/14 deep-dive slot and I’ll lock resources.

Thanks —
Olga
Olga Petrov
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
olga@redwood.com | (415) 555-0182

Attachments: ClearEdge_MAP_v1.xlsx, redwood-pricing-summary.pdf, technical-deep-dive-agenda.pdf

source.subject: Post-call: Atlas Health usage deep-dive & pricing path
messages:
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khan <samir@atlashealth.com>, Elena Morales <elena@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Dec 2026 15:10:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call recap: Atlas usage deep-dive & suggested pricing path

Hi Samir / Elena — thanks again for taking the time today. I wanted to capture the highlights and clear next steps so we’re all aligned.

Quick recap of what you told us:
- Primary goal: move ML inference for clinical triage scoring from proof-of-concept to a production pilot across 3 hospitals by Q2 2027.
- Current pain points: unpredictable latency during peak batch windows (evening batch jobs), ballooning per-token costs on long clinical notes, and lack of KV-cache reuse across similar requests.
- Workloads of interest: structured triage scoring (short prompts, high TPS), long-form discharge summary embeddings for search (long sequences), and a nightly batch inference pipeline for retrospective analytics.
- Current usage: ~1.2M tokens/month primarily on ad-hoc endpoints; Q2 projection if pilot expands: 8–12M tokens/month. See attached Atlas_Q2_usage_report.csv for today's export.

What we proposed on the call:
1) Short term (pilot): deploy a Dedicated low-latency pool for the triage scoring model, enable prefix caching for repeated clinical templates, and apply 8-bit quantization to the long-form embedding model to cut token cost.
2) Upsell / medium term: add a scheduled batching worker for the nightly pipeline and a Dedicated reserved capacity commit to stabilize unit pricing.
3) Pricing path: we sketched two commit options (see pricing_commit_options.pdf): a 3-month pilot commit with modest discount and a 12-month committed tier with deeper discounts and guaranteed latency SLOs.

Agreed next steps (owners):
- Samir: share an internal list of the three hospital endpoints + anticipated daily TPS per endpoint (by Tue 12/8).
- Dev: produce a tailored pilot proposal (resource plan, performance targets, preliminary pricing) by Thu 12/10.
- Marissa / Rafael: review the draft MSA amendment we’ll provide for the committed tier (we'll attach Draft_MSA_Amendment_v2.docx) and flag any procurement blockers.
- Elena: confirm procurement timeline and DocuSign contact for signature routing (aiming for legal review in Dec).

Attachments: Atlas_Q2_usage_report.csv, pricing_commit_options.pdf, Draft_MSA_Amendment_v2.docx (draft).

If that all looks right I’ll kick off the proposal and attach the pilot architecture diagram in a follow-up.

Thanks —
Dev Patel
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
dev.patel@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

From: Samir Khan <samir@atlashealth.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@atlashealth.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Dec 2026 16:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: Atlas usage deep-dive & suggested pricing path

Thanks Dev — helpful summary. Couple clarifications and additions: 

- Hospital endpoints: CH-East (EHR-form APIs, ~120 TPS peak), CH-North (HL7 bridge, ~40 TPS), CH-West (API gateway for telehealth, ~80 TPS). I’ll send a spreadsheet with exact burst patterns tomorrow.
- Latency pain: our main SLA concern is <250ms p95 for the triage scoring endpoint. Nightly batch can tolerate longer latency.
- Security: SOC2 + VPC peering are mandatory for pilot. Can Redwood run the pilot in our VPC or do we need a Private deployment?

Procurement timeline: legal wants to review the MSA amendment by Dec 20; procurement will need a PO number and a 30-day invoicing term. Elena will loop the procurement contact.

On the pricing side, the 3-month pilot makes sense as a fast path; for the 12-month commit we’ll need clearer unit economics for the embedding-heavy workloads. Pricing_commit_options.pdf was useful — I’ll add comments.

Appreciate the quick turnaround on the proposal.

—Samir
Director of Applied Data Science, Atlas Health

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 06 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Post-call recap: Atlas usage deep-dive & suggested pricing path

Great summary, Dev — a few internal notes for the proposal and negotiation strategy: 

1) Private deployment vs VPC: legal's ask for VPC peering + SOC2 can often be satisfied with Redwood Dedicated + VPC peering; we should confirm whether they require full Private (customer-managed control plane) or just VPC connectivity. Dev, include both options with delta pricing.

2) Latency SLO: p95 <250ms target — recommend we size Dedicated pool with headroom for 2x peak TPS and enable autoscale burst for sudden surges. Capture a fallback policy to hosted lower-cost model in case of capacity constraints. That language helps procurement accept staged SLAs.

3) Discount strategy: offer the 3-month pilot at 10% off list on token consumption + credits for engineering support. For the 12-month commit, propose a tiered discount (15% at 8M tokens/mo, 25% at 12M tokens/mo) with a clause to revisit if usage growth exceeds 30% MoM. Rafael, can you run math on margin at the 12M token point?

4) MSA amendment: attach Draft_MSA_Amendment_v2.docx and highlight the VPC and data retention sections for legal. Flag any customer requests that expand auditing needs — may require SRE time.

I’m ok to be on the next call if Atlas wants a procurement/legal handoff.

-Marissa
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference

From: Elena Morales <elena@atlashealth.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Samir Khan <samir@atlashealth.com>
Date: Mon, 07 Dec 2026 09:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: Atlas usage deep-dive & suggested pricing path

Thanks all — quick procurement update:

- Procurement contact: Lisa Hsu (lisa.hsu@atlashealth.com). She’s available for a 30-minute alignment on Tuesday 12/9; please include Marissa or legal if possible.
- Timeline: Legal review target by Dec 20, PO issuance by Jan 10 if economics are approved.
- DocuSign routing: I’ve included a link for our standard PO signature flow. I’ll need the finalized pricing appendix and any custom MSA language inserted before legal review.

Elena
Procurement Manager, Atlas Health

On Fri, Dec 5, 2026 at 03:10 PM Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Samir / Elena — thanks again for taking the time today. I wanted to capture the highlights and clear next steps so we’re all aligned.
> 
> Quick recap of what you told us:
> - Primary goal: move ML inference for clinical triage scoring from proof-of-concept to a production pilot across 3 hospitals by Q2 2027.
> - Current pain points: unpredictable latency during peak batch windows (evening batch jobs), ballooning per-token costs on long clinical notes, and lack of KV-cache reuse across similar requests.
> - Workloads of interest: structured triage scoring (short prompts, high TPS), long-form discharge summary embeddings for search (long sequences), and a nightly batch inference pipeline for retrospective analytics.
> 
> —Dev
